city.name: Sebastopyr
city.etymology: Sebastopyr fuses the Old-Tongue root *sebast-* (the martyr-name; "the venerable one who endured the arrows and did not die of them, then was martyred anyway") with *-pyr*, the Old-Tongue suffix for "wall" or "ward-stone" — literally a watchfire-stone set in the curtain wall of a holy keep. So Sebastopyr means, in its first reading, "The Wall Named For The Enduring One" — but the same root *sebast-* in the older liturgical case carries the secondary, bitter sense of "the one who endured for nothing," because the saint endured the arrows only to be clubbed to death in the second martyrdom. The founders chose the name in 6th-century-old liturgical court Old-Tongue, and the scholiasts have argued for six hundred years which sense was intended. The Cathedral's official position is that both senses were intended at once — that the city's name is a deliberate paradox the founders left lit for their descendants — but at least one suppressed gloss in the archive reads the name as a confession, set in stone by men who already knew the war could not be won and named their wall accordingly. The city is universally called "the Pyr" in everyday speech (Pyrian for its citizens, the Pyric Wall for the curtain), which has the unintended effect of letting people forget, daily, what the second half of the name actually means.

bastion_city_options_considered.name: Sebastrian Quietus
bastion_city_options_considered.rationale: Closest to the user's original instinct but elevated with the Old-Tongue suffix -trian (saint-of) and a martyred epithet (Quietus = the silencing, the stilling). Two-word name reads as a formal saint-titular city, like 'Santiago de Compostela' done in invented Latin.
bastion_city_options_considered.why_not: Two words is unwieldy on a map and in speech; players will inevitably shorten it to 'Sebastrian,' which lands too close to Sebastian — exactly the problem we were asked to solve. The Quietus half is too on-the-nose for the futility reading; it tells the joke instead of burying it.

bastion_city_options_considered.name: Aspex Sebastine
bastion_city_options_considered.rationale: Aspex = 'the watch' / 'the looking-toward' in invented liturgical Old-Tongue; Sebastine as the martyred adjective. Reads like a formal church-named outpost-grown-into-a-city. Carries the watch-on-the-wall flavor.
bastion_city_options_considered.why_not: The 'Aspex' prefix sounds more like a corporate biotech firm than a six-century cathedral-city when said aloud. The mouth-feel is wrong — too clipped, not enough weight. And it foregrounds watching rather than enduring, which is the wrong saint.

bastion_city_options_considered.name: Carcerost
bastion_city_options_considered.rationale: Carcer (the prison, the holding, from the user's own calibration list) + -ost (Old-Tongue locative for 'place-of'). Lands the futility hard: the city as the prison of its own endurance.
bastion_city_options_considered.why_not: Too dark in one direction — it commits the name to the futility reading and loses the paradox. A six-century church naming its capital 'Prison-place' is not in character; the founders would have chosen something the doctrine could spin. The whole point is the name argues with itself.

bastion_city_options_considered.name: Vigilarn
bastion_city_options_considered.rationale: Vigil (the wake, the watching-through-the-night) + -arn (an invented Old-Tongue agentive for fortified place, by analogy with real-world -burgh). Compact, watchful, baroque.
bastion_city_options_considered.why_not: Doesn't hold the saint. The user was explicit that the etymology must preserve the saint of endurance; Vigilarn drops the saint entirely and becomes a generic watch-fortress. Also too close in cadence to real-world Vigilance/Vigilante.

bastion_city_options_considered.name: Adamantium-of-the-Arrows
bastion_city_options_considered.rationale: Hyphenated epithet construction in the manner of 'Quietus-of-the-Wall' from the user's calibration. Adamantium = the unbreakable; of-the-Arrows binds it explicitly to the saint shot full of them.
bastion_city_options_considered.why_not: Adamantium is a real-world word (Wolverine, alchemy) — the user's calibration list used it as a tonal reference, not as an actual candidate. Importing it directly would feel like a tonal sample slipped into the final mix. The hyphenated form also reads as an epithet for a place rather than the place's name itself.

raw_gap_fill.paragon_legend_name: The half-remembered original: Halden Orriste — a sergeant of the Fourth Shieldwall, a name half-recorded in the muster-roll for the Long Vigil of the year 583 PD ('Post Damnationem'), and not even his first name is certain (the muster-roll abbreviates it as 'Hldn.', and at least one chancery copy reads 'Holden'). The legendary form: Saint-Aspirant Halidan Aurriste, the Arrow-Stander, the One Who Stood When the Line Broke, He-Who-Was-Looked-Upon — a four-title liturgical form the Church has been quietly extending year by year, with the most recent epithet ('the Walker-Beyond-the-Wall') added by chancery decree only six years ago. The polishing of 'Halden' into 'Halidan' (which scans as a Vespertine saint-name) and 'Orriste' into 'Aurriste' (which puns on the Old-Tongue *aur-*, 'gold' or 'dawn') is a perfect Constantine artifact: the curation is visible if you know to look, and no one does.
raw_gap_fill.paragon_title: Lord-Paragon of the Reclaimed Crown, Warden of Ferrostat, Hand of the Cathedral Beyond the Wall — informally 'the Paragon,' formally 'the Hand,' liturgically 'the Aspirant' (because the Cathedral has not yet canonized him; canonization is reserved for the dead, and his living status is an awkward theological problem the chancery has settled by inventing the Aspirant-rank).

elder_peoples.name: The Caer-Druin (the Hollowed)
elder_peoples.description: A people of the old hill-courts who once warded the high places of Therimaur with songs that bound the dark to stone. When the collapse came, they emptied their long-halls and walked down into Sebastopyr carrying their reliquary-harps and their dead. They sing the Watch-Cantos at the Pyric Wall every dusk; the Cathedral has folded their harp-rite into the orthodox vesper-mass and now claims the cantos were always Vespertine liturgy. The Caer-Druin permit this, which is its own kind of dying — they remember the theft and choose not to name it, because the alternative is to leave, and there is nowhere left to go.

elder_peoples.name: The Ferrenkin
elder_peoples.description: Stone-and-iron folk of the deep galleries beneath Therimaur, smaller and slower than men, with skin the grey of cooled slag and eyes that adjust to forge-light and not to sun. They came up out of the lower delvings six centuries ago when hell's heat began to rise from below, and they brought with them the smithing-rites that make the wall-plate and the rail-guns and the censers that burn the Damned. Half the holy weapons in Sebastopyr are Ferrenkin work; they are also, quietly, the only people in the city who can still read pre-collapse engineering and who therefore know exactly how many wall-towers have been silently condemned and re-numbered to hide the count.

elder_peoples.name: The Vesperine (the Half-Kind)
elder_peoples.description: Descendants of an elder bloodline that interbred with humanity in the long pre-collapse — outwardly indistinguishable from men, but carrying a thinned inheritance that makes them more legible to the Stain and more capable of sensing it in others. The Cathedral employs them as inquisitors and confessors precisely because of this gift, and treats them with a courteous, watched suspicion: the same blood that lets them see corruption is itself, in the wrong doctrinal light, evidence of an unsanctioned ancestry. Most of the high inquisition is Vesperine. None of them are entirely sure whether they are trusted servants or held hostages.

elder_peoples.name: The Mournwing
elder_peoples.description: A small remnant of a winged people whose flights once carried messages between the high citadels of pre-collapse Therimaur. There are perhaps four hundred left in Sebastopyr — bone-thin, dressed in black canvas wing-sheaths, employed as couriers and as the watch on the upper spires. They will not fly beyond the Pyric Wall; whatever happened to their cousins in the open sky during the collapse, they will not say, and the few who tried to map the no-fly territory in the early reclamation did not return. The Mournwing keep their own martyr-rolls and do not share them with the Cathedral.

naming_conventions.city_culture: Vespertine human names braid a clipped Old-Tongue first name with a saint-derived or trade-derived family name, often with a liturgical particle ('di', 'de la', 'os') as a mid-name connector. Common male: Halidan, Orric, Mauritz, Cassian, Velim, Thelos. Common female: Severa, Liturge, Marent, Nocta, Asperine, Vespera. Family names often end in -ost, -arde, -ille, -ine, -emaur (e.g., Vell-os-Carrenost, Marent de la Pyrille, Velim Aurriste). Common-folk in the under-wards drop the particles and use trade-bynames: Tom-of-the-Censers, Nera Slag-Hand, Old Pell the Rationer.
naming_conventions.church_titles: Strictly hierarchical and strictly Old-Tongue-rooted, even when worn by speakers who do not understand the roots. Ascending: Postulant; Lector; Veriarch (parish priest); Confessor-Ordinary; Inquisitor-Vested; Prelate-of-the-Pyr; Archvigil (the head of the wall-rites); Cantor-Magisterial (the head of liturgy); Hierarch (provincial); and at the top, the Sebastopater (literally 'father-of-the-enduring-one,' the Cathedral's living head). Specialist titles thread under these: Almoner (charity), Lampwarden (under-ward parish), Stain-Reader (Vesperine specialty), Aspirant (the unique living-saint rank invented for the Paragon).
naming_conventions.noble_houses: Vespertine noble houses are 'of' a holding ('House Aurriste of Carrenost,' 'House Veillarde of the Lower Pyr'), with house-words in Old-Tongue ('SUSTINEO' — I endure; 'NEC FRANGOR' — I do not break; 'IN UMBRA LUX' — light in the shadow). House sigils are heraldic, heavy with arrows-not-killing-the-saint imagery and broken-wall imagery. Cadet branches use the '-cadet' suffix ('Aurriste-cadet of Ferrostat') which marks them as eligible-but-junior in the chancery rolls.
naming_conventions.demons: Demons of hell are not given proper personal names in polite speech — they are referred to by ABSTRACTED OFFICES, the way you would refer to a foreign bureaucracy you do not wish to dignify. 'The Auditor.' 'The Quartermaster of Tongues.' 'The Long Ledger.' 'The Sixth Notary.' When their true names must be inscribed (for binding, for warding, for the strike_bargain rite), they are written in Old-Tongue accusative-vocative, hyphenated and barbed: Mors-Adimere-Quod-Reddidit, Vox-Quae-In-Tenebris-Numerat, Ferrum-Sub-Cute. These names are dangerous to say aloud and are read silently from the page; the chancery clerks who copy them wear blindfolds when they file.
naming_conventions.places_within_the_city: Sebastopyr is mapped by RING and by WARD. Rings (concentric, named for their distance from the Cathedral): the Sanctuary Ring, the Choir Ring, the Marshal Ring, the Common Ring, the Under-Ward Ring, the Cinderbelow. Wards (radial, named after their old gates or their function): Pyric Ward (the wall itself), Almongate Ward, Lamplost Ward, Reliquary Ward, Quietus Ward (the funeral district), the Carcer (the prison-quarter, single-named, never 'Carcer Ward' — the article 'the' is the marker that you are talking about the prison and not just any holding-place). Specific named places use Old-Tongue compound forms: the Cantor's Stair, the Halls of the Long Vigil, the Mort-Censorium, the Audit-Below, the Pyric Bell, the Drinkings-of-the-Last-Lamp.
naming_conventions.places_in_the_kingdom: The Reclaimed Crown of Vespertin is mapped in MARCHES and HOLDS. Marches are stretches of frontier ('the Iron March,' 'the Ash March,' 'the Mire March'). Holds are fortified towns or keeps inside the marches, named with -stat (a defended station: Ferrostat, Vigilstat, Halidan-stat — the last is recent and politically charged), -gard (a warded settlement: Aurgard, Lampgard), -caer (an old-tongue elder-style fortified place, usually pre-collapse and reclaimed: Caer Pyrigorne, Caer Vesselain), -mire (lost holdings in the marshlands east of Sebastopyr: Drownmire, Saintmire), -fall (places where battles broke, kept as memorial-names: Lector's Fall, Banner-Fall). Roads are named for their endpoints or for the saint whose relic was carried down them: the Aurriste Road, the Pilgrim's Cinder, the Long Procession.
naming_conventions.old_tongue: The Old Tongue is heavily Latinate-cadenced, with case-endings (nominative, accusative, vocative, genitive, locative, instrumental), a love of compound nouns built from a noun-root + a suffix indicating function (-stat = defended station; -ost = place-of; -arn = warded place; -pyr = wall/wardstone; -trian = saint-of; -magna = great-X; -bellum = war-X), and a small set of liturgical particles (os, di, de la, sub) that survived into Vespertine. It distinguishes formally between the indicative ('this is') and the deemed ('this is decreed-to-be'), which is theologically critical and the source of the doctrinal phrase 'the war is deemed eternal' rather than 'the war is eternal.' It has no future tense in the strict liturgical register — the Old-Tongue grammarians held that the future was God's tense and not for mortals to conjugate — and Vespertine-language speakers borrow a Vesperine future-construction when they need one, which is itself a small daily heresy no one notices.

raw_gap_fill.old_tongue_notes: The Old Tongue — called *Lingua Sebastina* by the Cathedral and just *the high speech* by everyone else — was the administrative and liturgical language of the pre-collapse polity that built the first wall and named Sebastopyr. It is no one's mother tongue anymore. It survives in three reservoirs and is dying or mutating in each: in LITURGY, where the Cathedral preserves it intact for the mass, the rites, the warding-formulae, and the names of demons (the priests memorize the sounds without always knowing the senses, which is theologically tolerated and historically catastrophic); in LAW AND CHANCERY, where contracts, oaths, bargains, charters of standing, and the formal language of strike_bargain and incur_debt are still drafted in Old-Tongue clauses because a debt sworn in Old-Tongue is held to bind more deeply than one sworn in Vespertine — the under-ward fixers know this and use it both ways; and in ARCHAIC PLACE-NAMES AND CURSE-WORDS, where the language has fossilized into the map and into the gutter. The vulgar Vespertine of the city is shot through with Old-Tongue oaths used as expletives by people who could not parse them: 'Sustineo!' ('I endure!' — used as a sigh of weary acknowledgment), 'Per Sebast!' ('by the Enduring One!' — a mild oath), 'Nec frangor' ('I do not break' — a stubborn refusal, often muttered), 'Ad audit!' ('to the Audit!' — the bastion's equivalent of 'to hell with it,' invoking the demonic Auditor). The Caer-Druin still speak fragments of an even older tongue that predates the Old Tongue itself, and the Cathedral has cataloged these fragments but does not teach them; the Ferrenkin speak Old-Tongue with an industrial dialect full of metallurgical terms the chancery does not recognize as Old-Tongue at all (and treats as Ferrenkin loanwords, when in fact they are the Old-Tongue's original technical vocabulary, which the surface scholiasts forgot). Most damning: there are sections of the deep archive written in a register of Old-Tongue called *cursivus sebastinus minor* — the small running hand used by sixth-century chancery clerks for internal memoranda — that almost no one alive can read fluently. The count of saints, if it is anywhere, is there.

bench_rate.rate: 1 Grace-dose (second tier) = 1 Crown-piece + 3 Bells OR 1 mortal-favor of second magnitude
bench_rate.source: Brokerage of the First Light, Drinkings-of-the-Last-Lamp, Cinderbelow (posted by Nera Slag-Hand the Elder); Old Pell the Rationer holds the parallel under-ward quote
bench_rate.note: Top-level summary; canonical posted rate-card is daily_life entry 'The Bench Rate, PD 606, 14th of Lauds' (id: daily_life-2). Do not duplicate the rates table here.

historical.note: first widening as Vas Halidani drainage curve became visible to Brokerage
3-Bell spread; the bench is pricing in an upcoming reading

accounts.name: Lady Severa's 144-month installment
accounts.struck: PD 595 (three winters past, counted from PD 606 frame)
accounts.principal: Her son lives to eighteen — clean span of years, no fever, no wall-draft, no Calling roll
accounts.price: Monthly fever-deaths drawn from her tithe-rolls — one tenant-name per month, marked 'fever' on the parish census; the Long Ledger collects the soul at the moment the family is most certain the fever was natural
accounts.collateral: Her grandson Renzo (age 7 in PD 606; the bible's bargain economy has him at 12 in some layers — held here as 7, per the named-and-aged collateral clause; he has begun coughing in the deep-history layer's 12-year frame). The collateral clause is sealed in Old-Tongue accusative-vocative; Lady Severa does not know the collateral is her grandson.

payment_schedule.payment_kind: Stain credit (each name pays one month of Stain-2 debt; her own Stain has not visibly moved because the debt rides the tenants)

markers_held: Old-Tongue Stain-token in Long Ledger's master account inscribed 'Veillarde, S., obligor, 144 installments, 11 paid'
Sealed collateral-rider naming Renzo (not shown to Lady Severa)
Witness-mark from Velim Aurriste of the Pyric Wall (the honest broker; he witnessed without knowing the collateral clause)

readings.notes: Initial reading at striking; the Long Ledger does not bloom a Stain on the first installment, by house custom
Five installments in; the parish census-clerk flagged a fever-cluster in the Veillarde tithe-rolls; the flag was filed under 'lower-Pyr damp' and not escalated
Eleven installments in; no Stain-read has been performed on Lady Severa since PD 600; the grandson has not been read at all
Confessor Mortane has quietly entered 'household Stain audit, House Veillarde' in the chancery's outyear ledger; it has not been confirmed it will execute

collection_triggers: Lady Severa misses a monthly tithe-roll signing
Renzo's eighth year completes (the collateral clause matures at age 8 in the Long Ledger's calendar)
Lady Severa attempts to confess the bargain to a Vesperine confessor in good standing
The son she bargained for dies before 18 (the bargain converts to immediate full-balance collection on the grandson)
discharge_terms: Substitute soul of equal or greater Stain-weight offered and accepted by the Long Ledger (no canonical instance of this house accepting substitution)
Lady Severa renounces the bargain BEFORE the next moonrise signing — converts to immediate full-balance collection on the collateral (Renzo)
accounts.notes: The under-ward calls the monthly deaths the Veillarde Vespers. Velim Aurriste of the Pyric Wall (npc_019, the honest divine-bargain broker) witnessed the original striking and is canonically uncertain whether he was complicit; he has been writing private confession-notes in his own ledger that no Reader has yet seen. PC fork-point: a PC at her elbow at moonrise hears the audited voice (per the deep-history layer scene).

accounts.name: The Octave Bargain (Velim Aurriste x Quartermaster of Tongues)

creditor.name: The Quartermaster of Tongues (Sixth Notary clerking)

accounts.principal: The Doctrine of Rescinded Eternity holds — Velim's PD 589 masterwork is permitted to land in the chancery without doctrinal counter-strike, and the Paragon's miracle is protected from re-reading for one human generation
accounts.price: A future silence to be named — one utterance Velim will not be permitted to make at the moment the Quartermaster chooses; the utterance is not specified in advance and is sealed against Velim's foreknowledge
accounts.collateral: Velim's own tongue (literal — the Auditor's tongue-tic has already begun in the PD 606 frame; the Quartermaster co-signed with the Auditor at the bench, which is the house cross-tier that explains the tic appearing on a Quartermaster account)

payment_schedule.payment_kind: single-event silence at the Quartermaster's call; the bench files it as an octave because the price will fall on the eighth canonical moment the Quartermaster chooses, counting from PD 588

markers_held: Old-Tongue silence-token in Quartermaster's Sixth-Notary ledger inscribed 'Aurriste, V., cantor-magisterial, one silence pending, octave-counted from PD 588'
Tongue-tic marker (Auditor's mark, co-signed) — physically expressed on Velim's body, visible to a trained Vesperine Stain-reader

readings.notes: Initial reading at striking; the Quartermaster pays the cost of CLEAN on year zero by house custom; Velim believed this was the bargain's whole shape
Six years in; faint Stain bloom that the chancery filed as 'consistent with high-altitude doctrinal work' and did not escalate. Confessor Mortane signed the file.
Twelve years in; the reading was inconclusive — the Reader's mercury would not settle. Filed as 'reader-state, retake'. Retake never scheduled.
Velim's twenty-year doctrinal review will require a full Stain-read. The bench has been pricing this in (current 3-Bell spread).

collection_triggers: The Quartermaster names the silence (single-event, no warning)
Velim attempts to confess the bargain to a Vesperine in good standing (converts to immediate collection)
The Doctrine of Rescinded Eternity is formally challenged in chancery (the bargain's principal becomes void and the price falls due immediately)
The Paragon is re-read and the reading comes back anything other than CLEAN (the principal's protection is forfeit; price collects)
discharge_terms: Quartermaster names the silence and Velim withholds the utterance as required
Velim dies before the silence is named (the bargain converts to a posthumous Stain-rider on his line — Aurriste-cadet branch named in the sealed collateral)
The Sixth Notary's incomplete name is bound before completion (the entire Quartermaster apparatus is sundered; this discharge is theoretical only)
accounts.canonical_moment_if_fired: Velim's PD 608 reading exposes the Sixth Notary contract — the tongue-tic is read by a Vesperine of sufficient rank, the Reader's mercury holds the bloom, and the chancery cannot file the result as anything but an open bargain at the magisterial level. The Doctrine of Rescinded Eternity falls under doctrinal review the same quarter. This is one of the bible's three big-bang reveals.
accounts.notes: Velim believed the price was 'a future silence' and not 'his tongue'; the collateral was inscribed in cursivus sebastinus minor that he could not read fluently. The Auditor's co-signature is the bench joke: a Quartermaster account that pays in Auditor-tic is the house cross-tier the chancery has never written down.

accounts.name: Halidan-Quartermaster open account (the disputed refusal)
accounts.struck: PD 583, the Long Vigil — the moment the line broke and the Quartermaster's offer reached Halden Orriste's ear under the arrows

obligor.name: Saint-Aspirant Halidan Aurriste (then Sergeant Halden Orriste of the Fourth Shieldwall)

accounts.principal: Survive the Standing — the offer was 'do not die here'; the canonical refusal-clause read 'unless taken in ignorance'
accounts.price: Unknown — the Quartermaster will not say; the bench reads it as 'a future office held in trust' but this is bench-speculation, not bench-record
accounts.collateral: Unknown — the Quartermaster's house custom would assign collateral at the moment the bargain is acknowledged by the obligor; Halidan has never acknowledged

payment_schedule.next_due: unknown — contingent on the Quartermaster naming the account
payment_schedule.payment_kind: Halidan's grace_pool currently sits at 1240 (~73% of his peak, drainage observed); if the marker IS held, the Vas Halidani drainage curve traces back through Halidan to this account

markers_held: unknown — the Quartermaster's seat shows an open account flagged 'Orriste/Aurriste, H., obligor uncertain' with refusal-clause 'unless taken in ignorance' attached; the bench cannot tell whether this is a held marker or a vestigial offer

readings.notes: Halidan's elevation Stain-read came back ANOMALY; chancery filed it as 'consistent with divine attention residue' (per gaps_identified). The ANOMALY is the same shape a refusal-clause-on-an-open-account would throw.

Confessor Mauritz reads him daily; readings come back CLEAN, which is itself the anomaly per the canon resolution. The daily CLEAN may be Mauritz's own un-questioned read (Mortane's secret) or it may be Halidan genuinely refusing on a frame Mauritz cannot test.

collection_triggers: Halidan acknowledges the bargain (converts disputed-refused to acknowledged-open and collateral attaches retroactively)
The Quartermaster's house declares the marker matured (no canonical timetable; bench reads this as 'when the Vas Halidani drainage curve hits a threshold')
The Sixth Notary's incomplete name reaches the syllable that names Halidan (per Mournwing intelligence; canonical timing unspecified)
discharge_terms: Halidan dies in canonical refusal — the disputed open account closes as 'refused, no collection'
Independent Vesperine re-reading confirms refusal and is formally entered in the Sealed Anomaly Ledger (Hierarch Nocta's drafted fourth report is the canonical mechanism)
The Quartermaster's account is sundered by external means (Sixth Notary binding, Cathedral counter-rite)
accounts.terms: Halidan refused but cannot be certain
accounts.risk: If the marker IS held, the Vas Halidani drainage curve traces back through Halidan; the doctrinal load is that the Cathedral's six-century capital depletion has been partly paid by an uncollected demonic account riding the Paragon
accounts.notes: Halidan refused but cannot be certain — the refusal-clause 'unless taken in ignorance' is a Quartermaster house specialty and the canonical 'unknown' is load-bearing. Confessor Mortane is the only living party who has even framed the question; he has not asked it of Halidan and has not asked his own Stain about it (per his secret: CLEAN for 23 years, never tested otherwise).

accounts.name: Mother Aspine's Lampgate counter-account (sanctioned brokerage career)
accounts.struck: Continuous from PD 568 (Aspine's first Lampgate striking) through PD 606

obligor.name: Mother Aspine Vell-os-Carrenost (in her brokerage capacity)

creditor.name: The Sixth Notary (collecting bench for sanctioned Stain-1/Stain-2 bargains)

accounts.principal: Aspine retains her Cathedral seal and her Lampgate Bench seat for life — the Cathedral's sanctioned-bargain franchise rides on her counter, and she is permitted to broker without inquisitorial audit so long as the counter is kept
accounts.price: Aspine herself functions as a Cathedral counter-volume — each sanctioned bargain she brokers is laundered through her name; she is the named witness on ~1100 lifetime strikings (counter as of PD 606), 327 of which remain open accounts on her counter
accounts.collateral: Her own Stain — Aspine's Stain reads at the line every season and has never read over; the bench treats her counter as a self-collateralizing instrument (the moment she reads ANOMALY, the franchise reverts)

payment_schedule.next_due: Continuous — average term per bargain ~18 months; Aspine's counter moves monthly
payment_schedule.payment_kind: Stain laundering (the bargain's Stain rides Aspine's counter for the term of the bargain, then transfers to the obligor at maturity; this is the sanctioned-franchise's central technical trick)

markers_held: Lampgate Bench seat (granted; revocable on ANOMALY)
Counter-volume in the Sixth Notary's archive (sealed; the counter is the bench's official record of every sanctioned strike-bargain in the Crown since PD 568)

readings.notes: Current; counter at ~1100 lifetime, 327 open. The reading is published quarterly in the Lampgate audit.

collection_triggers: Aspine reads ANOMALY at any quarterly audit (franchise reverts, all 327 open accounts return to obligors with full Stain attached, mass collection event)
Aspine dies (open accounts return to obligors at her death-hour; the bench's standard succession-clause)
The Cathedral formally revokes her seal (functionally identical to ANOMALY-read)
discharge_terms: All 327 open accounts mature, transfer, and close cleanly — Aspine retires with no successor (this is what she is canonically planning, per the Lampgate Bench layer)
Aspine names a sanctioned successor and the franchise transfers (no canonical successor has been named in PD 606)
accounts.notes: Aspine's counter-account is the bible's sanctioned-broker baseline against which heretic brokerage (Asperine Vesselain, bargain_005) is measured. The counter is the Cathedral's official record of the demonic economy in the Crown; the chancery does not know the count is published in cursivus sebastinus minor and would be unable to read the granular detail even if it requested the volume. lifetime_count=1100, current_open=327, average_term_months=18, payment_kind=Stain laundering.

accounts.name: Asperine Vesselain's pseudonymous Caer-Druin ledger (heretic brokerage)
accounts.struck: Continuous; the pseudonymous ledger has been running PD 595-606; the older-tongue accounts run back further but are unrecorded in the chancery

obligor.name: Asperine Vesselain (under the Caer-Druin pseudonym used at the Drownmire Hand parlor)

creditor.name: Various unsanctioned demonic counter-benches (the heretic brokerage operates retail; counterparties are negotiated per-bargain)

accounts.principal: Asperine retains her cover (the moveable parlor remains undetected; six failed Inquisition interrogations of her sanctioned identity stand) and the brokerage continues to write paper
accounts.price: One-time market reads of unpaid mortal-debt cycles — Asperine reads the Lamplost Wednesday market for her counterparties and tells them which mortal debts are about to default; the counterparties move on those debts to convert mortal-favor into Stain-credit before the default registers
accounts.collateral: Asperine's cover (sanctioned captaincy + clean-ledger reputation at Vesselain Roads); if the cover breaks, the Lamplost Wednesday Readings are exposed and the unsanctioned counterparties' positions are visible to the Inquisition

payment_schedule.payment_kind: per-reading favor-debt; Asperine accumulates mortal-favor IOUs from counterparties, which she cashes into Cinderbelow supply lines

markers_held: Pseudonymous Caer-Druin ledger written in older-tongue case-endings the chancery has cataloged but does not teach; physical volume held at the moveable Drownmire Hand parlor
Counterparty IOUs (14 currently outstanding, held by Asperine; the counterparties hold no markers on her in turn — the heretic brokerage runs one-way by design)

readings.notes: Inquisition interrogation #3 — sanctioned identity passed clean
Inquisition interrogation #5 — sanctioned identity passed clean
Inquisition interrogation #6 (most recent) — sanctioned identity passed clean. The Stain-load is held entirely on the pseudonymous counter, which the Inquisition does not know to test.

collection_triggers: An Inquisition aspersoir-cart finds the moveable parlor (cover breaks; counterparties' positions are exposed; the 14 open IOUs default and Asperine's Stain-load reverts to her sanctioned identity instantly)
A counterparty defaults on a favor-IOU and Asperine is unable to absorb the loss (the older-tongue ledger requires settled positions on a weekly cycle)

Asperine attempts to sell the name 'Halidan Aurriste' to a seventh buyer (per recent_history threats — she has sold pieces of the name to seven buyers including House Halidan-Aurriste; the eighth sale would trigger Mournwing counter-action)
discharge_terms: Asperine retires from the moveable parlor and the pseudonymous ledger is burned in the Cinderbelow forge (canonical exit; she has refused two offers to do this)
The Caer-Druin presbytery formally claims the older-tongue ledger as theirs (would transfer the counter to a sanctioned-Caer-Druin frame; politically explosive, has not been attempted)
accounts.terms: one-time market reads of unpaid mortal-debt cycles
accounts.stake: Asperine's cover (sanctioned captaincy at Vesselain Roads) plus the Cinderbelow supply-line favors
accounts.risk: If caught, Lamplost Wednesday Readings are exposed and the Inquisition's PD 606 surveillance on the Cinderbelow tightens by an order of magnitude
accounts.notes: Asperine is the bible's retail mortal-debt brokerage and the counterfactual to Mother Aspine's sanctioned counter (bargain_004). The Mournwing courier has refused two purchase offers from Asperine and is saving intelligence for an unnamed price (per the deep-history layer).

plot_threads.name: The Murder of Confessor Thelos di Carrenost
plot_threads.summary: The empty seventh seat of the Custodes Numeri is empty because someone in the Custodes Numeri killed its holder with a beeswax stylus on the eve of the Octave of Triumph PD 606. Inspector Nera Cazares is working the case because she is Called and expendable.

plot_threads.name: The Next Demonic Advance
plot_threads.summary: The Sixth Notary's name composition (begun PD 565) is twelve to forty syllables from completion. Upon completion, a writ of foreclosure naming Ferrostat as collateral and Halidan Aurriste as a clerical error to be corrected will be served by carrion-birds reading in pre-Vespertine. The Mournwing know. They have not told the chancery.

plot_threads.name: The False-Singularity of the Paragon
plot_threads.summary: Halidan is real. His miracle is real. His CLEAN readings are real. The lie is the singularity — the Polishing of the Roll, the keel-throne / surname coincidence, the Walker-Beyond-the-Wall succession apparatus, the projected Grace failure in 11 years. He has begun to ask. The chancery deflects.

Eleven names paid. Velim Aurriste of the Pyric Wall witnessed. The under-ward calls them the Veillarde Vespers.

plot_threads.name: The Mournwing's Withheld Intelligence
plot_threads.summary: The Mournwing hold three intelligences the Cathedral does not: the Sixth Notary's partial name (16 of 40 syllables), the 41 Vesperine self-reads filed as ANOMALY in 19 months, and the location of the Cohort of the Lost Vespers' suppressed memorial. They withhold them as policy, not malice.

raw.name: The Mournwing's Withheld Intelligence

They withhold them as policy, not malice.
hooks: Ven will meet at Drinkings-of-the-Last-Lamp off-hour pour if approached through Old Pell
Wren of the High Roost hiding in upper roosts with original Thelos folio
Mournwing pre-collapse martyr-roll fragment in Audit-Below third stratum (mistaken for Cathedral property)
Mournwing martyr-roll names the four pre-Halidan Paragons of living memory
Cathedral seal of upper-spire autonomy is the brokerage price; no Sebastopater has been willing
resolution_conditions: Cathedral seal granted (all three intelligences surface in one week)
Mournwing coerced (Inquisition raid; intelligence taken by force; Mournwing scatter)
Mournwing bypassed (PCs obtain intelligences without cooperation)
raw.canonical_moment_if_fired: The Seal in the Sanctuary Ring: Sebastopater Iohanne signs the Mournwing Charter; Ven reads the Sixth Notary's partial name in pre-Vespertine for the first time in Cathedral history; Mournwing in upper spires sing for the first time since the cousins did not return

plot_threads.name: The Walker-Beyond-the-Wall Succession Apparatus
plot_threads.summary: The PD 597 epithet Walker-Beyond-the-Wall is the liturgical succession formula. Three conditions trigger transfer: 10 years beyond Wall (Halidan: 10y 3m), 5 years beyond Wall by successor + accepted bargain (Orric Aurriste-cadet, 6y, bargain pending spring), Sebast-Auditor finding of Aspirant Grace Insufficiency (in draft).

raw.name: The Walker-Beyond-the-Wall Succession Apparatus

hooks: Lady Asperine Veillarde at Ferrostat winter visit as bargain-witness
PC at Ferrostat can intercept bargain-offering or warn Orric (Mortal Bargain minimum)
Mortane has draft Aspirant Grace Insufficiency finding in locked desk
Halidan's refusal can be bypassed; Aspirant's consent not canonically required
Conscripted League preparing to deny chancery the queued successor by faking Orric's Stain at 5.0
resolution_conditions: Succession fires (Halidan's Grace fails; Orric elevated; old Paragon walks into the Mire)
Succession exposed (apparatus read in open chapter; singularity rebuilds as LINEAGE)
Succession broken (Orric refuses bargain; Cathedral forced to canonize Halidan posthumously or admit multi-Paragon canon)
raw.canonical_moment_if_fired: The Offering at the Ferrostat Picket-Line: Lady Asperine at the gate with beeswax-sealed bargain-clause; Orric in Captain-Vested's cloak with unread folio; Halidan in the keep behind drawing his sext-hour ration; whoever speaks first determines the next century

plot_threads.name: The Conscripted League's Active Refusal
plot_threads.summary: Wren Ashlin's Conscripted League is operational where the Brothers of the Cinder Hand are documentary. Three actions in preparation: incapacitate Archvigil Severa di Mauritz at next Discernment, place a Conscripted substitute (Wren herself, unknown to others) in the Sixth Notary's writ-path, extract Jin of Macau from Carcer cell 14.

Severa di Mauritz drinks at Drinkings-of-the-Last-Lamp three nights in seven
Jin reads anomaly because Battle-Mage signature cross-trips aspersoir; she is not damned
PC who learns Wren is the only viable Ferrostat substitute changes the action by telling the League

League taken (Inquisition raid; Wren read CLEAN by broken aspersoir and released by accident; League scatters)
League absorbed (Conscripted Charter granted; Refusal becomes sanctioned status; League becomes chancery faction)

raw.name: The Pyrian Dating System

designations.PD: Post Damnationem - 'after the damning' - years following the Fall
designations.PT: Pre-Tenebras - 'before the dark' - years before the Fall, counted backward from PD 0
designations.PD 0: administrative-only year, never used liturgically; appearing in a sacred document marks the document as forgery or heresy

raw.year_beginning: Octave of the Standing, twenty-three days after autumnal equinox (PD 589 reform)
raw.indicative_vs_decreed: Anno PD = indicative (occurred); Anno Decreto PD = deemed (decided to be remembered thus). Most public chronicles use decreed without marking.

raw.name: The Before (Pre-Tenebras)
key_facts: Saemurian Theocracy administered Therimaur for ~12 centuries
Long Door slow-widened in lower delvings PT 400-100
Treaty Liturgies bound hell's offices in exchange for tribute (recorded in cursivus sebastinus minor, sealed stratum)
PT 47: trading ship Aurriste burned at port - the 'first foreclosure' per Caer-Druin singers
PT 1: Last Year of Tongues - Saemurian records end mid-quarter

key_dates.PD 0: Reading of the Numbers begins; Long Ledger calls in accounts; Saemurian administrators die at their desks
key_dates.PD 14: Watershed Reversal - rivers reverse course; Cathedral teaches as 'the land turning its face to the wall'
key_dates.PD 23: First curtain wall (inner ring) completed under First-Archvigil Severa di Pyrigorne
key_dates.PD 41: Refugee convergence - Caer-Druin, Ferrenkin, Vesperine, Mournwing arrive at the wall
key_dates.PD 47: THE READING. Saint Severa di Pyrigorne martyred at the Almongate. Mundus Damnatus Est canonized. Fall completed.

raw.name: The Early Siege
doctrines_established: PD 108: Bellum Aeternum Decretum - The War Is Deemed Eternal (Sebastopater Mauritz V, Cantor-Magisterial Orric of the Lower Pyr)
PD 141: Office of Sanctioned Bindings (Curiate Vincula) - binding formula identical to PD 712 confiscated heretic-broker clause
PD 167: First aspersoir scale calibration - clinical-concern threshold set at underlying 0.9 Stain
raw.first_paragon_official: Saint Iohanne of the Almongate, PD 51
raw.first_paragons_suppressed: Three souls in PD 14, PD 29, PD 41 - two Caer-Druin, one Ferrenkin smelter (name scraped from marker)
raw.keel_block_installed: PD 73 - charred Aurriste keel beneath Sebastopater's throne by Sebastopater Cassian II

timeline.name: The Middle Siege
timeline.summary: Operational steady-state. Three centuries of wall holding, Hordes breaking, dead counted, names read. Curation system matured.

raw.name: The Middle Siege
raw.summary: Operational steady-state. Three centuries of wall holding, Hordes breaking, dead counted, names read. Curation system matured.

key_events.PD 312: Office of the Cantorial Scribes / Custodes Numeri instituted by Sebastopater Velim III (dead within the year). Charter authorizes seven scribes to maintain parallel register of indicative truth.
key_events.PD 312_concurrent: Brigith Halloran of Caer-Druin (daughter of PT 47 Aurriste's captain) drowned in Drownmire - 'second foreclosure'
key_events.PD 359: Plate of Sebastian sealed - 40-foot stratum of fused saint-bone, ~400 blessed unnamed second-martyrs interred
key_events.PD 411: Seven concentric courses of Pyric Wall reach completion; total: 411 raised, 383 standing, 28 condemned across all seven courses
key_events.PD 451: Calling rite formalized as sanctioned annual rite under Cantor-Magisterial Severa Vell-os-Carrenost the Second. Cohort of the First Welcoming.

key_events.PD 565: Cohort of the Lost Vespers - 117 paragon-adjacent souls flagged by Archvigil Liturge di Mauritz (name later redacted PD 584), routed to deepest hazard postings, dead in disproportionate numbers within two years. Sixth Notary's name-composition begins in hell.

timeline.name: The Long Vigil and the Standing

raw.name: The Long Vigil and the Standing

phases.spring: Iron March forward outposts fall; refugee crisis; third-tier rationing under Regent-Lord Cassian Veillarde
phases.late_summer: Coordinated three-day Horde push at Almongate, Lower Pyr, eastern curtain. Fourth Shieldwall held in place for 22 hours (rotation should have been 11).
phases.the_standing: Sergeant Halden Orriste (28) of Fourth Shieldwall holds line past mortal limit at the Almongate breach. Divine attention manifests. First Paragon in living memory. Almongate holds.
phases.same_day_curation: Four outer-course towers (the old 14, 31, 49, and the Mute) silently condemned and renumbered. Orders signed by Cantor-Magisterial Orric of the Lower Pyr.

named_participants: Halden Orriste, Sergeant 4th Shieldwall, age 28 (becomes Halidan Aurriste)
Eleven other 4th Shieldwall survivors (all dead by PD 591)
Cantor-Magisterial Orric of the Lower Pyr (dies PD 588)
Archvigil Mauritz di Cassian (dies PD 585 of wall-cough)
The Quartermaster of Tongues (offered Halden a bargain night before; refused; account OPEN)
Sub-Vicar Iohanne Vell-os-Carrenost, 28 (becomes Sebastopater Iohanne IX)
raw.buried_truth: The Quartermaster of Tongues offered Halden a bargain the night before; Halden refused; divine attention found him hours later. Halden has never been certain the attention was not the Quartermaster's second offer accepted unknowing. Account is OPEN in Long Ledger. Known only to Halidan himself.

key_events.PD 583_autumn: Polishing of the Roll begins - 'Hldn. Orriste' becomes 'Halidan Aurriste'. Initiated by Postulant-Scribe Velim of the Lower Choir, age 22 (later Cantor-Magisterial Velim Aurriste).
key_events.PD 587: Concilium Sebastinum convened by Sebastopater Cassian VII. Aspirant-rank invented to categorize a living saint. Doctrine drafted by Confessor-Ordinary Velim Aurriste.
key_events.PD 588: Ferrostat-on-the-Mire taken in 3-day sortie - first push beyond wall in 93 years. Iohanne Vell-os-Carrenost elevated to Confessor-Ordinary at 63. Postulant-Scribe Velim Aurriste strikes the Octave Bargain with Quartermaster of Tongues, age 31. Grant: doctrinal clarity. Price: 'a future silence to be named.'
key_events.PD 589: Doctrine of Rescinded Eternity promulgated by Sebastopater Cassian VII, drafted by Velim Aurriste. Calendar reform: Octave of the Standing as new year-beginning, no Year Zero.
key_events.PD 591: Sebastopater Cassian VII dies of 'fatigue of the heart' 14 months after signing the Doctrine. Confessor Mortane the Half-Kind (then young Reader-Confirmed) refused his requested aspersoir reading week before. Sebastopater Mauritz I elevated.
key_events.PD 597: Walker-Beyond-the-Wall epithet decreed - fourth liturgical title for the Paragon. Drafters: Velim Aurriste, Confessor Mortane. Secondary function: liturgical succession apparatus for the Aspirant-rank. Vesperine Loyalty Tally faction lines crystallize.
key_events.PD 599: Aspirant-Protection Ordinance signed by Sebastopater Mauritz I. Restricts independent aspersoir readings, correspondence access, lineage Stain-investigations. Enforced by Inquisitor-Vested Orric de la Pyrille.

key_events.PD 600: Cantor-Magisterial Velim Aurriste's third aspersoir reading returns UNKNOWN (prior: PD 588 CLEAN, PD 594 ANOMALY). Filed under Aspirant-Protection lineage-clause by Sebast-Auditor.
key_events.PD 601: BANNER-FALL. Eastward advance to Saintmire breaks. Three banners lost. Paragon's left hand crushed - the only major Reclamation defeat he was present at. Marshal-Vested Severa Aurriste-cadet (then line-captain) drags Captain-Pyric Cassian di Pyrille from field (Cassian dies of wounds). Cousin Cassian de la Pyrille becomes new Captain-Pyric.
key_events.PD 602: The chancery clerk's stroke - senior cross-reference officer collapses compiling keel-throne provenance index. Successor (appointed by Velim Aurriste) does not complete it. Keel-block / Paragon surname coincidence never officially linked.
key_events.PD 604: Sebastopater Mauritz I dies of 'exhaustion of the conscience' (sealed). Iohanne Vell-os-Carrenost IX elevated at age 79. Second Sebastopater in 13 years - institutional fragility.
key_events.PD 605: THE MOURNWING LETTER. Paragon writes personal letter direct to Cathedral via Mournwing courier (not via Confessor Mauritz at Ferrostat). Single question: 'How many saints have there been?' Intercepted by Confessor Mortane. Filed without answer. Mournwing courier has not been heard from since.

raw.name: PD 606 - The Present
active_threats: Cohort of the Quiet Mercy summoned (211 souls; 31 translation-shock dead; 146 oathed; 34 Refused including Battle-Mage Jin of Macau 2019 in Carcer)
Carrion Census preparing at Ferrostat - Sixth Notary drafting foreclosure-writ naming Paragon as 'clerical error to be corrected'
Confessor-Ordinary Mauritz at Ferrostat filing aspersoir readings with unauthorized notation 'quaero' (I question) - 16 times so far
Octave of the Standing approaching - Paragon invited to Sebastopyr for first return in 10 years
Cantor-Magisterial Velim Aurriste's fourth aspersoir reading scheduled third day of Octave
Murder of Confessor Thelos di Carrenost (7th Custodes seat) under investigation by Detective-prototype Inspector Nera Cazares from Earth-1947 (clue state: INFERRED)
Postulant Liturge di Carrenost reaching cursivus sebastinus minor fluency; courted for empty 7th seat by potential murderers of her uncle
Brothers of the Cinder Hand publishing The Refusal weekly with 4 Inquisitor-Vested defectors contributing
Captain-Pyric Cassian de la Pyrille's anomaly Stain-reading filed and not actioned
14 high-Stain Carcer prisoners identified by Korreth Slag-Tongue's parallel readings; 3 politically loaded; none re-read
Paragon's Grace pool 1240 (73% of peak); projected minimum-operational in 11 years; he has not been told

raw.name: Where the Records Are Curated
locations: Audit-Below stratum 6 (sealed) - Fall-era Treaty Liturgy renegotiations and defaults
Audit-Below stratum 7 - the elided PT 400 to PT 47 chronicle in cursivus sebastinus minor
Audit-Below stratum 3 - depositions of the 11 surviving Fourth Shieldwall, sealed
Audit-Below stratum 4 - Cassian VII's refused aspersoir, unsigned medical-confessional
Audit-Below stratum 2 - Pyric Bell miscount records (3 instances in 6 centuries, including Standing night - true toll 451 not 447)
Audit-Below stratum 9 - unrecorded; nature unknown to all but possibly Velim Aurriste
The Suppressed Paragon Registry - 73 named + 48 nameless = 121 closed cases, held by Cantorial Scribes / Custodes Numeri
Sealed Anomaly Ledger - 312 entries, kept by Sebast-Auditor, includes Velim Aurriste's three readings
The PD 584 requisition slip removing the 565 PD Refusers' Index - signed in cipher of a redacted Sebastopater, identifiable to Cantor-Magisterial Asperine os Veillarde
Mournwing martyr-rolls - kept separately, never shared with Cathedral; include Sixth Notary's name-composition tracking

player_brief.summary: Trimmed handout for new players — see docs/bastion/00-campaign-bible.md §X.

raw.content:
# Player Brief — What Your Character Knows Day One

*For the player. Read this tonight. Tomorrow you wake up in a city that was named for a saint who endured arrows and was clubbed to death anyway.*

---

## YOU JUST ARRIVED. HERE'S WHAT YOU NEED TO KNOW.

You don't know any of this from a book. You know it because a Veriarch in a damp grey vestment told you, because a woman in the bread line muttered it, because a Lampwarden snapped at you when you said the wrong word, because the priest who pinned a tin sigil on your collar said *welcome* and the inquisitor three steps behind him did not smile.

You have been in Sebastopyr for **less than a week**. The Cathedral calls you **one of the Called**. Some of the other people they pulled across with you have started calling themselves something else. You haven't decided yet.

This brief is the surface of what the city teaches you in your first week. It is not the truth. It is what is *taught*, *whispered*, and *worried about*. Hold both columns at once.

---

## I. THE OFFICIAL STORY (what the Church tells you, your first morning)

You are walked from the **Vocation House** in the Choir Ring to a stone bench in the **Sanctuary Ring** and given hot watered wine and a Postulant-Lector reads the **Catechism of the Called** to you. It takes nine minutes. It is the same nine minutes every cohort gets. Here is what it says, plain:

> *Soul-of-the-borne-across, you were not stolen. You were Called.*
>
> *Sebastopyr is the last bastion. The wall holds. Beyond the wall, the Hordes. Beneath the world, hell. Within the city, the only light left.*
>
> *We have been at war six hundred and six years. The war was once deemed eternal. It is no longer. Twenty-three years ago, a sergeant of the Fourth Shieldwall stood at the wall past the hour a body holds, and a divine attention found him, and we have a **Paragon** now. He commands from **Ferrostat**, beyond the wall. He has taken back ground. He will take more. You are part of the next advance.*
>
> *You will be given a station. You will be given an oath. You will be given **Grace**. You will be given a place in the only war that matters. Weep if you must. Eat. Then come to the Lector.*

That is the official voice. **It is sincere.** The Lector who read it to you was weeping a little by the end. He believed every word. That is the first hard thing about this city: **the people doing this to you are not villains**. They think they're rescuing you.

---

## II. CHURCH PUBLIC TEACHING (the four things everyone learns by their fourth day)

These get drilled into you at orientation, repeated by parish priests, painted on the walls of the Marshal Ring, sung in the **Watch-Cantos** at dusk:

| Teaching | What it actually means in practice |
|---|---|
| **The wall holds.** | The Pyric Wall has stood six centuries. Don't ask about the towers. |
| **The Paragon is the proof.** | One man's miracle reopened the future. The war is no longer deemed eternal. *(Old word: "deemed." Not "is." This matters more than you think.)* |
| **Grace is sacred. Grace is metered.** | Holy power is a fuel. The Cathedral grants it. The Cathedral can withdraw it. **You will be tested for it.** |
| **The Stain is real, and it hides.** | Damnation is a thing you can carry without knowing. The Inquisition reads souls. Most readings come back **CLEAN**. Some come back **ANOMALY**. A few come back **UNKNOWN**, and that is theologically worse than ANOMALY. |

**Things the Church does NOT teach you in week one, but assumes you know:**
- That **the Paragon is the first.** (He is *deemed* the first. No catechism says "the first." They say "*ours.*" Listen for it.)
- That your refusal is possible. (It is. But every Lector pretends it isn't.)
- That the Calling is a **Bargain**. (It is. Power for a price. They call it *a gift*.)

---

## III. LOWER WARDS WHISPERS (what you hear when no Lector is in the room)

You will end up in the **Under-Ward Ring** at some point in your first week. Maybe you go down for cheap bread. Maybe a fellow Called drags you to the **Drinkings-of-the-Last-Lamp** because she has heard there are *others*. Maybe you get lost. Down there, in the rain-light of the Cinderbelow, in tea-houses that smell like coal and incense and wet wool, you hear different things.

These are not lies. They are not facts either. They are *whispers*, and a whisper in Sebastopyr is its own kind of currency.

- **"They call you Called. We call ourselves Conscripted."** — said by someone who looks tired and is watching the door.
- **"Don't take a bargain at the Lampgate bench. Mother Aspine is lovely. The thing she works for is not."**
- **"The aspersoir doesn't read Battle-Mages right. If they read you and it ticks anomaly, that's the arcane crossing the holy. It's not damnation. Probably."**
- **"Tower count on the wall is eighty. Ask any soldier. Then ask a Ferrenkin. The number changes."**
- **"The Paragon hasn't been to the city in ten years. The chancery told him not to come."**
- **"There was a saint in five hundred eighty-one. Two years before the Standing. There's a chant for her in the archive. They don't sing it."**
- **"If your Stain reads CLEAN three times in a row, that's not clean. That's something else."**
- **"Old Pell at the Last Lamp will trade you Grace for a favor. Don't owe Old Pell more than one favor. Two is the death of you."**
- **"The Mournwing courier broke the seal on a letter last summer. The letter was the Paragon's. The letter asked a question. The chancery filed it."**

You don't know which of these are paranoia and which are load-bearing. **That is the point.** The city teaches you to live without certainty, while telling you certainty is the only thing worth dying for.

---

## IV. COMMON NEWCOMER CONCERNS (what every Called asks in their first week)

The Lectors have rehearsed answers for the first four. The fifth they pretend you didn't ask. The sixth they will report.

1. **"Can I go home?"**
   — *"You are home, soul-of-the-borne-across. The world that bore you is not the world that needs you. Drink your wine."*
   *(Actual answer: No. No one has. The rite is one-way. The Conscripted League knows of three attempts; all three are dead.)*

2. **"What if I refuse?"**
   — *"Refusal is a holy right. The Refused are tended by the Brothers of the Cinder Hand and kept in the Carcer until grace finds them."*
   *(Actual answer: You go to the Carcer. Some come out. Some are still there from cohorts twenty years past. There is a cell — number eight — with someone in it who has not aged since 591 PD and speaks only in the indicative case of a language no one alive remembers.)*

3. **"Am I damned?"**
   — *"You will be Read. The reading will tell. Most are clean. Be of comfort."*
   *(Actual answer: The reading tells you what the instrument tells you. The instrument is calibrated. The calibration has been adjusted four times in living memory. The threshold has gone **up.** Be of comfort.)*

4. **"What is Grace and why do I keep hearing about it?"**
   — *"Grace is the divine fuel of the holy work. You will be granted a measure suitable to your station. Spend it well. Rest restores it. Sin does not."*
   *(Actual answer: It's a metered pool. You spend, you run out, you can't do the holy thing anymore. The Cathedral holds the spigot. If they decide you are heretical, the spigot closes. The mercenary and the saint draw from the same well by the same rules. Belief does not refill it.)*

5. **"Why are some people on the wall called Numerati?"**
   — *(Lector glances up, then down.)* — *"The Order of the Stood Line keeps the count. It is a sacred office. You needn't trouble."*
   *(Actual answer: The Order's central doctrine is that the count of the dead **is** the holiness. They are not guardians. They are witnesses. Their motto is SUSTINEO ET NUMERO — *I endure and I count.* They don't expect to win. They expect to be counted. This is supposed to be a comfort.)*

6. **"How many saints have there been?"**
   — *(Long pause. Lector smiles.)* — *"One hundred and forty-three, child. The list is in the lectionary. Now, your station."*
   *(Actual answer: Nobody asks this. The Lectors have a rehearsed pause for it. The Paragon asked it last year, in a letter, and the letter was intercepted and never answered. **Do not be the second person to ask this in writing.**)*

---

## V. THE SUMMONERS' WELCOME vs THE WATCHFUL PRIEST AFTERWARD

This is the most important page of this brief. Read it twice.

### The Welcome (Vocation House, the moment after the rite)

There are **twenty-four summoners** in the Vocation House. They wear plain white and ash-grey, their faces are wet with sweat from the thirteen-hour rite, and when you stagger out of the door of crossing onto the cold flagstones, they **lift you up**. They wrap you in a wool blanket. The eldest of them — usually **Cantor-Magisterial Asperine os Veillarde**, a woman of sixty-two who used to sing opera in the Reliquary Ward — touches your forehead with her thumb and says, in Old-Tongue:

> *"Per Sebast — venisti. Numero te."*
> *"By the Enduring One — you came. I count you."*

She means it. She is weeping. They all are. This is, to them, the most sacred moment in their year. They have just **pulled a soul out of the dark** and into the only war that matters, and they love you for it before they know your name. They will press warm bread into your hands. One of them will say in halting Vespertine, *"You are our gift to the wall. Thank you, thank you, thank you."*

**The summoners are not lying.** They are not cynics. They believe with their whole hearts that they have just saved you. This is the Constantine heart of the whole city: **the worst thing being done to you is being done by people who are sure they are doing good, and who weep doing it.**

### The Priest Afterward (the bench in the Sanctuary Ring, nine minutes later)

After the welcome, you are walked — gently, with a hand at your elbow — out of the Vocation House and up the **Cantor's Stair** to the Sanctuary Ring. You are seated on a stone bench. You are given hot wine. A different cleric sits down across from you. He is younger. His vestments are darker. He has a small brass instrument on a chain at his belt that **ticks** when he sets it on the bench.

He smiles. He does not weep. He is **kind** and he is **watching**.

He is a **Reader** of the **Office of the Pyric Audit** — what the streets call the Inquisition. He is here, in his own words, *"only to take your first reading, child. It is the gentlest of rites. A formality."*

He places the **aspersoir** — a small clear vial of mercury with a brass tick-counter beside it — between you and him. He says a low phrase in Old-Tongue. The mercury moves a little. The brass clicks: *one, two…*

He smiles wider. *"CLEAN, soul-of-the-borne-across. As is proper."*

He writes it down in a small book with a beeswax stylus. He stands up. He pats your knee. He says, *"Welcome to the bastion. You will be very useful."*

**The gap between those two clerics is the moral spine of this city.** One of them loves you. One of them is filing you. They are wearing the same robes. They went to the same seminary. They both believe.

---

## VI. LEXICON — TERMS YOU WILL HEAR IN WEEK ONE

Memorize these. If you don't know them by the third day, someone will mark you as ignorant — which in Sebastopyr is not the same as innocent.

### THE BIG WORDS

| Term | What it means (street level) | What it actually is (deeper) |
|---|---|---|
| **GRACE** | The holy fuel. The thing in your soul the Cathedral grants you. Spent on warding, holy fire, healing, on burning the Damned. | A metered resource. `{ current, max }`. Affordability is checked. Out of Grace, the holy weapon is just a weapon. **Recovers on rest and rite, not on belief.** |
| **THE STAIN** | Damnation in a soul. What the aspersoir reads. What hides. | A hidden state. Has an **actual** value and an **apparent** value. The instrument reads the apparent. The apparent can be wrong. **Especially when it says CLEAN.** |
| **A BARGAIN** | A deal — for power, for access, for a favor. | One of three kinds: **demonic** (paid in Stain), **divine** (paid in oath to the Cathedral), **mortal** (paid in debt to a person). All three are logged. Something always collects. |
| **THE CALLED** | What the Cathedral calls you and the others pulled across in the rite. | The Cathedral's gentle word. It implies *chosen*. Cf. Conscripted, below. |
| **THE CONSCRIPTED** | What the refused and the bitter Called call themselves. | The same population as the Called, named honestly. **Which word you use is a confession of what you think happened to you.** |
| **THE PARAGON** | Halidan Aurriste. The living saint. The one who Stood. Commands from Ferrostat. | A real, weary, holy man. Forty-eight. Has a tremor in his left hand. Has not been to Sebastopyr in ten years. Asked a question last year that no one will answer. |
| **THE YOUNG TRIUMPH** | The last twenty-three years. The Reclamation. The kingdom rising. | The Reclaimed Crown of Vespertin. A modest, bleeding, daily-harassed frontier. The doctrine that grew up around the Paragon's miracle and now profits from itself. |
| **THE WALL** | The Pyric Wall. Sebastopyr's curtain. The thing the Hordes break against. | Seven concentric courses. Eighty towers on the chancery roll. Eighty-four if you ask a Ferrenkin. Four condemned the year of the Standing, which is a coincidence the chancery does not discuss. |
| **THE HORDES** | The damned host beyond the wall. The enemy. | They are real. They come at every dawn. They are the *loud* enemy. The Stain inside the city is the *quiet* one, and the city is half-blind to it. |

### THE SMALLER WORDS (heard in the first week)

- **The Pyr** — colloquial for Sebastopyr. *"Welcome to the Pyr."* Lets the speaker forget that *pyr* means *wall* and *sebast-* in its bitter sense means *the one who endured for nothing.*
- **The Cathedral** — the Cathedral of the Enduring One. The institution. Capital-C is the building; lowercase-c is the bureaucracy.
- **The Sebastopater** — the living head of the Cathedral. Currently **Iohanne Vell-os-Carrenost the Ninth**, 81 years old, blind in his left eye, carries ivory tally-beads for every soul personally summoned, weeps at every Calling. Beloved. Filtered.
- **The Order** — the Order of the Stood Line. The military arm. Witnesses, not guardians. Motto: *SUSTINEO ET NUMERO.*
- **The Inquisition / the Office / the Brass / the Tickers** — the Office of the Pyric Audit. They read Stain. They are usually polite.
- **A Lector** — a junior priest. The one who reads to you on your first day.
- **A Veriarch** — a parish priest. The one in your ward.
- **A Confessor** — a senior cleric, usually Vesperine, with the gift of reading Stain by hand. Powerful. Always watched.
- **A Vesperine** — a person of the half-kind elder bloodline. Outwardly human. More sensitive to Stain. Most of the high Inquisition is Vesperine. They are not entirely sure if they are trusted servants or hostages.
- **A Ferrenkin** — a stone-and-iron person. Grey-skinned, forge-eyed, smaller than humans. They make the wall-plate, the rail-guns, the censers. They remember things the chancery doesn't.
- **A Caer-Druin** — one of the hollowed hill-court folk. They sing the Watch-Cantos at dusk. The Cathedral claims their songs were always Vespertine. They permit this.
- **A Mournwing** — one of the winged courier folk. Black canvas wing-sheaths. They do not fly beyond the wall.
- **The aspersoir** — the Stain-reading instrument. A vial of mercury, a brass tick-counter. *CLICK* once: clean. *CLICK CLICK CLICK*: anomaly. Anything more: trouble.
- **The Vocation House** — where the Calling rite is performed. In the Choir Ring.
- **Ferrostat-on-the-Mire** — the frontier outpost. The Paragon's seat. A day's ride out, a day's ride back, more if the road is wet.
- **The Cinderbelow** — the lowest ring of the city. Hell-adjacent. The brokers work there.
- **The Carcer** — the prison-quarter. *"The Carcer"* with the article. Not *Carcer Ward.*
- **A Numerati** — a soldier of the Order who has taken the Oath of Numeration. Their hand is tattooed with a roll-number.
- **The Drinkings-of-the-Last-Lamp** — a tea-house in the Cinderbelow. Where you go when you need something nobody official will give you.

### THE OATHS-AS-EXPLETIVES (you will hear these in the first hour)

- **"Per Sebast!"** — *By the Enduring One!* A mild oath. *Damn.*
- **"Sustineo!"** — *I endure!* A weary sigh. *Of course this is happening.*
- **"Nec frangor."** — *I do not break.* Muttered. Stubborn refusal.
- **"Ad audit!"** — *To the Audit!* The bastion's equivalent of *to hell with it.* Invokes the demonic Auditor. Considered crude but used freely.
- **"Numero te."** — *I count you.* The summoners' greeting. Also said at deaths. Also said before bargains.

---

## VII. THINGS THAT WILL TRIP YOU UP (first-week mistakes)

- **Saying "you kidnapped me."** The summoners will look at you like you slapped a grandmother. They will be **hurt**. They will pray for you. They will not stop summoning.
- **Calling Grace "magic."** This is a category error and an offense. Magic is what Battle-Mages carry from their old lives. Grace is what the Cathedral grants. *They draw from different wells.* (Yours draw from a third — the Conscripted will ask which.)
- **Asking how many saints there have been.** Don't do this in writing. Don't do this near a Veriarch. If you must ask, ask Asperine os Veillarde when she is alone, after she has finished singing.
- **Owing Old Pell two favors.** One is a relationship. Two is ownership.
- **Trusting a CLEAN reading.** Clean means the instrument said clean. It does not mean clean.
- **Trusting an ANOMALY reading either.** Anomaly is what they call it when they don't want to say what it is. Battle-Mages tick anomaly on a third of readings because the arcane crosses the holy and the brass doesn't know the difference.
- **Mentioning the Quartermaster of Tongues by name.** Use *the office.* Use *the duke.* Don't use the name *Vox-Quae-In-Tenebris-Numerat.* It writes itself down when said aloud.

---

## VIII. WHAT YOU ARE (the three of you, specifically)

You are a party of three. You came across in the same rite. The Cathedral routes you by what you carry. **Archvigil Severa di Mauritz**, head of the wall-rites, took **nine seconds** to look at each of you and decide where you go. She drinks heavily in the Marshal Ring after every Calling. She did this morning too.

### THE HUMAN
You came across with nothing the Cathedral can measure. No arcane pool. No clue-engine. **The Cathedral loves this.** You are *theologically clean* — a pure vessel for Grace, eligible for the full grant, routed straight into the **Order Numerati**. You will be offered the **divine bargain** — the oath that grants you Grace in exchange for an obligation to the Order. The price is real. Read every word before you sign. Especially the **gap-where-the-saint-stood** — the mandated breath of silence between the grant-clause and the price-clause. Three hundred fourteen years of records show no one has ever used it to withdraw. **You could be the first.** *(They will be very surprised if you are.)*

### THE BATTLE-MAGE
You came across with an **arcane pool** the Cathedral did not give you. This is **theologically inconvenient**. Your magic predates their grant. They cannot take credit for it and they cannot fully trust it. You will be routed to the **Reclamation Corps** at Ferrostat — frontier work, out beyond the wall, far from the city's measurements. They will offer you Grace alongside your arcane pool, but Grace will be **rationed** and **watched**. Your aspersoir readings will run hot — **expect ANOMALY in your first reading**, which is not necessarily damnation but is *definitely* a flag in your file. A clerical category was invented two cohorts ago for people like you: **Aspirant-Reluctant.** It is a holding pattern. It means *we do not know what to do with you and we are watching.* The **Brothers of the Cinder Hand** will know your name within three days.

### THE DETECTIVE
You came across with no magic and no holy capacity — and an instinct for what people are not saying. The Cathedral has a use for this. You will be routed to the **Inquisition** or to the **Order's officer-track**. There is currently an open case the chancery is quietly looking for someone to assign: **the murder of Confessor Thelos di Carrenost**, a senior cleric of the Cantorial Scribes, killed clean three months ago. The official line is *heretical agents.* The Inquisition has not solved it. They have not really tried. **You may be offered this case.** If you accept, you will be given a tin sigil and a small allowance of Grace-by-proxy (you can call on it through a confessor). You will also be given a **partner**, who will be a Vesperine confessor with their own agenda. Treat every clue as **status: unknown** until you can mark it **proven**. *The Cathedral marks things proven all the time. They are often wrong, and they are never overruled.*

---

## IX. WHAT YOU FEAR (honestly, the three of you, on day one)

- That the rite was not a calling but a harvest.
- That the kind clerics are the dangerous ones.
- That refusal is possible but exits only into the Carcer.
- That CLEAN is worse than ANOMALY.
- That the Paragon is real, and the doctrine around him is not.
- That something you do not see is keeping a ledger on you, and the ledger started the moment the rite finished.
- That you will start to believe.
- *That you will be useful.*

---

## X. THE SHAPE OF YOUR FIRST WEEK (so you can plan tonight)

**Day 1 (today, in fiction):** The rite. The welcome. The Reader. Hot wine. A bed in the Postulants' Hall above the Vocation House. The bell at midnight that wakes you sobbing without knowing why.

**Day 2:** Catechism. Stain reading recorded. Station assigned. (Human: Order. Battle-Mage: Reclamation Corps, posting to Ferrostat pending. Detective: case file delivered.) You will be given a tin sigil with your **roll-number**. Memorize it. They will ask you for it the rest of your life.

**Day 3:** Your first descent into the Common Ring on your own errand. You will see the city without a Lector at your elbow. You will hear, for the first time, the word **Conscripted**.

**Day 4:** First contact with someone who is not what they appear. (DM will engineer this.) You will need to decide, fast, whether you trust your gut or the catechism. You will not know yet which is worse to trust.

**Day 5:** The first **Bargain** is offered to you. Could be divine. Could be mortal. Could — quietly, in a doorway in the Cinderbelow — be demonic. **The price is logged the instant it is struck.** The clerk does not need to be present. The ledger writes itself.

**Day 6:** You meet someone who knew the rite was coming for you before it did.

**Day 7:** First sabbath. The Watch-Cantos at dusk. The Caer-Druin singing in a tongue older than the Cathedral. The Cathedral folding the song into their orthodoxy in real time. You will feel something you cannot name and will not be able to ask about safely.

---

## XI. A CLOSING NOTE FROM THE DM

You are not playing heroes. You are not playing villains. You are playing people who **just arrived** in a city that thinks it saved you, that is wrong about almost everything it is most sure of, and that will hand you holy fuel and a sword and ask you to count the dead.

Faith here is **operational**. Damnation **hides**. Every deal has a **price**. The righteous are the **danger**. The official story is **curated**.

You will be told, every day, that the war is being won.

It is. A little. For now.

The lamp does not go out — yet.

**Welcome to the Pyr.**

**Per Sebast — venisti. Numero te.**
raw.title: Player Brief — What Your Character Knows Day One

structured_entries.public_definition: The divine fuel of holy work. Granted by the Cathedral. Spent on warding, holy fire, healing, and burning the Damned. Recovers on rest and rite.
structured_entries.operational_truth: A metered resource. { current, max }. Affordability is gate-checked. Belief does not refill it. The Cathedral controls the spigot and can withdraw it.
structured_entries.week_one_exposure: Heard within the first hour. Spoken by every Lector. Painted on the Marshal Ring walls.

structured_entries.public_definition: Damnation. The taint of hell in a soul. Read by the aspersoir. Most readings come back CLEAN.
structured_entries.operational_truth: Hidden state with actual and apparent values. Apparent can false-negative to CLEAN, ANOMALY, or UNKNOWN. Most advanced precisely where no one is looking.
structured_entries.week_one_exposure: First reading taken on Day One, nine minutes after the welcome rite.

structured_entries.public_definition: A deal for power, access, or favor. Three kinds: demonic, divine, mortal.
structured_entries.operational_truth: Atomic grant + price. Demonic paid in Stain, divine paid in oath, mortal paid in debt. All logged. Something collects.
structured_entries.week_one_exposure: By Day 5, the first bargain will be offered. May be any of the three kinds.

structured_entries.public_definition: Those summoned across by the rite to serve the war. Gentle, holy, chosen.
structured_entries.operational_truth: The Cathedral's preferred word. Implies chosen-ness. The same population the bitter call Conscripted.
structured_entries.week_one_exposure: Spoken constantly. The first word the summoners say to you.

structured_entries.public_definition: What the Called who see clearly call themselves.
structured_entries.operational_truth: Honest naming of the harvest. Which word a player uses is a confession of how they read the rite.
structured_entries.week_one_exposure: Heard for the first time around Day 3, in the Cinderbelow.

structured_entries.public_definition: Saint-Aspirant Halidan Aurriste. The Arrow-Stander. He-Who-Was-Looked-Upon. The Walker-Beyond-the-Wall. Commands from Ferrostat. Has reopened the future.
structured_entries.operational_truth: Forty-eight. Real. Genuinely without measurable Stain. Tremor in left hand. Black silk glove. Has not visited Sebastopyr in ten years. Asked a question in PD 605 the chancery has chosen not to answer.
structured_entries.week_one_exposure: Named on Day One in the catechism. His banner hangs in every Cathedral chapel.

structured_entries.public_definition: The twenty-three years since the Standing. The Reclamation. The kingdom rising.
structured_entries.operational_truth: The Reclaimed Crown of Vespertin. A modest bleeding frontier. The doctrine that grew up around the Paragon's miracle and profits from itself.
structured_entries.week_one_exposure: Referenced constantly as the reason for everything.

structured_entries.public_definition: The Pyric Wall. Sebastopyr's curtain. Six centuries old. The thing the Hordes break against.
structured_entries.operational_truth: Seven concentric courses. Eighty towers on the chancery roll, eighty-four true. Four silently condemned PD 583 — same year as the Standing.
structured_entries.week_one_exposure: Visible from anywhere in the upper rings. The Marshal Ring counts its towers in cadence at dawn.

structured_entries.public_definition: The damned host beyond the wall. The enemy. They come at every dawn.
structured_entries.operational_truth: Real. The loud enemy. The honest enemy. The dangerous enemy is the Stain inside the city, and the city is half-blind to it.
structured_entries.week_one_exposure: You will hear the dawn-horns from the wall in your first three nights.

structured_entries.question: Can I go home?
structured_entries.official_answer: You are home, soul-of-the-borne-across. The world that bore you is not the world that needs you. Drink your wine.
structured_entries.true_answer: No. The rite is one-way. Three known attempts to reverse it; all three dead.

structured_entries.question: What if I refuse?
structured_entries.official_answer: Refusal is a holy right. The Refused are tended by the Brothers of the Cinder Hand and kept in the Carcer until grace finds them.
structured_entries.true_answer: You go to the Carcer. Some come out. Cell eight has been occupied since PD 591 by someone who has not aged and speaks only in the indicative case of a pre-Fall Saemurian dialect.

structured_entries.question: Am I damned?
structured_entries.official_answer: You will be Read. The reading will tell. Most are clean. Be of comfort.
structured_entries.true_answer: The instrument tells you what the calibration permits. The clinical-concern threshold has been raised four times in living memory. Be of comfort.

structured_entries.question: How many saints have there been?
structured_entries.official_answer: One hundred and forty-three, child. The list is in the lectionary.
structured_entries.true_answer: Officially 143 saints over six centuries. Separately and secretly: a Suppressed Paragon registry of 73 named plus 48 nameless. The Paragon asked this question in writing PD 605. The letter was intercepted. Do not be the second person to ask in writing.

structured_entries.cathedral_view: Theologically clean vessel. Eligible for full Grace grant.
structured_entries.routing: Order Numerati. Will be offered the divine bargain (oath of Numeration) within first week.
structured_entries.first_week_pressure: Sign the oath. Read the gap-where-the-saint-stood clause. 314 years of records show no one has withdrawn.

structured_entries.cathedral_view: Theologically inconvenient. Arcane pool predates the Cathedral's grant.
structured_entries.routing: Reclamation Corps, posting to Ferrostat. Auxiliary Roll. Grace rationed and watched.
structured_entries.first_week_pressure: Aspersoir reads ANOMALY on first reading because arcane crosses holy. Filed as Aspirant-Reluctant. Brothers of the Cinder Hand will know your name within three days.

structured_entries.cathedral_view: Useful instrument. No magic, but reads what people are not saying.
structured_entries.routing: Inquisition or Order officer-track. Likely assigned the open case of Confessor Thelos di Carrenost's murder.
structured_entries.first_week_pressure: Partnered with a Vesperine confessor who has their own agenda. Clue-state machine. Cathedral marks things proven all the time and is never overruled.

entries.title: The Welcome and the Reader
entries.summary: Day One pattern: the summoners weep over each new Called with sincere love (Cantor-Magisterial Asperine os Veillarde leads the welcome).
