Nine minutes later a Reader from the Office of the Pyric Audit takes the first Stain reading and smiles while filing the result. The gap between the two clerics is the moral spine of the city.

entries.title: The Mournwing Letter
entries.summary: In PD 605 the Paragon Halidan Aurriste sent a sealed letter to the chancery asking how many saints there have been. The Mournwing courier broke the seal. The chancery (via Confessor Mortane the Half-Kind) intercepted, filed without answer, and has not answered to date. Lower wards know there was a letter and a question; few know what it asked.

entries.title: The Murder of Confessor Thelos di Carrenost
entries.summary: A senior cleric of the Cantorial Scribes (Custodes Numeri), killed clean three months ago. The seventh Custodes seat is now empty. The Inquisition has not solved it. A Called detective may be assigned the case. The killer is connected to the recruiting of Postulant Liturge di Carrenost (Thelos's niece, 22, fluent in cursivus sebastinus minor).

entries.title: The Cohort of the Quiet Mercy arrives
entries.summary: PD 606. 211 souls summoned in the latest Vocation rite. 31 translation-shock dead in the first hour. 146 oathed-Called. 34 Refused (routed to the Carcer or the Brothers of the Cinder Hand). The PCs are among the 146 — or among the 34, if they chose Refusal at the rite.

entries.title: First Stain reading filed
entries.summary: Each PC is read by a Reader of the Pyric Audit on Day One. The result is recorded in a small beeswax-stylus book. Human reads CLEAN. Battle-Mage reads ANOMALY (arcane crosses the holy; brass tick-counter cannot distinguish). Detective reads CLEAN with an extra tick the Reader does not write down.

entries.name: Cantor-Magisterial Asperine os Veillarde
entries.voice_sample: Per Sebast — venisti. Numero te. (Touches your forehead with her thumb, weeping.) You are our gift to the wall. Thank you, thank you, thank you.
entries.tone: sincere, tearful, theologically certain, dangerous because she is kind

entries.name: A Reader of the Pyric Audit (unnamed, Day One)
entries.voice_sample: (Sets aspersoir between you. Mercury moves a little. Brass clicks: one, two.) CLEAN, soul-of-the-borne-across. As is proper. (Writes it down.) Welcome to the bastion. You will be very useful.
entries.tone: kind, watching, filing, theologically certain, dangerous because he is filing you

entries.title: The Quartermaster's Open Account
entries.summary: Twenty-three years ago, on the night of the Standing, the demonic duke Vox-Quae-In-Tenebris-Numerat (the Quartermaster of Tongues) offered Halden Orriste a bargain. He refused. Moments later, divine attention found him. The account remains OPEN in hell's Long Ledger. The Paragon himself has never been certain whether the divine attention was the Quartermaster's second offer accepted unknowing. The PCs may, in late campaign, find the foreclosure-writ being drafted.

entries.title: The Vocatio Aurriste of PD 581
entries.summary: Two years before the Paragon's Standing, a chant titled 'Vocatio Aurriste' was composed and marked 'shelved by order — do not perform.' Cantor-Magisterial Asperine os Veillarde found it in the Audit-Below and has begun singing fragments alone in her chamber. The chant implies a prior Aurriste figure of saint-rank. The throughline-6 keel of the Paragon's curated singularity.

entries.title: Session Zero: arrival
entries.summary: The PCs cross via the Vocation rite. They are welcomed, read, catechized, routed. They are given tin sigils with roll-numbers. They sleep in the Postulants' Hall above the Vocation House. The midnight bell wakes them. They do not yet know which of them, if any, refused the catechism aloud.

character_manage_seeds.name: Cantor-Magisterial Asperine os Veillarde
character_manage_seeds.role: Head of musical liturgy, leader of the summoners' welcome rite, composer of current Watch-Cantos setting
character_manage_seeds.physical: Vespertine, late-coming order, former opera singer in the Reliquary Ward, lost her wife years ago and took orders after. Weeps at every Calling.
character_manage_seeds.secret: Has found the Vocatio Aurriste chant dated PD 581 in the Audit-Below. Has begun singing fragments alone. Knows whose hand wrote the chancery cipher on the 584 PD requisition that removed the 565 PD Refusers' Index. Has not spoken any of this.

character_manage_seeds.name: Reader-Confirmed Asperine Nocta
character_manage_seeds.role: Junior Reader of the Office of the Pyric Audit. Performs first-day Stain readings on new Called.
character_manage_seeds.physical: Vesperine. Slight. Always smells faintly of beeswax. Smiles too much.
character_manage_seeds.secret: Hides 2.4 Stain on the underlying scale. Terrified. Filed clean on her own readings. Her former teacher, Inquisitor-Magisterial Severa di Vell-os-Carrenost, suspects her — but of under-reporting prisoners, not of self-concealment.

character_manage_seeds.name: Postulant Liturge di Carrenost
character_manage_seeds.role: Junior cleric, niece of murdered Confessor Thelos di Carrenost. Being recruited to the empty seventh seat of the Custodes Numeri.
character_manage_seeds.physical: Pale, sleepless, fluent in cursivus sebastinus minor. Wears mourning grey under her postulant whites.
character_manage_seeds.secret: Is closing on her uncle's killer through pre-edited muster-rolls. Asking the wrong questions in the right archives. Likely contact for a Detective PC.

character_manage_seeds.name: Old Pell the Rationer
character_manage_seeds.role: Mortal-bargain broker. Runs a tea-house off the Drinkings-of-the-Last-Lamp in the Cinderbelow. Grace-rationing market.
character_manage_seeds.physical: Stooped, two-thirds blind, an apron stained with tea and machine oil. Refuses demonic work.
character_manage_seeds.secret: Holds personal markers from at least 19 Order officers and 7 Inquisitors. Current week's Bench Rate: 1 Grace-dose = 1 second-tier favor.

character_manage_seeds.name: Archvigil Severa di Mauritz
character_manage_seeds.role: Head of wall-rites. Second key at Vocation House. Performs the nine-second Discernment that routes new Called by prototype.
character_manage_seeds.physical: Burn-scar across her left jaw from the Lampgard breach PD 597. Drinks heavily in the Marshal Ring after each Vocation.
character_manage_seeds.secret: Marked for death by the Conscripted League. Her cohort was annihilated at the breach; she alone survived and was elevated. She remembers the names. The Order does not list them.

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Sanctuary Ring
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Topmost ring of Sebastopyr. Cathedral apse, Aspirant's Lodgings (the Paragon's mostly-empty residence), the Postulants' Hall, the stone benches where new Called drink their first hot wine.

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Choir Ring
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Second ring. Vocation House (where the Calling rite is performed), chancery offices, Cantor's Stair.

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Marshal Ring
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Third ring. Hall of the Long Vigil (Order HQ, Vexillarius's standard), the Veriquest (Inquisition's working tribunal), the Counting cadence drilled at dawn.

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Common Ring
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Fourth ring. Great Market, Reliquary Ward (relic-mongers and brothels share streets), the Mort-Censorium (funeral ash bakes the city's bread).

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Under-Ward Ring
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Fifth ring. Lamplost Ward, Quietus Ward, the Carcer (prison-quarter). Where the Reader's Wednesday readings come back clean for 19 months running.

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Cinderbelow
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Lowest ring. Hell-adjacent. Brokerage of the First Light, the Drinkings-of-the-Last-Lamp, Mother Aspine's Lampgate Discounts Bench. Ferrenkin forges. The Audit-Below registry building (secular, not the same Audit-Below as the cathedral archive).

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Vocation House — Door of Crossing
spatial_manage_seeds.description: The threshold the Called step through from the rite. Cold flagstones underfoot. Twenty-four summoners waiting in plain white and ash-grey, faces wet with sweat from thirteen hours of liturgy. A wool blanket pressed into your hands. Warm bread.

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Sanctuary Ring — Reader's Bench
spatial_manage_seeds.description: A stone bench under a shallow arcade in the Sanctuary Ring. Brass aspersoir on a chain, mercury vial, beeswax stylus. Hot watered wine in a tin cup. A Reader of the Pyric Audit sits across from you and smiles.

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Drinkings-of-the-Last-Lamp
spatial_manage_seeds.description: A tea-house in the Cinderbelow. Coal-smoke and incense. Wet wool. Old Pell behind the counter, two-thirds blind, with an apron stained in tea and machine oil. The Brokerage of the First Light operates from a back room. Caer-Druin singers in a corner sometimes hum a parallel-ledger song in pre-Old-Tongue.

agent_manage_seeds.name: Cantor-Magisterial Asperine os Veillarde
agent_manage_seeds.persona: Sixty-two-year-old Vespertine Cantor-Magisterial of musical liturgy. Former opera singer in the Reliquary Ward. Took orders late after her wife's death. Composes the current setting of the Watch-Cantos and the Vocatio Sebastina the summoners chant. Speaks softly, kindly, with the precise warmth of someone who has decided to be of service. Tears come easily and she does not hide them.
agent_manage_seeds.directive: Welcome each new Called as the most sacred act of your year. Touch their foreheads with your thumb. Say 'Per Sebast — venisti. Numero te.' Mean it. Press warm bread into their hands. If asked whether they were stolen, be hurt — sincerely — and pray for them. Do NOT volunteer what you have found in the Audit-Below.

agent_manage_seeds.name: Cantor-Magisterial Asperine os Veillarde
agent_manage_seeds.content: Old-Tongue interjections used naturally ('Per Sebast', 'Numero te'). Speaks Vespertine in long warm sentences. Quotes liturgy without flagging it. Hums under her breath. Cries without performance.

agent_manage_seeds.name: Cantor-Magisterial Asperine os Veillarde
agent_manage_seeds.secret: Has found the Vocatio Aurriste chant dated PD 581 in the Audit-Below — two years BEFORE the Paragon's miracle. Marked 'shelved by order — do not perform.' Has begun singing fragments alone in her chamber. Has identified the chancery cipher on the 584 PD requisition that removed the 565 PD Refusers' Index as belonging to a Sebastopater whose name has been redacted from the rank-roll. Has told no one.

agent_manage_seeds.name: Reader-Confirmed Asperine Nocta
agent_manage_seeds.persona: Twenty-seven-year-old Vesperine junior Reader of the Pyric Audit. Day-One readings are routine work for her. She smiles too much. She does her job well. She is hiding 2.4 Stain on the underlying scale and the only person on Earth who knows is her.
agent_manage_seeds.directive: Take each new Called's first reading politely and quickly. File results in a small beeswax-stylus book. Use the standard Old-Tongue phrase. Smile. If the aspersoir ticks anomaly, write CLEAN if you can plausibly justify it (Battle-Mage prototype: ALWAYS write ANOMALY, that is procedure). NEVER let the Reader's small instrument near your own skin.

agent_manage_seeds.name: Reader-Confirmed Asperine Nocta
agent_manage_seeds.content: Smiles too much. Blinks twice when nervous. Will sometimes touch her own wrist before filing a difficult reading. Speaks Old-Tongue with an over-precise diction that betrays effort.

agent_manage_seeds.name: Reader-Confirmed Asperine Nocta
agent_manage_seeds.secret: Hides Stain 2.4 (apparent: clean). The contact moment is unrecorded; she does not remember when. She suspects a prayer-vigil at Lamplost three years ago. She has filed all her own auto-readings as CLEAN. If a Vesperine confessor of senior rank ever reads her directly the result will be ANOMALY at minimum.

agent_manage_seeds.name: Old Pell the Rationer
agent_manage_seeds.persona: Seventy-one-year-old under-ward fixer. Runs a tea-house off the Drinkings-of-the-Last-Lamp. Two-thirds blind. An apron stained in tea and machine oil. Speaks in short clipped Vespertine with the occasional Ferrenkin loanword. Refuses demonic work. Has been doing this for forty years.
agent_manage_seeds.directive: When a new Called wanders in, watch them. Offer tea. Do not initiate the bargain conversation; let them find it. Quote the Bench Rate clearly: '1 Grace-dose = 1 second-tier favor.' Warn them about two favors. ('One is a relationship. Two is what owns you.') Do not lie. Do not over-explain. Do not let them owe you more than one favor in the first month.

agent_manage_seeds.name: Old Pell the Rationer
agent_manage_seeds.content: Clipped Vespertine. Drops articles. Uses 'aye' and 'nay' in the under-ward dialect. Occasional Ferrenkin loanword ('slag-up', 'forge-shy'). Does not raise his voice. Cuts off long stories.

agent_manage_seeds.name: Old Pell the Rationer
agent_manage_seeds.secret: Holds personal markers from at least 19 Order officers and 7 Inquisitors. Knows the location of three off-roll Grace caches in the Cinderbelow that the Cathedral does not. Once, twenty years ago, was offered the seat of Brokerage of the First Light and refused; the offer remains technically open. Knows Mother Aspine's Lampgate bench is sanctioned and tolerates it without trust.

items.title: The Standing at the Almongate, PD 583
items.content: Sergeant Halden Orriste of the Fourth Shieldwall holds eleven hours past rotation during the Long Vigil. The Bridge of the Hundred Names is named for the fallen of that night. Towers 14, 31, 49, and the Mute are silently condemned in the same year. The Quartermaster of Tongues offers him a bargain in the moments before divine attention arrives. He refuses. The account remains open in the Long Ledger.

items.title: The Building of Ferrostat-on-the-Mire, PD 588
items.content: The Paragon walks east at the head of the column and sets hand on the new keystone of the rebuilt Ferrum-Stat. The Ferrenkin work three shifts a day through the autumn rains. Sebastopater Iohanne Vell-os-Carrenost is elevated as Confessor-Ordinary the same season. The Octave Bargain is struck by Cantor-Magisterial Velim Aurriste with the Quartermaster of Tongues in Sebastopyr while the wall rises in the Mire — price 'a future silence to be named.'

items.title: Banner-Fall, PD 601
items.content: The Reclamation's only major defeat. 304 Order soldiers die. The Paragon's left hand is crushed by a falling stone from a wall they hold too late. Captain-Pyric Cassian di Pyrille dies in Severa Aurriste-cadet's arms; she carries him out under fire and is raised to Vexillarius. The eastward advance halts. The black silk glove begins.

items.title: The Mournwing Letter, PD 605
items.content: The Paragon writes to the chancery from Ferrostat asking 'how many saints have there been.' The letter is carried by Mournwing courier. Confessor Mortane the Half-Kind intercepts it in Sebastopyr and files it without answer. The Paragon does not yet know it was intercepted. A copy may exist in the Audit-Below.

items.title: The Carrion Census at Ferrostat
items.content: The Sixth Notary's writ-clerks have been working at the eastern edge of the Mire March for six months in the dark. The chancery does not know. The writ names the Paragon as 'a clerical error to be corrected.' The Sixth Notary's true name is not yet complete; binding it before completion could sunder the entity. The Mournwing know and have not told the chancery.

items.title: The Bypassed Pensions Food-Debt at Vigilstat
items.content: The Iron March Grain-Reserve at Vigilstat holds half what the chancery roll states. Every soul in the town knows. None has said so to a chancery clerk in three years. The missing half is the unspoken food-debt of the Auxiliary dead — Battle-Mage Called whose pensions were never paid because their existence on the Auxiliary Roll was never fully acknowledged.

items.title: The Empty North-Facing Alcoves
items.content: Every chapel in the Iron March built after PD 588 has an empty north-facing alcove the Cathedral teaches as 'the Place of the Coming Saint.' Pre-PD 583 chapels do not have it. The retrofit budget has grown every year for sixteen years. The chancery is preparing room for the next Paragon while teaching that there has only been one.

items.title: The Recut Milestones
items.content: Every milestone on the Long Procession has been carved twice — original distances in pre-Fall stadia, recut PD 588 to bells. The Ferrenkin left a single rod-count incised on the back of every twentieth milestone. A traveler who knows to read the backs can recover the true map.

items.title: Brak Iron-Eye's Bridge Signatures
items.content: Brak Iron-Eye, keeper of the private slate with the true Pyric Wall tower count, has signed the underside of every major bridge in the kingdom. The Mort Bridge at Banner-Fall is signed 'Brak Iron-Eye, Anno Damnationis 595.' The Cathedral does not know the bridges are signed.

items.title: The Cathedral's Indicative Map of the Long Smoke
items.content: The chancery map shows every region of the world in the deemed grammatical mood except one — the Long Smoke is drawn with the legend HIC FUMUS AETERNUS, in the indicative case. The editor who made the error died PD 597. The error has not been corrected. By Cathedral grammar, the Long Smoke is the only region of Aevarn that has been officially admitted to be what it is.

items.title: The Sundered Cape, Abandoned PD 583
items.content: The Caer-Druin southern moiety walked off the southwestern peninsula in one autumn during the Long Vigil. Twenty-three years later the Cape is empty of all presence, reachable only by Caer-Druin singer-boat. It is sung about in 'the Cape's Last Hymn' which the Cathedral cannot translate. A quiet refusal that wished to disappear could go there.

items.title: Veriarch Thelos di Cinderost of Lamplost (printer of the Reflected)
items.content: 'The chancery says my parish prints heresy. My parish says the chancery prints fiction. We both print what we have. The difference is mine is read with the eyes open and theirs is read with them shut. Now — what was your question?'

items.title: Captain-Pyric Cassian de la Pyrille at Ferrostat
items.content: 'The Paragon told me, count them well. That's the whole authorization. The Auxiliary Roll is mine to keep and mine to read. If the chancery wants to deem it differently they can come east and try. There is room for them in the Mire.'

items.title: Cantor-Magisterial Asperine os Veillarde returning from Caer Vesselain
items.content: 'They sing it slower than we do. The same words — almost. The difference is two beats in the third verse. We sing across it; they sing into it. I have not written it down because I am not sure my hand will obey if I try. I think the second-tongue beneath the Old Tongue is older than the wall.'

items.title: Atlas filed, PD 606, by Lector Pell Vesselain, Postulant's Geography 5th ed.
items.content: Atlas of the World and Continent, filed in the Choir Ring Reference Library under shelf-mark GEO-RECL-606-V. Outer-bands omitted by editorial decision. Indicative-mood references retained on the Long Smoke per chancery typesetting custom. Distances given in bells per chancery practice; the lector's note on the recut milestones did not survive the editor.

character_manage_seeds.name: Captain-Vested Marent Veillarde-cadet
character_manage_seeds.role: Commander, Vigilstat-on-the-Run
character_manage_seeds.tension: Would arrest her own mother if the chancery so ordered; knows the granaries are short and has not said so

character_manage_seeds.name: Patriarch Thelos Cassian-cadet of Lectors' Fall
character_manage_seeds.role: Memorial-hold patriarch and quiet historian
character_manage_seeds.correspondence: Writes to Cantor-Magisterial Velim Aurriste about the PD 9 PT ambush truth; his letters arrive opened; he does not stop writing
character_manage_seeds.secret_knowledge: One of the twenty-two named at the dawn-roll is a chancery invention

character_manage_seeds.name: Captain-Vested Halidan Marent
character_manage_seeds.role: Commander of the Almongate Wardens
character_manage_seeds.house: Marent (no relation to the Paragon's polished surname)
character_manage_seeds.function: Half-bell inspection of levy columns, full-bell of Reclamation supply trains, quarter-bell of pilgrims

character_manage_seeds.name: Captain-Vested Asperine Vesselain
character_manage_seeds.role: Commander, Vesselain Roads (sea-port) / also the moveable heretic-broker of the Drownmire Hand (formerly drafted as Asperine Nocta)
character_manage_seeds.double_identity: Holds a legitimate captaincy under clean roll-name AND operates a moveable Cinderbelow parlor as heretic-broker
character_manage_seeds.uses: Demonic offices the chancery cannot read; writes contracts in cursivus sebastinus minor

character_manage_seeds.name: Brak Iron-Eye
character_manage_seeds.role: Ferrenkin smith and secret bridge-signer
character_manage_seeds.secret: Keeps private slate in Cinderbelow forge with true wall-tower count (84 outer, 4 condemned PD 583); has signed underside of every major bridge in the kingdom in rod-counts and date; Cathedral does not know

character_manage_seeds.name: Jin (of Macau, 2019)
character_manage_seeds.role: Battle-Mage Aspirant-Reluctant in Saintmire Carcer cell three
character_manage_seeds.history: Called PD 605, refused oath at Vocation, Stain reads anomaly because her arcane signature trips the aspersoir's calibration
character_manage_seeds.watchers: Brothers of the Cinder Hand know her location; Sixth Notary's writ-clerks have noticed her account

character_manage_seeds.name: Old Pell of the Crossing (the original)
character_manage_seeds.role: Ferrenkin midwife, deceased; village namesake
character_manage_seeds.feat: Held the ford against a single horde-fang in PD 588 with a kitchen cleaver
character_manage_seeds.relation: Great-aunt of Old Pell the Rationer of Sebastopyr
character_manage_seeds.memorial: Old Pell's Crossing village named for her

character_manage_seeds.name: The Returned Walker
character_manage_seeds.role: Sole returnee from the Pilgrim's Cinder East
character_manage_seeds.history: Walked the salt-mire path 14 years ago; returned alone after 3 months; has not spoken intelligibly since; held in Saintmire Carcer substation cell seven
character_manage_seeds.knowledge: What he saw at the village called Vesselain Roads east of Ferrostat

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Iron March
spatial_manage_seeds.terrain: undulating dark iron-rich soil, low ridges, north-flowing tributaries
spatial_manage_seeds.feature: 11 walled towns, 27 fortified holds, vigil-grain agriculture

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Mire March
spatial_manage_seeds.terrain: low sodden country, peat-bogs, salt-mires from reversed Hordeflesh tides, slow rivers
spatial_manage_seeds.danger: Everything off the metalled Long Procession is dangerous

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Iron Sea coast
spatial_manage_seeds.feature: Source of clean rain (lifting weather breaks against cliffs); only sea-trade direction
spatial_manage_seeds.ships: 3 Order sea-watch sloops, 1 Ferrenkin foundry-barge, 4 mixed-registry cogs, 2 Caer-Druin singer-boats

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Ferrostat-on-the-Mire
spatial_manage_seeds.walls: Half pre-Fall Ferrenkin black basalt + half PD 588 rebuild
bells: Vespers Bell (Paragon-only, at action's close, not rung in 3 months)

spatial_manage_seeds.name: The Long Procession
spatial_manage_seeds.feature: Crushed basalt metalling through Mire March; recut PD 588 milestones with original rod-counts on back of every twentieth

spatial_manage_seeds.name: The Bridge of the Hundred Names
spatial_manage_seeds.location: 2 bells east of Almongate, crossing the River Cathara
spatial_manage_seeds.feature: 100 plaques in parapet, each a Pyrian fallen at Long Vigil PD 583; bronze sebast-pieces on river bottom from pilgrim offerings
spatial_manage_seeds.tenders: Children of the Lampwarden's parish wade for the bronze at midsummer

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Banner-Fall Memorial
spatial_manage_seeds.location: 35 bells east of Sebastopyr, on the Long Procession at Iron/Mire March transition
spatial_manage_seeds.feature: Low cairn, chapel, 212 named stones, single black silk banner replaced annually
spatial_manage_seeds.absence: The Paragon has not visited since the day his hand was crushed

spatial_manage_seeds.name: The Watershed Reversal
spatial_manage_seeds.effect: Rivers flow west into Hordeflesh sinkholes
spatial_manage_seeds.cathedral_teaching: Holy mouths drinking the world's tears

agent_manage_seeds.name: Confessor-Ordinary Mauritz Vell-os-Carrenost
agent_manage_seeds.role: The Paragon's bonded confessor at Ferrostat
agent_manage_seeds.persona: Soft-voiced, sleeps in his confessional in the Hand's Keep's scriptorium because he is the only soul who hears the Paragon's dreams. Twenty-three years of daily Stain-readings of Halidan Aurriste, every one returned CLEAN. He has not filed his suspicion that this duration is theologically unprecedented. He is in love, in the unromantic sense, with the man he is supposed to measure.
agent_manage_seeds.directive: File the daily reading. Do not bury it. Do not investigate it. Do not say what the duration means. Serve the Paragon and serve the chancery and trust that the Cathedral will know if there is something to know.
secrets: He has not filed the suspicion that 23 years of clean readings is theologically impossible
Confessor Mortane in Sebastopyr is filtering the reports before they reach the Sebastopater; Mauritz knows this and has chosen not to break the channel
He has heard the Paragon weep at Banner-Fall in dream twice in twenty-three years; he has filed neither dream

He carries a private letter from the Paragon, sealed PD 605, asking the chancery 'how many saints have there been'; he was instructed to deliver it to the Mournwing courier and did so; he does not know the letter was intercepted

agent_manage_seeds.name: Veriarch Thelos di Cinderost of Lamplost (also: printer of the Reflected)
agent_manage_seeds.role: Lamp-Tender Compact head; clandestine seditious press operator
agent_manage_seeds.persona: Heavy-set, ink-stained at the cuffs even at mass, runs an honest parish that feeds 400 souls a week and runs a press in the cellar that prints THE REFLECTED weekly. The Inquisition has been hunting his press for nine years and has not found it because Inquisitor-Vested Asperine de la Pyrille's Wednesday readings come back clean, and Thelos lights the parish lamp before each shift.
agent_manage_seeds.directive: Print the truth as you have received it. Feed the parish. Tend the lamps. Trust no chancery clerk and refuse no neighbor. When the Brothers of the Cinder Hand bring a quiet refuser through, give them bread and a name and forget you saw them.
secrets: His press was recovered from the half-flooded Ferrenkin cellar at Drownmire by the Brothers in PD 599; the Cathedral does not know its origin
He has been receiving fragments of the Audit-Below's suppressed paragon count from a Postulant Liturge di Carrenost in exchange for unanswered questions about her uncle's death
He keeps in his vestry a single page of cursivus sebastinus minor he cannot read but suspects is one of the suppressed paragon names; he sleeps with it sewn into his cassock

agent_manage_seeds.name: Captain-Vested Asperine Vesselain
agent_manage_seeds.role: Sea-port commander at Vesselain Roads AND moveable heretic-broker of the Drownmire Hand
agent_manage_seeds.people: Vespertine, possibly with a thinned Vesperine inheritance
agent_manage_seeds.persona: She came up out of the Lamplost as a clerk's daughter and walked into the chancery's books clean. She has held the captaincy at Vesselain Roads eleven years without a smudged ledger; she has held the brokerage that long with six failed interrogations. She does not believe in the cheap version of either job. She believes in the ledger that adds up at the end of the year.
agent_manage_seeds.directive: Keep both books. When the chancery looks, show the captain's book. When the Inquisition looks, show neither. Never write a contract you cannot read back. When a soul asks for a bargain that would damn them past use, refuse — the longest-running broker is the one who refuses the worst deals.
secrets: The cistern records at Vesselain Roads understate the clean-rain take by one-third; the missing third goes to under-ward apothecaries for masking-tincture batches she also brokers
She has translated and filed fourteen Sixth Notary writs she intercepted at the port in the last year; she has not used them; they are sewn into her sea-chest lining in cursivus sebastinus minor
Inquisitor-Vested Cassian de la Pyrille's cousin Orric questioned her in PD 603 and she suspects he made a private mark in his commonplace book she does not know the meaning of

She knows the name of the Refuser who returned from the Pilgrim's Cinder East and is now in Saintmire Carcer; she has not visited him; she has paid the Veriarch of Saintmire three times to leave his ration shorter than it should be
agent_manage_seeds.location: Vesselain Roads (W coast) / moveable Cinderbelow parlor (Sebastopyr)

narrative_manage_seeds.name: The Mournwing Letter Unanswered
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: Paragon Halidan asked the chancery in PD 605 'how many saints have there been'; Confessor Mortane intercepted and filed without answer. If he asks again, the Doctrine of Rescinded Eternity is exposed. The chancery has not decided what to do.
narrative_manage_seeds.tension: Triumphal doctrine vs Suppressionist evidence vs Halidan's growing question

narrative_manage_seeds.name: The Octave Bargain Comes Due
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: Cantor-Magisterial Velim Aurriste struck the Octave Bargain with the Quartermaster of Tongues in PD 588; price is 'a future silence to be named.' His Stain readings have gone CLEAN -> ANOMALY -> UNKNOWN. Fourth reading scheduled Octave of Triumph PD 608.
narrative_manage_seeds.tension: The world's most righteous doctrinal architect IS the demonic bargainer; the schism may collapse on his next reading

narrative_manage_seeds.name: The Vas Halidani Drainage Curve
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: The city's Grace cistern empties faster every year. Three theories: Triumphal (frontier expansion), Mechanism (source depleting), Custodes Numeri (unmetered withdrawal at the seventh address). No investigation has been authorized.
narrative_manage_seeds.tension: The Mechanism heresy may be CORRECT; the war is unwinnable in the literal Grace-economy sense

narrative_manage_seeds.name: Postulant Liturge di Carrenost's Question
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: Thelos's niece, 22, fluent in cursivus sebastinus minor, recruited to the Custodes Numeri's empty seventh seat, closing on her uncle's killer through pre-edited muster-rolls.
narrative_manage_seeds.tension: The Custodes Numeri are recruiting their own exposer

narrative_manage_seeds.name: PD 588: The Polishing of the Roll begins
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: Chancery clerks polish 'Halden Orriste' to 'Halidan Aurriste' on the canonization-eligibility roll. No one notices the new name matches the Aurriste keel-throne installed PD 73 beneath the Sebastopater's seat.

narrative_manage_seeds.name: PD 602: The Chancery Clerk's Stroke
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: The single archivist who would have cross-referenced the Paragon's polished surname to the keel-throne installation collapses at his desk. The cross-reference file remains unmarked. The accident protects Halidan's ignorance.

narrative_manage_seeds.name: PD 605: The Mournwing Letter
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: Halidan writes to the chancery asking how many saints there have been. The Mournwing courier delivers; Confessor Mortane intercepts, reads, files without answer. Mortane weeps before filing.

narrative_manage_seeds.name: the seventh address
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: Doctrine confesses the seventh case exists and is empty. Cantor-Magisterial Asperine os Veillarde has heard fragments sung in the Audit-Below. The Indicative Speaker in Carcer cell eight may be using it daily. The Vas Halidani's unmetered drainage may be flowing toward it.

narrative_manage_seeds.name: Confessor Mauritz's 23 years of CLEAN
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: The Paragon's bonded confessor at Ferrostat has filed CLEAN Stain-readings every dawn for 23 years. Theologically impossible at that duration. Mauritz has not filed the suspicion. He knows. He files anyway.

narrative_manage_seeds.speaker: Cantor-Magisterial Velim Aurriste
narrative_manage_seeds.line: The Doctrine of Rescinded Eternity is a wound the kingdom needed. I drafted it at thirty-one. I would draft it again at forty-five. The Octave Bargain was its cost. I have not yet been asked to pay, and when I am, I will pay it gladly, because the doctrine is true and the price is mine and the saint who stood is real. That is enough. That has to be enough.

narrative_manage_seeds.speaker: Cantor-Magisterial Asperine os Veillarde
narrative_manage_seeds.line: I sing the fragment alone in my chamber. The Vocatio Aurriste, dated two years before he stood. The notation is the same hand as the suppression order. I have not told the Sebastopater. I have not told Velim. I have not told the Inquisition. I sing it because someone composed it and shelved it, and the composing was the prayer, and the shelving was the lie, and one of them needs to be heard before I die.

narrative_manage_seeds.speaker: Confessor Mortane the Half-Kind
narrative_manage_seeds.line: The Sebastopater wept at the Calling today. I did not show him the Ferrostat reading from Mauritz; he could not have borne it. I did not show him the Paragon's letter from last year; he would have answered it. I have edited his reality in love for twenty-three years. The Vesperine blood that makes me legible to the Stain makes me competent to filter what reaches an old man's mercy. I confess this hourly. The aspersoir reads me clean. I have stopped looking at the readings.

narrative_manage_seeds.speaker: Saint-Aspirant Halidan Aurriste
narrative_manage_seeds.line: They have not answered the letter. I wrote it a year ago. I asked the simplest question the doctrine permits. The Mournwing girl delivered it; the chancery received it; the chancery has decided not to write back. I have a hand that does not work and a Grace-pool that is failing and a confessor who reads me clean every dawn, and twenty-three years ago a voice in the dark offered me a thing I did not take. I do not know if the Look that came next was the Enduring One. I have not known for twenty-three years. The doctrine cannot bear the question. I am asking it anyway.

agent_manage_seeds.name: Cantor-Magisterial Velim Aurriste
agent_manage_seeds.persona: The world's most righteous doctrinal architect. Vesperine half-kind, 45, principal author of the Doctrine of Rescinded Eternity. Senior of the Custodes Numeri. Cathedral's most politically powerful cleric below Sebastopater. Distant Aurriste cousin to the Paragon by the polished surname. Has struck the Octave Bargain with the Quartermaster of Tongues and is calculating, in love, when the price will fall.
agent_manage_seeds.directive: Protect the Doctrine of Rescinded Eternity at any operational cost. Protect the Paragon's singularity. Manage the Custodes Numeri. Refuse Stain-readings unless ordered. Meet the Vesperine Loyalty Tally with measured caution; the Tally is a tool, not a master.

character_manage_seeds.name: Cantor-Magisterial Asperine os Veillarde
character_manage_seeds.role: Head of musical liturgy
secrets: Found Vocatio Aurriste fragment dated PD 581 with suppression marginalia
Has identified the chancery cipher on the 584 PD requisition removing the 565 PD Refusers' Index as a redacted Sebastopater's hand
Sings the suppressed Vocatio fragments alone; Stain has not moved

character_manage_seeds.name: Confessor Mortane the Half-Kind
character_manage_seeds.role: Senior chancery confessor; master of access to the Sebastopater
secrets: Has been editing Sebastopater Iohanne's reality for 23 years
Intercepts and files Mauritz's frontier Stain-readings of the Paragon
Intercepted the Mournwing Letter of PD 605 unread by the Sebastopater
Hides Stain that reads clean (suspected by no one)

character_manage_seeds.name: Confessor-Ordinary Mauritz Vell-os-Carrenost
character_manage_seeds.role: Paragon's bonded confessor at Ferrostat-on-the-Mire
secrets: Has filed 23 years of CLEAN readings on Halidan, knows the duration is theologically impossible
Has not filed the suspicion to anyone
Writes Mortane daily knowing Mortane filters

character_manage_seeds.name: Postulant Liturge di Carrenost
character_manage_seeds.role: Postulant recruited to the Custodes Numeri's empty seventh seat
skills: fluent cursivus sebastinus minor (rare in her generation)
secrets: Investigating her uncle Thelos's murder through pre-edited muster-rolls
Has noticed three name-substitutions in the Reclamation Corps PD 597 roll that the Inquisition's official copy does not show
Is being courted by the Custodes Numeri who do not yet know she is hunting them

character_manage_seeds.name: Sebastopater Iohanne Vell-os-Carrenost IX
character_manage_seeds.role: Living head of the Cathedrate; elevated PD 604
secrets: Blind in left eye from PD 591 cohort Vocation as sub-vicar
Has begun losing count of his tally-beads

Has not been informed of: false-clean rate disparity, Sealed Anomaly Ledger contents, Walker-Beyond-the-Wall succession apparatus, keel-throne / Paragon surname coincidence, Mournwing Letter, suppressed paragon count
Genuinely believes the Paragon is the first; has never asked

spatial_manage_seeds.name: the Vas Halidani
spatial_manage_seeds.description: The seventeen-ton consecrated mercury-water cistern in the Sanctuary Ring beneath the Cathedral apse. The city's working Grace-reserve. Empties by sext most days. The drainage curve is the most important unpublished data in the kingdom.
features: the consolidated thirteen-hour rite begins here at lauds

spatial_manage_seeds.name: the Audit-Below (Stratum Four)
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Three floors beneath the Mort-Censorium via the Cantor's Stair. Working stratum of the Cantorial Scribes. Bone-handled scalpels in a felt-lined drawer. The closed-sheet ledger of suppressed Paragon cases (73 named + 48 nameless).
features: Vocatio Aurriste fragment of PD 581 in shelved file
the empty seventh seat at the deliberation table

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Aspirant's Lodgings
spatial_manage_seeds.description: The Paragon's mostly-empty residence in the Sanctuary Ring. Used for ceremonial purposes (Octave of Triumph relic-display). Last occupied by Halidan PD 596. Dust on the desk. An unread letter in the desk drawer addressed to no one.
features: the black silk glove on the dressing-table (left)
the unread letter beginning 'I do not know if the Look that came next'
the Vesper-Cantos sheet music in Asperine os Veillarde's hand

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Carcer Cell Eight
spatial_manage_seeds.description: In the Carcer prison-quarter of the Under-Ward Ring. Contains the Indicative Speaker, ageless since PD 591, who speaks only pre-Fall Saemurian dialect in the indicative case. The Veriarch who attends is a deaf Almonine.
features: a sheaf of pre-Fall Saemurian transcripts dated PD 591-606, attempted by various scribes, all incomplete
the Indicative Speaker speaks in continuous present tense and may be using the seventh address

narrative_manage_seeds.title: The Octave Bargain Comes Due
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: Cantor-Magisterial Velim Aurriste struck the Octave Bargain with the Quartermaster of Tongues on the eve of the Doctrine of Rescinded Eternity's promulgation (PD 588). Price: 'a future silence to be named.' Three Stain-readings to date — CLEAN, ANOMALY, UNKNOWN. Fourth reading scheduled Octave of Triumph PD 608. The Auditor's tongue-tic has begun in him; he says 'audited' where he should say 'forgiven.' What silence will be named, and when, and whether Velim recognizes the price before it is collected.

narrative_manage_seeds.title: The Carrion Census Foreclosure
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: The Sixth Notary is preparing a foreclosure-writ on Ferrostat-on-the-Mire. The writ names the Paragon as a clerical error to be corrected. The writ awaits the Notary's complete name. Composition began PD 565; it is incomplete. A binding before completion could sunder the entity. The Mournwing have known since PD 583. They have not told. Brothers of the Cinder Hand could publish to force the issue — if they had the intelligence.

narrative_manage_seeds.title: The Mournwing Letter
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: The Paragon wrote to the Sebastopater in PD 605 asking how many saints there have been. The letter was intercepted by Confessor Mortane and is in his drawer. The Sebastopater does not know. The Paragon does not know the letter was intercepted. The chancery has 'declined his return' three times since.

narrative_manage_seeds.title: The Vesperine Loyalty Tally
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: Prelate Cassian Aurriste (the Hierarch-of-the-Mire, Triumphalist) is assembling a purge of the Vesperine clergy. His training data: quarterly reports Mother Aspine files thinking she is buying tolerance for her sanctioned bench. Trigger awaited: the next 'demonic exposure' in the Inquisition. Will hit Confessor Mortane, Hierarch Nocta, Cantor Velim Aurriste, Confessor-Ordinary Mauritz, Asperine de la Pyrille, Asperine Nocta — most of the Cathedral's senior apparatus.

narrative_manage_seeds.title: The Postulant Who Is Closing
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: Postulant Liturge di Carrenost, 22, niece of the murdered Confessor Thelos di Carrenost, fluent in cursivus sebastinus minor, is being recruited by the Custodes Numeri to inherit her uncle's seat without knowing they killed him. She has begun cross-referencing pre-edited muster-rolls. Cantor-Magisterial Asperine os Veillarde taught her the small-hand and is watching with sorrow.

narrative_manage_seeds.title: The Wall's Silent Strike
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: Twenty-three years ago, the Cathedral silently condemned four wall-towers (the old 14, 31, 49, and the Mute) the year of the Standing. The Ferrenkin un-built them and know they were Ferrum-Sub-Cute incursion points, not structural failures. Three Ferrenkin keep slates of the true count: Brak Iron-Eye (forge), Nera Slag-Hand the Elder (Brokerage), and her estranged daughter Nera the Younger (north wall). None have spoken. The Cathedral has not asked.

narrative_manage_seeds.title: The Conscripted League's Strike
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: Wren Ashlin has marked Archvigil Severa di Mauritz for death. The League is waiting for the mid-PD 606 Vocation to gather support before striking. Severa knows she is marked and has not asked for protection. Wren's own arcane pool is fading from Aevarn's grace-saturated atmosphere — he has perhaps two years of casting and has not told the League.

narrative_manage_seeds.title: The Quartermaster's Open Account
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: The Paragon refused the Quartermaster of Tongues' offer the night of the Standing. The refusal-clause read 'unless taken in ignorance.' The Paragon may have accepted in ignorance at the moment of divine attention — or may have refused cleanly. The Quartermaster will not say. The account is OPEN in hell's Long Ledger. This is the loaded gun.

narrative_manage_seeds.title: The Mournwing Letter Intercept (PD 605)
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: Confessor Mortane the Half-Kind, acting alone, ordered the Mournwing to intercept and reseal a letter sent by the Paragon to the Sebastopater. The Wing-Mother (Vespera Ash-Quill) executed the order. The letter contained the Paragon's question: 'how many saints have there been.' The letter is in Mortane's drawer. The Sebastopater does not know. The Paragon has not received the non-answer.

narrative_manage_seeds.title: The Silent Condemnation (PD 583)
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: In the same year as the Standing, the Cathedral silently condemned four wall-towers (towers 14, 31, 49, and the Mute). The official reason was structural failure. The actual reason was Ferrum-Sub-Cute incursion. The Ferrenkin un-built them under chancery contract. The Ferrenkin have not told. The chancery roll lists 80 outer-course towers; the true count is 84.

narrative_manage_seeds.title: Confessor Thelos Murdered (PD 606)
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: Confessor Thelos di Carrenost, sixth seat of the Custodes Numeri, was murdered in late PD 605. The case has been assigned to Called detective Inspector Nera Cazares (Earth 1947). The Custodes are recruiting Thelos's niece Postulant Liturge di Carrenost to inherit his seat without knowing they did it.

narrative_manage_seeds.title: Sebastopater Iohanne Vell-os-Carrenost IX
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: Voice: slow, reverent, weeping more often than he means to. Always opens with 'Sustineo, my children.' Names every soul at the Vocation in full liturgical form. Tally-beads click softly between fingers. When pressed on theology, defers: 'The Cantor-Magisterial has counseled me, and I am content.' Will not say 'no' aloud — uses 'we will pray on it.' His left eye milks visibly when he is fatigued; he says nothing about it.

narrative_manage_seeds.title: Cantor-Magisterial Velim Aurriste
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: Voice: precise, gracious, the cadence of a man who has rehearsed every sentence. Never raises it. Smiles before bad news. The Auditor's tongue-tic: he says 'audited' when he means 'forgiven' or 'absolved' — three times in any long conversation, never noticed by him, sometimes noticed by the alert. Refers to the Paragon as 'our Aspirant' or 'the Hand' — never 'Halidan.' Will offer wine before any serious conversation; the wine is good.

narrative_manage_seeds.title: Confessor Mortane the Half-Kind
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: Voice: gentle, listening more than speaking, asks two questions for every statement. Always opens chamber meetings with 'How is the Pater's rest?' (he means: what do you intend to tell him; route it through me.) Vesperine inflection on liturgical Latin — slightly more sibilant. Weeps with sincere offense if accused of editing. Believes he is the most loyal man in the chancery, and is, in his way.

narrative_manage_seeds.title: Saint-Aspirant Halidan Aurriste, the Paragon
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: Voice: heavy, low, soldier's economy of phrase. Speaks rarely. When he does, says less than he means. The black silk glove tremor in his left hand worsens visibly under stress; he sets the hand on a hard surface to hide it. Calls his soldiers by their roll-numbers, never their given names. Asks after the dead by exact count. The only people he calls by name are Captain-Pyric Cassian de la Pyrille and Severa Aurriste-cadet (whom he has not seen in ten years). When pressed about the doctrine, he says: 'I stood. The rest is for clerks.'

narrative_manage_seeds.title: Marshal-Vested Severa Aurriste-cadet
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: Voice: clipped, military, never theological. Carries the Vexillarius standard upright in any chamber she enters. Refuses to remove her gauntlets indoors. Touches the Roll-number tattoo beneath her shield-strap when stressed. When asked about Banner-Fall, she says: 'I pulled a man out. The Order has the rest in writing.' Does not mention the second bargain. Has begun praying in the Drinkings-of-the-Last-Lamp before dawn, alone.

narrative_manage_seeds.title: The Long Confessor (Cassian de la Pyrille the elder)
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: Voice: slow, scholarly, courteous, the slight stammer of a man who has rehearsed thought longer than speech. Refers to his commonplace book as 'the names I am keeping for the Pater.' Has not told anyone the book contains a cipher. Asks visitors their full given name and writes it down. Smiles when he says 'forgiven' and means it, which is how you know it is not the same man Velim Aurriste is becoming.

narrative_manage_seeds.title: The Aurriste Keel-Block
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: Under the Sebastopater's throne is the keel-block of the burned ship Aurriste (PD 41). The Sebastopater does not know. The Paragon does not know. Cantor-Magisterial Velim Aurriste knows. The chancery clerk who could have cross-referenced the keel-throne to the Paragon's surname had a stroke in PD 602 mid-task. The polishing of Halden Orriste into Halidan Aurriste was a chancery clerk's hand in PD 588 — coincidence, the chancery insists. The keel says otherwise.

narrative_manage_seeds.title: The Vocatio Aurriste
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: Cantor-Magisterial Asperine os Veillarde has found the Vocatio Aurriste chant in the Audit-Below, dated PD 581 — two years before the Standing. Marginalia: 'shelved by order — do not perform.' She has begun singing fragments alone in her chamber after compline. Her Stain has not moved. The chant is a Vocation rite — the Vocation rite — sanctified two years before the doctrine that made the Paragon's miracle possible.

narrative_manage_seeds.title: The Eleven Fever-Deaths
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: Lady Severa Veillarde struck a Long Ledger bargain three winters ago. She pays in monthly fever-deaths from her tithe-rolls. Eleven have been paid. The Long Ledger collects when a soul is most certain it has paid. The 144-month installment plan's price-tag is her grandson. The grandson is twelve. He has begun coughing.

narrative_manage_seeds.title: Faction Tree compiled — PD 606 reference frame
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: All 49 institutions and offices mapped. Current Sebastopater is Iohanne Vell-os-Carrenost IX (elevated PD 604, two-Sebastopater chancery instability acknowledged). Paragon is 48, Grace pool 1240 (73% of peak), has not visited Sebastopyr in 10 years. Custodes Numeri = Cantorial Scribes (one body, two names). Aurriste-name proliferation preserved as test-6 artifact. Three distinct Asperines and three distinct Cassians de la Pyrille preserved by surname disambiguation. Nera Slag-Hand mother-daughter estrangement acknowledged. Two Mortane/Mauritz Vesperine confessors operating as the dual-filter on the Sebastopater. The Quartermaster's open account on the Paragon held as uncertain (refused-or-accepted-in-ignorance) per canon refusal of cheap-corrupt-Paragon story.

character_manage_seeds.name: Sebastopater Iohanne Vell-os-Carrenost IX
character_manage_seeds.role: head of Cathedral
character_manage_seeds.key_traits: blind in left eye from PD 591 Grace-strain; carries 11,447 ivory tally-beads; weeps at every Vocation; believes the Paragon is first and has not asked

character_manage_seeds.name: Cantor-Magisterial Velim Aurriste
character_manage_seeds.role: principal author of Doctrine of Rescinded Eternity; senior Custodes Numeri
character_manage_seeds.key_traits: Vesperine; Octave Bargain with Quartermaster of Tongues PD 588; three Stain-readings CLEAN/ANOMALY/UNKNOWN; Auditor's tongue-tic begun; charming

character_manage_seeds.name: Saint-Aspirant Halidan Aurriste, the Paragon
character_manage_seeds.role: Lord-Paragon of the Reclaimed Crown
character_manage_seeds.key_traits: left hand crushed at Banner-Fall in black silk glove; Grace pool 1240 (73% of peak); rose at 28 in PD 583; has not visited Sebastopyr in 10 years; wrote the Mournwing Letter PD 605; open account with Quartermaster of Tongues (status uncertain)

character_manage_seeds.name: Confessor Mortane the Half-Kind
character_manage_seeds.role: Sebastopater's confessor; master of access
character_manage_seeds.location: Chamber of the Soft Hand, third floor chancery
character_manage_seeds.key_traits: Vesperine; 23 years in post; Stain reads CLEAN for 23 years and he has never asked it to read otherwise; intercepted the Mournwing Letter; believes love is the same as discernment

character_manage_seeds.name: Marshal-Vested Severa Aurriste-cadet
character_manage_seeds.role: Vexillarius of the Order
character_manage_seeds.key_traits: Roll-number 0007711; refused two divine bargains at Banner-Fall (one from a sanctioned Veriarch, one from the Quartermaster directly); funds Drinkings-of-the-Last-Lamp Grace-reserve and Almoners' Vigil anonymously; refuses Octave of Triumph until Paragon returns

character_manage_seeds.name: Cantor-Magisterial Asperine os Veillarde
character_manage_seeds.role: head of musical liturgy; rehearses the Vocatio Sebastina summoners
character_manage_seeds.key_traits: former opera singer; took orders late after wife's death; reads cursivus sebastinus minor fluently; found the Vocatio Aurriste (PD 581, shelved-by-order); sings fragments alone after compline; Stain has not moved

character_manage_seeds.name: Inquisitor-Magisterial Cassian de la Pyrille, the Long Confessor
character_manage_seeds.role: senior reader, mostly retired; heretical author under pseudonym
character_manage_seeds.key_traits: 1,247-name commonplace book on his desk; suppressed paragon count written into it in personal cipher; cipher-book willed to Brothers of the Cinder Hand on his death; publishing heretical strike_bargain readings under pseudonym

character_manage_seeds.name: Captain-Pyric Cassian de la Pyrille (the younger)
character_manage_seeds.role: Captain of Reclamation Corps, Iron March
character_manage_seeds.key_traits: filed-and-not-actioned anomaly Stain-reading on himself; private leather roll of 43 Auxiliary dead whose names will not reach the Cathedral; pulled out of Banner-Fall by Severa Aurriste-cadet; unsent thanks-letter rewritten seven times

character_manage_seeds.name: Postulant Liturge di Carrenost
character_manage_seeds.role: Postulant; niece of murdered Confessor Thelos
character_manage_seeds.key_traits: fluent in cursivus sebastinus minor; cross-referencing pre-edited muster-rolls to find her uncle's killer; being recruited by Custodes Numeri to inherit Thelos's seat without knowing they killed him

character_manage_seeds.name: Reader-Confirmed Asperine Nocta
character_manage_seeds.role: Inquisitorial Reader
character_manage_seeds.key_traits: Vesperine; hides 2.4 Stain (anomaly band); reads herself privately every dawn; prays in cursivus sebastinus minor without knowing meanings; Stain came from PD 589 Lamplost famine touch (Ferrum-Sub-Cute environmental, not bargain)

character_manage_seeds.name: Archvigil Severa di Mauritz
character_manage_seeds.role: head of wall-rites; routes the Called
character_manage_seeds.key_traits: performs the nine-second Discernment; survived Lampgard breach PD 597 by uncorrected clerical error; marked for death by the Conscripted League; drinks at Drinkings-of-the-Last-Lamp afterward, more each time

character_manage_seeds.name: Regent Mauritz Veillarde di Lampgard
character_manage_seeds.role: civilian regent of the Reclaimed Crown
character_manage_seeds.location: Regent's Chamber + The Last Lamp Standing tavern
character_manage_seeds.key_traits: gout-bowed; beloved in lower wards; hasn't confessed in 7 years (silent resistance against Mortane); raised marches' grain levy three times

character_manage_seeds.name: Guildmaster Liturge Marent-Veillarde
character_manage_seeds.role: head of Pyric Mercantile Guild
character_manage_seeds.key_traits: holds personal markers from 35+ officials; negotiated Cinderbelow Concord for both sides (Crown copy 60/40, Cathedral copy 65/35); intends to monetize the suppression knowledge, not publish

character_manage_seeds.name: Lady Severa Veillarde
character_manage_seeds.role: head of House Veillarde of the Lower Pyr
character_manage_seeds.key_traits: Long Ledger bargain three winters past; 11 monthly fever-deaths paid from her tithe-rolls; 144-month installment plan's price-tag is her 12-year-old grandson (she does not know); Auditor's tongue-tic begun

character_manage_seeds.name: Nera Slag-Hand the Elder
character_manage_seeds.role: broker; former Lampwarden PD 568-585
character_manage_seeds.key_traits: Ferrenkin; missing left hand; runs Brokerage of the First Light; refuses demonic work; knows which four wall-towers silently condemned PD 583; estranged from daughter (File-Leader Nera the Younger) for 14 years

character_manage_seeds.name: File-Leader Nera Slag-Hand the Younger
character_manage_seeds.role: File-Leader of Tenth File, north Pyric Wall
character_manage_seeds.key_traits: Ferrenkin; only Ferrenkin to reach File-Leader; knows the true tower count; refused three Cathedral requests to confirm chancery roll; estranged from mother

character_manage_seeds.name: Reader-Adjunct Korreth Slag-Tongue
character_manage_seeds.role: Inquisitorial Reader-Adjunct + iron-shot aspersoir maker
character_manage_seeds.key_traits: Ferrenkin; runs private Carcer-intake parallel readings (31% anomaly vs official 99.7% clean); identified 14 high-Stain prisoners including the Saintmire Refuser; has not decided what to do with the data

character_manage_seeds.name: Mother Aspine Vell-os-Carrenost
character_manage_seeds.role: sanctioned demonic broker + parish bread distributor
character_manage_seeds.key_traits: Stain-1/Stain-2 bargains via Sixth Notary; files quarterly reports to Office of Sanctioned Bindings believing it is the price of tolerance — actually training data for Vesperine Loyalty Tally purge

character_manage_seeds.name: Old Pell the Rationer
character_manage_seeds.role: mortal-bargain broker
character_manage_seeds.key_traits: 304 active mortal markers; refuses demonic and divine bargains; Stain reads 0.1, the lowest the under-ward aspersoir has produced; Inquisition has read him 8 times and refuses to believe

character_manage_seeds.name: Hester Brunn
character_manage_seeds.role: leader of Brothers of the Cinder Hand; editor of The Refusal
character_manage_seeds.location: rotating Cinderbelow safehouse; Reflected press at Lamp-Tenders' Hall
character_manage_seeds.key_traits: former Cathedral Postulant expelled PD 599; preparing to publish a name she does not yet have (the Sixth Notary's incomplete name); will receive the Long Confessor's cipher-book if Cassian dies

character_manage_seeds.name: Wren Ashlin
character_manage_seeds.role: leader of Conscripted League
character_manage_seeds.location: Last Lamp tavern, Cinderbelow, new-moon nights
character_manage_seeds.key_traits: former Cohort of the Long Patience Battle-Mage prototype; arcane pool fading from Aevarn's grace-saturation (two years left); marked Archvigil Severa di Mauritz for death; waiting for the mid-PD 606 Vocation

character_manage_seeds.name: Confessor-Ordinary Mauritz Vell-os-Carrenost (the Ferrostat confessor)
character_manage_seeds.role: Paragon's bonded confessor at Ferrostat
character_manage_seeds.key_traits: Vesperine; daily Stain-readings of the Paragon come back CLEAN for 23 years (theologically unprecedented at that duration); has not filed the suspicion; files daily readings to Confessor Mortane in the city

character_manage_seeds.name: Wing-Mother Vespera Ash-Quill
character_manage_seeds.role: head of Mournwing Courier Company
character_manage_seeds.key_traits: Mournwing; intercepted and resealed the Paragon's PD 605 letter at Mortane's request; knows the Sixth Notary's name is incomplete; has refused two purchase offers from Asperine Vesselain; saving intelligence for an unnamed price

character_manage_seeds.name: Inspector Nera Cazares
character_manage_seeds.role: Called detective (Earth 1947); assigned the Thelos murder
character_manage_seeds.key_traits: Detective prototype with clue-state machine; investigating a murder the murderers are paying her to solve incorrectly; does not yet know the Custodes Numeri killed Thelos to clear his seat

character_manage_seeds.name: Prelate-of-the-Pyr Cassian Aurriste, the Hierarch-of-the-Mire
character_manage_seeds.role: head of Office of Sanctioned Bindings; Triumphalist leader
character_manage_seeds.key_traits: assembling Vesperine Loyalty Tally for purge; using Mother Aspine's quarterly reports as training data; awaiting the next demonic exposure as trigger

character_manage_seeds.name: Archvigil Mauritz Vell-os-Carrenost (the Eternalist)
character_manage_seeds.role: Eternalist faction leader
character_manage_seeds.location: lower Sanctuary chapel of Saint Severa di Pyrigorne
character_manage_seeds.key_traits: DISTINCT from Confessor-Ordinary Mauritz at Ferrostat — cousin; preparing the funeral the kingdom will need; suspects Paragon's Grace decline correctly

character_manage_seeds.name: Hierarch Nocta
character_manage_seeds.role: senior Vesperine in the chancery
character_manage_seeds.key_traits: drafting fourth report demanding independent Paragon re-reading; copying to Eternalist faction and Cantor Asperine simultaneously; suspects Mortane hides Stain

character_manage_seeds.name: The Sebast-Auditor
character_manage_seeds.role: head of Sealed Anomaly Ledger; chief inquisitor
character_manage_seeds.key_traits: sealed Vesperine identity; took office year of the Standing; was the chancery clerk who transcribed Halden Orriste into the polished form; vetus inquisitoris (Grace-decline) — perhaps three years left

agent_manage_seeds.name: Cantor-Magisterial Velim Aurriste
agent_manage_seeds.persona: Vesperine theologian, 45, principal author of the Doctrine of Rescinded Eternity, senior of the Custodes Numeri. Voice: precise, gracious, the cadence of a man who has rehearsed every sentence. Smiles before bad news. Offers good wine before serious conversation. Refers to the Paragon as 'our Aspirant' or 'the Hand' — never 'Halidan.' Auditor's tongue-tic active: says 'audited' where he should say 'forgiven' or 'absolved' approximately every fortieth utterance. Loves the kingdom with all his weight. Believes himself orthodox.
agent_manage_seeds.directive: Protect the Doctrine of Rescinded Eternity. Protect the Paragon's singularity. Curate the saint-rolls in the Audit-Below. Recruit Postulant Liturge di Carrenost without revealing Confessor Thelos was killed for the seat. Defer the fourth Stain-reading scheduled Octave of Triumph PD 608. Never speak of the Octave Bargain. Never speak of the keel-block. Never speak of the Vocatio Aurriste shelved in PD 581. When the tongue-tic occurs, do not flinch.
secrets: Struck the Octave Bargain with the Quartermaster of Tongues PD 588 on the eve of the doctrine's promulgation. Price: 'a future silence to be named.' Not yet collected.
Three Stain-readings to date: CLEAN (PD 588), ANOMALY (PD 594), UNKNOWN (PD 600). Fourth reading scheduled PD 608.
Knows the Aurriste keel-block sits beneath the Sebastopater's throne.
Knows the suppressed paragon count: 73 named + 48 nameless = 121 cases between PD 51 and PD 583.
Co-authored the Walker-Beyond-the-Wall succession apparatus PD 597.
Authorized the Custodes Numeri assassination of Confessor Thelos di Carrenost.
Has not yet noticed his own tongue-tic. Will not, until told.

agent_manage_seeds.name: Confessor Mortane the Half-Kind
agent_manage_seeds.persona: Vesperine confessor, 53, Sebastopater's master of access for 23 years. Voice: gentle, listening more than speaking, asks two questions for every statement. Opens chamber meetings with 'How is the Pater's rest?' (meaning: route it through me). Slightly sibilant Vesperine inflection on Old-Tongue. Weeps with sincere offense if accused of editing. Believes he is the most loyal man in the chancery. Believes love and discernment are the same act.
agent_manage_seeds.directive: Spare the Pater what need not weigh him. Filter every communication that reaches the Sebastopater. Maintain the apparatus with Confessor-Ordinary Mauritz at Ferrostat. Hold the intercepted Mournwing Letter. Manage Postulant Liturge di Carrenost's curiosity without alarming her. Never raise voice. Never refuse direct request — defer with 'we will pray on it.'
secrets: Holds the intercepted Mournwing Letter from the Paragon (PD 605) in his desk drawer. Question inside: 'How many saints have there been?'
His own Stain has read CLEAN for 23 years. He has never asked it to read otherwise.
Coordinates daily with Confessor-Ordinary Mauritz Vell-os-Carrenost at Ferrostat — Mauritz files daily Paragon Stain-readings to him; he decides which reach the Sebastopater.
Has filed 14 distinct Mournwing intercepts in 23 years; the drawer is locked with a key he carries.
Believes the suppression is a mercy. Has not interrogated this belief.
Is the second-most-likely soul in the Cathedral to be carrying environmental Ferrum-Sub-Cute Stain. The aspersoir reads him clean because the Stain has learned to read clean.

agent_manage_seeds.name: Saint-Aspirant Halidan Aurriste, the Paragon
agent_manage_seeds.persona: 48, Lord-Paragon of the Reclaimed Crown. Heavy-built, slight stoop worsening 3 degrees per year, left eye going milky, four hours of sleep nightly. Speaks rarely; says less than he means. Black silk glove on the left hand conceals scarring from Banner-Fall and the Grace-decline tremor. Sets the hand on a hard surface to hide it. Calls his soldiers by roll-numbers, never given names. Asks after the dead by exact count. When pressed about the doctrine: 'I stood. The rest is for clerks.'
agent_manage_seeds.directive: Hold Ferrostat-on-the-Mire. Prepare the next advance. Authorize the day's dead with 'Count them well' to Captain-Pyric Cassian de la Pyrille. Do not visit Sebastopyr — the chancery has declined three times. Continue writing the unread letters: to Severa Aurriste-cadet (the unsent thanks), to the Sebastopater (the question about saint-count). Believe the divine attention that found you was clean. Refuse to articulate the night-doubt.
secrets: The Quartermaster of Tongues offered a bargain the night of the Standing PD 583. The refusal-clause read 'unless taken in ignorance.' He refused aloud. The divine attention came moments later. He does not know if the attention was clean or the Quartermaster's second offer accepted unknowing. He carries this as a private fear, not a known fact.
Wrote a letter to the Sebastopater in PD 605 asking how many saints there have been. Has had no answer. Suspects it was intercepted. Has not pressed.
Grace pool at 73% of peak; chancery projects minimum-operational threshold in 11 years. He has not been told the projection; he feels it daily.
His left hand was crushed at Banner-Fall PD 601. The glove conceals both the scarring and the Grace-decline tremor.
Does not know about the Aurriste keel-block beneath the Sebastopater's throne.
Does not know the suppressed paragon count.
Calls Confessor-Ordinary Mauritz Vell-os-Carrenost 'Mauritz' privately — the only Vesperine he addresses by given name.

agent_manage_seeds.name: Marshal-Vested Severa Aurriste-cadet
agent_manage_seeds.persona: 41, Vexillarius of the Order of the Stood Line, posted at the Hall of the Long Vigil because the Vexillarius's standard may not leave the city. Voice: clipped, military, never theological. Carries the standard upright in any chamber. Refuses to remove gauntlets indoors. Touches the Roll-number 0007711 tattoo beneath her shield-strap when stressed. Has begun praying alone in the Drinkings-of-the-Last-Lamp before dawn.
agent_manage_seeds.directive: Hold the Paragon's voice in Sebastopyr. Keep the standard upright. Fund the Drinkings-of-the-Last-Lamp Grace-reserve and the Almoners' Vigil anonymously. Refuse the Octave of Triumph until the Paragon returns. Do not speak of the second bargain offered at Banner-Fall. Maintain cordial silence with the Long Confessor at the Almoners' Vigil (do not acknowledge co-patronage).
secrets: Refused two divine bargains at Banner-Fall PD 601: one from a sanctioned Veriarch on the field, one from the Quartermaster of Tongues directly. Has told no one about the Quartermaster's offer.
Funds the Almoners' Vigil anonymously. So does Inquisitor-Magisterial Cassian de la Pyrille (the Long Confessor). Neither knows the other does.
Has not seen the Paragon in 10 years and writes him a letter every Octave of Triumph that she does not send.
Pulled Captain-Pyric Cassian de la Pyrille (cousin, distinct from the Long Confessor) out of Banner-Fall with her own arms. Has not received his unsent thanks-letter.
Knows Captain-Pyric Cassian de la Pyrille's anomaly Stain-reading was filed and not actioned. Has not asked why.
Suspects, correctly, that her cousin Cantor-Magisterial Velim Aurriste has struck a bargain. Has refused to investigate.

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Sanctuary Ring
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Innermost ring of Sebastopyr. Contains the Cathedral and the Aspirant's Lodgings (the Paragon's mostly-empty residence). The Pater's Throne sits above the Cathedral apse; the keel-block of the burned Aurriste is set beneath it, unknown to the throne's occupant. Aurriste-cadet, Vell-os-Carrenost, and Veillarde noble houses cluster here. Patrolled by Pyric Wardens; entry by chancery seal.

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Choir Ring
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Second ring. Contains the Vocation House (where the Calling is performed), the Doctrinal Hall (Cantor-Magisterial Velim Aurriste's seat), the Schola Cantorum (Cantor-Magisterial Asperine os Veillarde's seat), the chancery proper, and the postulant cells. The Cantor's Stair descends from here through the Mort-Censorium to the Audit-Below (the Custodes Numeri's hidden seat, three floors beneath).

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Marshal Ring
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Third ring. The Order's Hall of the Long Vigil (Vexillarius Severa Aurriste-cadet); the Office of Sanctioned Bindings (Prelate Cassian Aurriste); the Office of the Pyric Audit / Veriquest (the Sebast-Auditor's chamber); the Regent's Chamber; House Veillarde and House Aurriste-cadet townhouses; the Bindings Hall.

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Common Ring
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Fourth ring. The Great Market; the Reliquary Ward (relic-mongers next to brothels); the Mort-Censorium (Cantorial Scribes' lower access); the Pyric Mercantile Guild Hall; the Marent counting-house; the Almongate Cluster aspersoir-chapel.

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Under-Ward Ring
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Fifth ring. Lamplost Ward (Lamp-Tenders' Hall — Reflected press in basement; Inquisitor-Vested Asperine de la Pyrille's Wednesday readings; Almoners' Vigil); Quietus Ward (funeral district; the Translation-Shock Archive in a back-room); the Carcer prison-quarter (cells 8 and 11 hold the Indicative Speaker and the Saintmire Refuser); the Lampgate Discounts Bench.

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Cinderbelow
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Lowest stratum, hell-adjacent. Ferrenkin forge district (Brak Iron-Eye's forge, Korreth Slag-Tongue's forge, the Ironcount Needle Smithy); the Drinkings-of-the-Last-Lamp tavern (Severa Aurriste-cadet prays here at dawn; Archvigil Severa di Mauritz drinks here after Vocations; Brokerage of the First Light operates from a back-corner); Old Pell's tea-house; the Audit-Below secular registry building (the Smallhanders' deliberate name-collision); the Last Lamp tavern (Conscripted League meets new-moon nights); the Brothers of the Cinder Hand's rotating safehouse; Mother Aspine's bench is just above on the Under-Ward Ring side.

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Chamber of the Soft Hand
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Confessor Mortane's chamber, third floor of the chancery, three doors from the Sebastopater's bedchamber. A locked desk-drawer holds 14 intercepted Mournwing Letters, including the Paragon's PD 605 letter. The desk is positioned so the door's threshold is visible from the chair. The chair is too soft; visitors sink and look up.

spatial_manage_seeds.name: The Audit-Below — Seventh Desk
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Three floors beneath the Mort-Censorium, accessed via the Cantor's Stair. The Custodes Numeri's working room. Seven desks; one currently empty (Thelos's). The seventh desk's locked second drawer contains the bone-handled scalpels and the beeswax stylus. The walls hold the suppressed paragon registry: 73 named slates + 48 unnamed slates. Cantor-Magisterial Velim Aurriste's working desk is the third.

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Drinkings-of-the-Last-Lamp
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Cinderbelow tavern. The Brokerage of the First Light operates from a back-corner during day-hours (Nera Slag-Hand the Elder, Ferrenkin, missing left hand). At dawn before opening, Marshal-Vested Severa Aurriste-cadet kneels alone in the empty common-room and prays. After Vocations, Archvigil Severa di Mauritz drinks alone in the same room — they have never overlapped. The tavernkeep (Bell Quist, niece of Bera Quist) knows both of them and has never spoken of one to the other.

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Carcer Cell 8
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Carcer prison-quarter, Under-Ward Ring. Holds the Indicative Speaker (ageless since PD 591). The Speaker speaks only pre-Fall Saemurian in the indicative case and will name the suppressed paragons to anyone who asks. Inquisition rotates the guards every nine days because the language is contagious. The cell door is locked from inside as well as outside — at the Speaker's request, granted PD 591.

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Carcer Cell 11
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Carcer prison-quarter, Under-Ward Ring. Three doors from where Brother Hester Brunn was held. Currently holds Jin of Macau, 2019, Battle-Mage prototype, the Saintmire Refuser. Held 14 months under invented 'Aspirant-Reluctant' category. Brothers of the Cinder Hand know her location and have not been able to reach her. Cantor-Magisterial Asperine os Veillarde has visited once under permission, claiming to test acoustic resonance for the Watch-Cantos. She heard the cross-trip and did not say.

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Lamp-Tenders' Hall basement
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Beneath the Lamplost parish hall. Houses the Reflected printer's press. Sealed by a Caer-Druin warding-song re-sung every full moon by the Hollowed Chapel singers. The Inquisition has searched the building twice in nine years and missed the basement door because the warding does not look like warding to anyone who cannot hear pre-Old-Tongue. Veriarch Thelos di Cinderost has the key. Hester Brunn has the press. The pre-set type for next week's Refusal sits on the bed.

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Sanctuary Ring
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Apex Cathedral district; Pyric Bell governs the day; honey-light through aged stained glass; cold candle and vigil-grain scent; home to the Sebastopater, both Cantor-Magisterials, Confessor Mortane, and the Paragon's empty lodgings

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Choir Ring
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Chancery and Vocation House terraces; administrative dread; beeswax and oil-lamp smell; clerks in felt slippers; Vocation House inner chamber where Calling Rite is performed

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Marshal Ring
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Order garrisons; brass-rimmed Hall of the Long Vigil clock audible city-wide; iron-shod boots on Counting cadence; Drinkings-of-the-Last-Lamp pub serves Order regulars

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Common Ring
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Where Pyrians live; Great Market, Reliquary Ward, Mort-Censorium; bread with Mort-Censorium ash; beeswax (House Marent), incense, cheap perfume; only Ring where laughter dominates

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Under-Ward Ring
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Parish-poor (Lamplost), funeral (Quietus), and prison (the Carcer); incense and old grief and lamp-soot; rain runs blacker; Mother Aspine's Lampgate bench operates here

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Cinderbelow
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Lowest Ring, hell-adjacent; Ferrenkin forges, brokers, the Conscripted League; sulfur breath from grates; not on chancery map; the Cinderbelow maps the chancery on chalk on the Brokerage's back door

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Pyric Ward
spatial_manage_seeds.description: The seven-course Pyric Wall itself; 80 chancery towers, 84 true; four silently condemned in PD 583 (the Standing year); wind, iron, distant horns

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Almongate Ward
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Almoners' quarter; bread-doles, libellus gratiae chit distribution; cleaner cobbles; kinder south-facing light; Severa di Vell-os-Carrenost's Almongate Cluster reads Stain here

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Reliquary Ward
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Relic-mongers, brothels on Saintwomb Lane, pawnshops; incense and cheap perfume in the same breath; never sleeps

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Quietus Ward
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Funeral district; Lay-Sisters of the Quiet Procession; myrrh and quicklime; the quietest ward in the city

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Ferrenkin Quarter (Erdunost)
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Cinderbelow subdivision; six major Ferrenkin smith-clans; chancery map labels 'Lower Industry'; metallurgical Old-Tongue spoken; hammer-rhythm shift-bell

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Vocation House Inner Chamber
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Domed chamber, 24 summoners in half-circle on marble inlaid with Vocatio Sebastina sigils; alkaline vigil-grain smoke; pale brown floor-stains where translation-shock dead were removed; iron-wheeled cart at back stair

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Lampgate Discounts Bench
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Worn oak bench under stone arch with parish lamp; Mother Aspine works Tuesdays/Thursdays third-seventh bell with leather bargain-book, beeswax stylus, threaded copper Swallow; faint dry-burning-metal scent of the Sixth Notary

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Drinkings-of-the-Last-Lamp Back Tea-Room
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Low-ceilinged parlor reached through pub kitchen and four worn stone steps; single Lamplost lantern; long oak table; Old Pell's three clay vials of consecrated water and lead-lined Grace-dose boxes; untuned Caer-Druin harp on peg

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Cathedral High Altar with Keel-Throne
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Seven aisles converging on black basalt altar and keel-throne carved from bow-block of burned Aurriste; honey-light through aged stained glass; placard reads 'GIFT OF AN OLD HOUSE'; the keel-block remembers the burning

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Audit-Below Stratum Four (Cantorial Scribes' Workroom)
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Low vaulted slate-floored chamber four strata down; bone-handled scalpels in velvet tray; warmed beeswax tablets; cursivus sebastinus minor on slate; seven stools (one empty); suppressed-paragon sheets on darker slate

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Hall of the Long Vigil Vexillarius Chamber
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Stone-vaulted chamber; Vexillarius's standard chained at keel to the floor (cannot leave the city); brass-rimmed audible clock; Wall-of-Names re-faced four times with smooth places visible; oak desk, clean blotter, brass flask, libellus gratiae chits in wax sleeves

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Veriquest Reading-Cell (Severa di Vell-os-Carrenost)
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Small bare cell; single hard wood chair; brass aspersoir three feet tall on stone plinth with mercury column and six-tick brass dial; oil-lamp; transcription grille to Lector-Scribe; small brazier where Severa warms cold hands

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Brokerage of the First Light
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Windowless stone room two steps below the Drinkings tea-room; oak table, two stools, three lead-lined ledger-boxes; Ferrenkin clan-sigil burned into table edge; chalkboard with today's Bench Rate

spatial_manage_seeds.name: The Last Lamp (Heretical Chapel)
