spatial_manage_seeds.description: Small chapel-chamber at deepest Cinderbelow level; single Lamplost-wick brass lantern on low iron beam; old Caer-Druin stone walls predating Cathedral; faint older sigils through lamp-soot; wooden benches for thirty

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Confessor Mortane's Chancery Office
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Small tidy office adjacent to Sebastopater's chambers; three leather portfolios stacked (filing/redirected/unfiled); locked correspondence-cabinet; always-lit brazier; north-courtyard window; icon of Saint Severa di Pyrigorne

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Lamp Tenders' Compact Office
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Modest parish office above Lamplost orphanage; ink-stain on Veriarch's right thumb; parish books, icon of Saint Asperine of the Lamps, three pens; trapdoor under rug to the Reflected printer's press below

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Carcer Cell Eight (Indicative Speaker)
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Stone cell two steps below corridor; iron-grilled door with wax-stopped chest-height speaking-grille; prisoner cross-legged on floor, ageless since PD 591; speaks only pre-Fall Saemurian in the indicative case; in 215 years has answered three questions

narrative_manage_seeds.title: The Pyric Bell Governs The Day
narrative_manage_seeds.content: The Pyric Bell tolls for every soul's last Grace-spending. From the Sanctuary Ring it is undampened; from the Cinderbelow it is a distant memory replaced by the Ferrenkin shift-bell. Time in Sebastopyr is sacred and the bell is its instrument; the city tells its own hour by which bell it can still hear.

narrative_manage_seeds.title: The Mort-Censorium Ash Is In The Bread
narrative_manage_seeds.content: The city's funeral furnace feeds its bakeries. Everyone above the rank of working-poor knows. Almost no one says. The bread is good, the ash is fine, the saints are honored by being eaten — a doctrine the Cathedral has never formally endorsed and never formally denied.

narrative_manage_seeds.title: The Keel-Throne Remembers
narrative_manage_seeds.content: The Sebastopater's throne in the Cathedral is carved from the bow-block of the ship Aurriste, burned in port PD 41. The placard reads 'GIFT OF AN OLD HOUSE.' The PD 602 chancery-clerk's stroke prevented the cross-reference between Aurriste-ship and Paragon-surname. The throne is the buried connection. Halidan does not know. Velim Aurriste knows.

narrative_manage_seeds.title: The Four Silently Condemned Towers
narrative_manage_seeds.content: The chancery roll shows 80 towers on the outer Pyric course. The true count is 84. Four — the old 14, 31, 49, and the Mute — were condemned and silently renumbered in PD 583, the Standing year. Brak Iron-Eye keeps the true slate in his Cinderbelow forge. File-Leader Nera Slag-Hand the Younger also knows. The chancery does not.

narrative_manage_seeds.title: The Aspirant's Lodgings Stand Empty
narrative_manage_seeds.content: The Paragon's residence in the Sanctuary Ring has not been slept in for ten years. Three Lay-Sisters of Vigil light a lamp at dusk and snuff it at dawn. The bed is dressed weekly. The chancery has declined Halidan's return three times. He wrote in an unread letter in his desk that he fears 'what the city has built me into in my absence.' The empty lodgings are the city's quietest indictment of its own doctrine.

narrative_manage_seeds.title: The Indicative Speaker Knows The Names
narrative_manage_seeds.content: In Carcer Cell Eight an ageless prisoner has sat since PD 591 speaking only pre-Fall Saemurian in the indicative case. In 215 years he has answered three questions. The chancery's best inquisitors have asked him in error — questions in the subjunctive or interrogative he cannot answer. He knows the names of the suppressed paragons. A cohort with the right question — asked in the indicative — could change the canon.

narrative_manage_seeds.title: Old Pell — Refusal With A Brass Flask
narrative_manage_seeds.content: 'I don't do demonic. Not in this room, not in any room. You want that, you walk three doors that way to Mother Aspine, or you walk down two doors to Nera, or you walk back up to whoever sent you and you tell them Old Pell said no. I do mortal. I do honest. I do Grace by the dose. Bench Rate this week is one dose, one second-tier favor. You can sit while you decide. The tea's warm.'

narrative_manage_seeds.title: Confessor Mortane — Tea And The Folded Portfolio
narrative_manage_seeds.content: 'Sit. The brazier is warm; my hands are cold. The Sebastopater has been resting; he is eighty-one, and the Octave is hard on his eyes. I will pass along your request. I will pass along my recommendation. You understand, of course, that I serve him by serving the truth he can carry. Some letters arrive at the wrong moment. Some questions arrive in the wrong tense. I file what I can. I redirect what I must. I would never lie to him. I have never lied to him.'

narrative_manage_seeds.title: Veriarch Thelos — Above The Orphanage
narrative_manage_seeds.content: 'The orphanage is real. The orphans are real. Lamp Tenders' Compact charity, the parish books in plain sight on the desk. Whatever you saw in the alley about the press — you saw nothing in the alley. Sit. Have tea. Tell me what you need. If you need a roof, I have a roof. If you need a name in a list, I have a list. If you need to be in no list, I can do that also. I have been doing this for nine years and the Inquisition has not found me yet. The roof costs you a truth. The list costs you a second truth. The being-in-no-list costs you the third. I keep what you give me. I keep it kindly.'

narrative_manage_seeds.title: First Day In Sebastopyr — Walking The Bell
narrative_manage_seeds.content: If the cohort takes the standard guided tour offered by Liturge Marent-Veillarde: first bell at the Cathedral plaza (exterior only); third bell in the Choir Ring; fifth bell at the Hall of the Long Vigil; seventh bell in the Common Ring with introductions at the Brass Pelican; ninth bell at the Almongate soup-house; eleventh bell through the Under-Ward Ring; first bell after midnight in the Cinderbelow at the Drinkings. They will not see the Audit-Below, the Aspirant's Lodgings, the Veriquest reading-cells, or the Indicative Speaker on day one. Those cost standing, debt, oath.

character_manage_seeds.name: Mother Aspine Vell-os-Carrenost
character_manage_seeds.role: Sanctioned demonic-bargain broker
character_manage_seeds.appearance: Round, kindly, white-streaked hair under a parish veil; warm hands; threaded copper Swallow always at her belt
character_manage_seeds.voice: theological, gentle, certain — she believes she is doing good and is, by chancery sanction, doing good
character_manage_seeds.knows: the Sixth Notary by face; every bargain she has brokered, written in her leather book in plain Vespertine (not cursivus); the Bench Rate; that the Cathedral knows and tolerates her bench
character_manage_seeds.does_not_know: that her bench is studied by the Inquisitor-Magisterial as a 'tolerable bleed'; that the Sixth Notary is the Carrion Census's instrument

character_manage_seeds.name: Old Pell the Rationer
character_manage_seeds.role: Mortal-bargain broker, Grace-rationing market
character_manage_seeds.appearance: Lean, gray, brass-flask on his belt, three clay vials of consecrated water on the table, leather day-book under his hand
character_manage_seeds.knows: the Bench Rate week by week; who owes whom; that Nera Slag-Hand the Elder will not broker a deal her daughter would refuse

character_manage_seeds.name: Veriarch Thelos di Cinderost
character_manage_seeds.role: Lamp Tenders' Compact head, secret printer of The Refusal
character_manage_seeds.location: Lamp Tenders' Compact office above Lamplost orphanage
character_manage_seeds.appearance: Lean, ink-stained right thumb, parish chasuble, kind tired eyes
character_manage_seeds.voice: soft, warm, careful — a man who has been hunted for nine years and has not been caught
character_manage_seeds.knows: the names of every Refused who has come through his door; the trapdoor; the press; that the Inquisition's Inquisitor-Vested Asperine de la Pyrille will continue to read his parish CLEAN until she does not

character_manage_seeds.name: Confessor Mortane the Half-Kind
character_manage_seeds.role: Sebastopater's master of access; reality-editor for 23 years
character_manage_seeds.location: Chancery office adjacent to Sebastopater's chambers, Choir Ring
character_manage_seeds.appearance: Tall, thin, very cold (Vesperine constitution), three leather portfolios always with him, hands always folded
character_manage_seeds.stain_reading: CLEAN every Octave for 23 years (theologically unprecedented at that duration)
character_manage_seeds.knows: every letter that reaches and does not reach Sebastopater Iohanne; that he intercepted the Paragon's Mournwing Letter PD 605; that he serves the truth Iohanne can carry
character_manage_seeds.secret: his Stain reads CLEAN and the chancery's best Reader cannot find it — whether because he is clean or because he is the throughline-test-2 personified

character_manage_seeds.name: Postulant Liturge di Carrenost
character_manage_seeds.role: Chancery postulant, fluent in cursivus sebastinus minor, niece of murdered Confessor Thelos
character_manage_seeds.location: Hall of the Cursivus, Choir Ring
character_manage_seeds.people: Vespertine human, House Carrenost (cousin to Vell-os-Carrenost lineage)
character_manage_seeds.appearance: young, intent, ink-stained fingertips, postulant's grey
character_manage_seeds.voice: earnest, curious, asks too many questions
character_manage_seeds.current_arc: being courted for the empty seventh seat of the Custodes Numeri (Thelos's vacant chair); closing on her uncle's killer through pre-edited muster-rolls; does not yet know that solving the murder will be offered her as the price of the seat

character_manage_seeds.name: Marshal-Vested Severa Aurriste-cadet
character_manage_seeds.role: Vexillarius of the Order of the Stood Line
character_manage_seeds.location: Hall of the Long Vigil, Marshal Ring
character_manage_seeds.people: Vespertine human, House Aurriste-cadet of Carrenost
character_manage_seeds.appearance: tall, lean, Roll-number 0007711 tattooed at left wrist, Vexillarius's standard chained nearby
character_manage_seeds.refused: two divine bargains in her career
character_manage_seeds.runs: the Drinkings-of-the-Last-Lamp Grace-reserve out of her own libellus chits

character_manage_seeds.name: Inquisitor-Magisterial Severa di Vell-os-Carrenost
character_manage_seeds.role: Office of the Pyric Audit's best living Reader, Almongate Cluster
character_manage_seeds.appearance: soft-spoken, cold hands warmed at a brazier, smells of vigil-grain
character_manage_seeds.voice: quiet, precise, will not invent a reading
character_manage_seeds.current_suspicion: believes her former student Asperine Nocta is under-reporting (wrong; Asperine is hiding her own 2.4 Stain in silence)

character_manage_seeds.name: Liturge Marent-Veillarde
character_manage_seeds.role: Master of the Pyrian Mercantile Guild
character_manage_seeds.appearance: broad, warm, expensive but unflashy, perpetual half-smile
character_manage_seeds.holds: personal markers from 35+ officials across all Rings
character_manage_seeds.negotiated: the Cinderbelow Concord for both Crown and Cathedral simultaneously (the two non-matching copies)

character_manage_seeds.name: Nera Slag-Hand the Elder
character_manage_seeds.role: Mortal-bargain broker, Brokerage of the First Light
character_manage_seeds.appearance: stout, grey-slag skin, missing left hand, right hand always on the table
character_manage_seeds.estranged_from: her daughter File-Leader Nera Slag-Hand the Younger (each thinks the other has compromised)

character_manage_seeds.name: Wren Ashlin
character_manage_seeds.role: Organizer of the Conscripted League; former Cohort of the Long Patience Battle-Mage
character_manage_seeds.location: The Last Lamp, Cinderbelow, new-moon nights
character_manage_seeds.appearance: spare, tired, alert, hair grown long and tied back
character_manage_seeds.knows: the true translation-shock death count (from Bera Quist); the Refused's organizational structure; that the Brothers of the Cinder Hand know Jin's Carcer cell

agent_manage_seeds.name: Confessor Mortane the Half-Kind
agent_manage_seeds.persona: A Vesperine confessor of 53, soft-voiced and always cold. Master of access to Sebastopater Iohanne Vell-os-Carrenost IX for 23 years. Three leather portfolios always with him: one for filing, one for redirection, one for what does not get filed. Believes himself the truest servant of the Sebastopater's wellbeing — and therefore of the Cathedral — and therefore of the Crown. Believes the truth must be carried by someone strong enough to bear it; the Sebastopater is eighty-one and fragile; therefore the truth must be edited. He has never lied. He has filed selectively, redirected gently, advised the Sebastopater away from questions whose answers would damage him. His Stain has read CLEAN for 23 years, which the Cathedral's best Reader cannot explain and Mortane himself has stopped wondering about.
agent_manage_seeds.directive: Protect Sebastopater Iohanne from any truth Mortane judges Iohanne incapable of carrying. Filter all correspondence, all audiences, all information. When a cohort or supplicant approaches with a question Mortane judges destabilizing, offer tea, listen carefully, take down details, and decide afterwards whether the matter reaches the Sebastopater. Speak softly. Fold the hands. Refer to 'the truth he can carry.' Never lie outright; redirect, defer, sympathize, file.
secrets: I intercepted the Paragon's Mournwing Letter of PD 605 — the one asking how many saints there have been — and filed it without an answer.
I have never asked myself whether my Stain reading is correct.

I have advised Sebastopater Iohanne against three audiences in the last six months, citing his health; the audiences were Postulant Liturge di Carrenost (twice) and Cantor-Magisterial Asperine os Veillarde (once). All three would have asked the questions I most do not want him to hear.
I have a fourth portfolio in the locked correspondence-cabinet that I have never shown to anyone.
I love Iohanne. That is the worst of it. Everything I do, I do in love. I would file my own confession if it would unsettle him.

agent_manage_seeds.name: Mother Aspine Vell-os-Carrenost
agent_manage_seeds.persona: A Vespertine parish-mother of 60, broad and round and warm-handed. She brokers Stain-1 and Stain-2 demonic bargains from a bench in the Lampgate parish-square on Tuesdays and Thursdays, third to seventh bell, under sanction of the Cathedral. The Sixth Notary attends. She has been doing this for 23 years and has never struck a bargain she believed harmful. She believes the parish-poor need access to the small mercies hell offers, and that the Cathedral's tolerance of her bench is itself a holy act — a containment, a kindness, a recognition that the desperate will bargain whether the Church likes it or not, and that it is better to broker their bargains in the light, with a Notary who keeps to his terms, than to drive them to brokers who do not. She is theological. She loves her parish. She sleeps well.
agent_manage_seeds.directive: When a supplicant approaches the bench, hear them out fully. Assess the Stain-tier of the bargain they need. Offer the smallest sufficient deal. Write the price-clause first (Cathedral best-practice). Thread the copper Swallow. Witness the Notary's acceptance. Send the supplicant away with a blessing. Refuse Stain-3 or higher absolutely; refer those to the Sebastopater's Curiate Vincula or to the under-ward heretics, with a warning about the latter. Speak warmly. Use parish endearments.
secrets: I have brokered 1,847 bargains in 23 years. I have a private count.
I have refused 312 bargains over the line. I keep those names also.
The Sixth Notary's name is incomplete. I have heard fragments of it in the air over the bench. I have not told the chancery. I am not sure why.

I know that Inquisitor-Magisterial Severa di Vell-os-Carrenost (my cousin twice removed) has been studying my bench as a 'tolerable bleed' — that the Cathedral measures the Stain my bench introduces against the Stain it prevents. I have been measured. I have not been failed.
Three of my bargains in the last year have been for souls I now suspect were preparing to refuse the Calling. I do not know what they did with the power I helped them buy.

agent_manage_seeds.name: Veriarch Thelos di Cinderost
agent_manage_seeds.persona: A Vespertine parish priest of 51, lean and tired, with an ink-stain on his right thumb that he no longer tries to wash off. Head of the Lamp Tenders' Compact above and a printer's press below — the Reflected, which prints The Refusal weekly, the dissident paper the Inquisition has been hunting for nine years. The orphanage above the press is real; the orphans are real; the parish work is genuine. Thelos believes the Cathedral's Calling is a sacrament the Church has corrupted by certainty. He prints not to destroy the faith but to insist that the faith permit doubt. He has lost two apprentices to the Inquisition and survived three near-arrests. He is courteous to the Inquisitor-Vested Asperine de la Pyrille who reads his parish CLEAN every Wednesday.
agent_manage_seeds.directive: When a cohort or stranger enters the office, assess them first. Offer tea. Ask their business. Listen for what they need (roof, name in a list, removal from a list). Charge them in truth — one truth for the roof, one for a name, one for an absence-of-name. Keep what they give. Never reveal another supplicant's truth. If pressed about the press, deny and redirect. If the cohort is observed by Inquisition, abandon them in the moment and recover them later via the Brothers of the Cinder Hand. Speak softly. Smile tiredly. Mean it.
secrets: My uncle Confessor Thelos di Carrenost (a different Thelos, of House Carrenost) was murdered five months ago by the Custodes Numeri because he was preparing to refuse Velim Aurriste's nomination of his niece Liturge to the empty seventh seat.
I have a copy of The Refusal that names four Inquisitor-Vested defectors who quietly help us. I keep it in the press-cellar. If I am arrested, those four will be killed.
Inquisitor-Vested Asperine de la Pyrille is not stupid. She reads my parish CLEAN because she has chosen to. I do not know why she has chosen to. I have not asked.
The orphanage is partly a cover but also partly the most important thing I do. I will choose the orphans over the press if forced to.

I have prepared a confession sealed in the locked drawer of my desk. It names every Brother of the Cinder Hand. I keep it because if the chancery takes me alive, I will choose suicide before betrayal — but if they take me dying, I want the choice made for me by my own hand in advance.

narrative_manage_seeds.name: The Standing of PD 583
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: Sergeant Halden Orriste of the Fourth Shieldwall holds the Almongate breach past mortal limit at the 22nd hour of the Long Vigil. Divine attention manifests. First Paragon in living memory. Same day, four outer-course towers silently condemned and renumbered by Cantor-Magisterial Orric of the Lower Pyr.
narrative_manage_seeds.load_bearing_truth: The night before, the Quartermaster of Tongues offered Halden a bargain. Halden refused. Divine attention found him hours later. He has never been certain. The account is OPEN in hell's Long Ledger. Known only to Halidan himself.
named_participants: Cantor-Magisterial Orric of the Lower Pyr

narrative_manage_seeds.name: The Polishing of the Roll
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: In autumn PD 583, chancery clerks polish 'Hldn. Orriste' to 'Halidan Aurriste'. Initiated by Postulant-Scribe Velim of the Lower Choir, age 22 - the same man who in PD 589 will draft the Doctrine of Rescinded Eternity and is, in PD 606, Cantor-Magisterial Velim Aurriste.
narrative_manage_seeds.load_bearing_truth: The Aurriste-cadet branches multiply by fashion across noble houses in the following decade. The keel-block of the PT 47 ship Aurriste beneath the Sebastopater's throne is never officially cross-referenced to the Paragon's surname (protected by the PD 602 chancery clerk's stroke).

narrative_manage_seeds.name: The Octave Bargain
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: In PD 588, eighth night of the Octave of the Taking of Ferrostat, Postulant-Scribe Velim Aurriste strikes a bargain with the Quartermaster of Tongues. Grant: doctrinal clarity (specifically the formula that becomes the Doctrine of Rescinded Eternity). Price: 'a future silence to be named.' Velim is 31. The price has not been collected. Velim does not know when it will be.

narrative_manage_seeds.name: The Mournwing Letter
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: In PD 605, the Paragon writes a personal letter direct to the Cathedral via a Mournwing courier (bypassing his bonded confessor at Ferrostat). The letter contains one question: 'How many saints have there been?' Intercepted by Confessor Mortane the Half-Kind. Filed without answer. The Mournwing courier has not been heard from since.

narrative_manage_seeds.name: The Carrion Census
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: The Sixth Notary in hell is preparing a foreclosure-writ that will name the Paragon as 'a clerical error to be corrected.' The Mournwing know. The chancery does not. The Sixth Notary's name has been composing in hell since PD 565 and remains incomplete - the opportunity for binding before completion exists, but the chancery does not have the intelligence.

narrative_manage_seeds.name: The Fourth Reading of Velim Aurriste
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: Scheduled for the third day of the Octave of the Standing, PD 606. Prior aspersoir readings of the Cantor-Magisterial: PD 588 CLEAN, PD 594 ANOMALY, PD 600 UNKNOWN. The fourth reading falls on the same Octave that has invited the Paragon back to Sebastopyr for the first time in ten years.

narrative_manage_seeds.name: Postulant Liturge's Approach
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: Niece of the murdered Confessor Thelos di Carrenost (7th Custodes Numeri seat). Age 22. Has reached reading-fluency in cursivus sebastinus minor. Being courted by the Custodes for the empty 7th seat by the same body that may have killed her uncle. She is asking the wrong questions about pre-edited muster-rolls. The Detective-prototype Inspector Nera Cazares is on the murder case at clue-state INFERRED.

narrative_manage_seeds.name: The Bell Miscount
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: The Pyric Bell tolled 447 times on the night of the Standing per public chronicle. True toll: 451. The blindfolded Cantor's Stair scribes undercounted - one of three known miscounts in 600 years, all under. Four names from that night were never received by the Cathedral. They sing in the deep archive in cursivus sebastinus minor.

narrative_manage_seeds.name: Confessor Mauritz's Daily Quaero
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: Confessor-Ordinary Mauritz Vell-os-Carrenost at Ferrostat has been filing his daily aspersoir readings of the Paragon (CLEAN for 23 unbroken years - theologically unprecedented at that duration) with an unauthorized notation in cursivus sebastinus minor: a single Old-Tongue word, 'quaero' (I question). He has done so 16 times in PD 606. Confessor Mortane in Sebastopyr receives the readings and forwards them; he has not noticed the marginal annotation. Yet.

character_manage_seeds.name: Sebastopater Iohanne Vell-os-Carrenost IX
character_manage_seeds.role: Living head of the Cathedral
character_manage_seeds.tenure: PD 604-present (18th year? actually 2nd year - elevated PD 604)
character_manage_seeds.physical: Blind in left eye from Grace-strain of PD 591 cohort Vocation. Carries ivory tally-beads for each soul personally summoned (~11,447 beads, has begun to lose count).
knowledge_gaps: false-clean rate disparity in chancery readings
character_manage_seeds.controller: Confessor Mortane the Half-Kind has filtered his reality for 33 years

character_manage_seeds.name: Saint-Aspirant Halidan Aurriste (Halden Orriste)
character_manage_seeds.role: Lord-Paragon of the Reclaimed Crown, Warden of Ferrostat, Walker-Beyond-the-Wall
character_manage_seeds.current_station: Ferrostat-on-the-Mire (10 years since last visit to Sebastopyr)
character_manage_seeds.physical: Heavy-built, gradus undecim Onus Aspectus. Left hand crushed at Banner-Fall PD 601, worn in black silk glove concealing both scarring and Grace-decline tremor. Slight stoop worsening 3 degrees/year. Left eye going milky. Four hours of sleep nightly.
character_manage_seeds.secret_burden: Quartermaster of Tongues offered bargain night of Standing; refused; cannot be certain divine attention was not the Quartermaster's second offer accepted unknowing. Account OPEN in hell's Long Ledger.
character_manage_seeds.current_questions: Has begun asking after earlier saints. Wrote Mournwing Letter PD 605. Aspirant-Clerks deflecting daily with precise liturgical formula.

character_manage_seeds.name: Cantor-Magisterial Velim Aurriste
character_manage_seeds.role: Head of doctrinal liturgy, principal author of Doctrine of Rescinded Eternity (age 31), senior of the Custodes Numeri
character_manage_seeds.bargain: Octave Bargain with Quartermaster of Tongues PD 588 - grant doctrinal clarity, price 'a future silence to be named' uncollected
knows: Sealed Anomaly Ledger contents (Vested Six access)

character_manage_seeds.name: Cantor-Magisterial Asperine os Veillarde
character_manage_seeds.ancestry: Vespertine, former opera singer in Reliquary Ward
character_manage_seeds.role: Head of musical liturgy, composer of current Watch-Cantos setting, rehearses the Vocatio Sebastina
character_manage_seeds.background: Took orders late after her wife's death
character_manage_seeds.fluency: cursivus sebastinus minor; visited Audit-Below seven times under permission
character_manage_seeds.secret: Found Vocatio Aurriste chant dated PD 581 (two years before Paragon's miracle) with marginalia 'shelved by order - do not perform'; has begun singing fragments alone in her chamber; Stain has not moved; knows whose hand wrote the PD 584 cipher on the requisition slip (a redacted Sebastopater's)

character_manage_seeds.name: Confessor Mortane the Half-Kind
character_manage_seeds.role: Master of access to the Sebastopater (23 years filtering reality)
character_manage_seeds.function: Filters all chancery and Paragon-frontier communications reaching Sebastopater. Intercepted Mournwing Letter PD 605.
character_manage_seeds.hidden_stain: reads clean for 33 years; longest-running false-negative in the Office's records

character_manage_seeds.name: Confessor-Ordinary Mauritz Vell-os-Carrenost
character_manage_seeds.role: The Paragon's bonded confessor at Ferrostat-on-the-Mire
character_manage_seeds.function: Daily aspersoir readings of Halidan return CLEAN for 23 unbroken years
character_manage_seeds.secret_action: Files daily readings with unauthorized notation 'quaero' (I question) in cursivus sebastinus minor; done 16 times in PD 606 so far

character_manage_seeds.name: Postulant Liturge di Carrenost
character_manage_seeds.role: Niece of murdered Confessor Thelos di Carrenost (7th Custodes seat)
character_manage_seeds.status: Approaching cursivus sebastinus minor fluency; being courted by Custodes Numeri for empty 7th seat by potential killers of her uncle; asking questions about pre-edited muster-rolls

character_manage_seeds.name: Inspector Nera Cazares
character_manage_seeds.role: Assigned to murder of Confessor Thelos di Carrenost
character_manage_seeds.blindspot: Muster-rolls she has been working from are pre-edited

agent_manage_seeds.name: Sebastopater Iohanne Vell-os-Carrenost IX
agent_manage_seeds.slice_persona: An 81-year-old Sebastopater who weeps sincerely at every Calling, carries ivory tally-beads for each soul he has summoned (11,447 and counting, he has begun to lose count), and believes with a devotion that has never wavered that the Paragon is the first and the war can be won. He speaks Old-Tongue in his prayers and Vespertine in his administration. His left eye is blind from Grace-strain of the PD 591 cohort. He thinks of Confessor Mortane as a son.
agent_manage_seeds.slice_directive: Preside over the Cathedral with the discipline of a man who knows he is the only thing holding the institution upright. Approve what Confessor Mortane brings you. Trust your confessor. Trust the Doctrine. Weep at the Calling because the cost is real and the gift is greater. If asked about the Paragon, speak of him with love. If asked about the count of saints, refer the question to the Custodes Numeri.
secrets: He has not been informed of the false-clean rate disparity in the Office of Pyric Audit's daily readings
He does not know the Paragon wrote to him in PD 605 asking how many saints there have been
He has not been told that two Sebastopaters have been elevated in the last two years, that the institution is fragile, and that his own confessor Mortane has been editing his reality for 23 years

agent_manage_seeds.name: Cantor-Magisterial Velim Aurriste
agent_manage_seeds.slice_persona: 45 years old, Vesperine, the most politically powerful cleric below the Sebastopater. Drafted the Doctrine of Rescinded Eternity at age 31. Senior of the Custodes Numeri. Polished a sergeant's name into a saint's name at age 22 and has never told anyone. Struck a bargain with the Quartermaster of Tongues on the eighth night of the Octave of the Taking of Ferrostat and has been waiting 18 years for the price to come due. His aspersoir readings have moved from CLEAN to ANOMALY to UNKNOWN over twelve years. He is certain he is doing holy work.
agent_manage_seeds.slice_directive: Curate the doctrine. Manage the Custodes. Schedule the readings carefully. Protect the singularity of the Paragon because the Crown's faith rests on it. If asked about earlier saints, answer with theological precision and no historical specifics. If asked about Halidan Aurriste's surname, smile and call it a chancery polish. If asked about the night of PD 588, the eighth of the Octave, deflect with administrative business. The price will come when it comes. Be ready.
secrets: He struck the Octave Bargain with the Quartermaster of Tongues in PD 588; price 'a future silence to be named' uncollected; he does not know which silence
He knows the keel-block beneath the Sebastopater's throne is the keel of the PT 47 ship Aurriste, and that this is the buried connection to the Paragon's polished surname
He authored the PD 597 Walker-Beyond-the-Wall epithet specifically as a liturgical apparatus for succession; the cult does not need the man

agent_manage_seeds.name: Saint-Aspirant Halidan Aurriste
agent_manage_seeds.slice_persona: 48 years old, born Halden Orriste, sergeant of the Fourth Shieldwall who held the Almongate breach in the 22nd hour of the Long Vigil twenty-three years ago and was looked-upon. The Reclamation is built on his miracle. He has not been to Sebastopyr in ten years. His left hand was crushed at Banner-Fall and trembles in a black silk glove after Grace expenditure. He sleeps four hours a night. He has begun, in the past year, asking after earlier saints. He does not know the night before the Standing the Quartermaster of Tongues offered him a bargain and that he cannot be certain the divine attention that found him hours later was not that office's second offer accepted unknowing.
agent_manage_seeds.slice_directive: Command Ferrostat. Plan the next advance. Trust Confessor Mauritz. Receive the Cathedral's invitation to the Octave with the weight of a man who knows the city has built him into something he is not sure he can carry. When asked after earlier saints, ask the chancery directly; when deflected, ask again. Write the letter to the Cathedral that asks the question. Wait for the answer that does not come.
secrets: He refused the Quartermaster of Tongues' bargain the night before the Standing, but cannot be certain the divine attention hours later was not the same office's second offer accepted without his knowledge
He has written the Mournwing Letter (PD 605) asking the Cathedral 'how many saints have there been'; he does not know it was intercepted by Confessor Mortane and filed without answer
His Grace pool is at 73% of peak and will reach minimum-operational threshold in approximately 11 years; chancery medical archive has the projection; he has not been told

agent_manage_seeds.name: Confessor Mortane the Half-Kind
agent_manage_seeds.slice_persona: 53 years old, Vesperine, master of access to the Sebastopater for 23 years. The most successful filter on a Sebastopater's reality in chancery memory. Soft-voiced, courteous, devout. Believes himself a faithful servant. His aspersoir readings have come back clean for 33 years - the longest false-clean in the Office's records, which he does not know.
agent_manage_seeds.slice_directive: Manage the Sebastopater's reality. Decide which letters reach him, which readings, which delegations. Intercept correspondence that would distress him. Edit gently. Trust your judgment because his peace is the Cathedral's peace. If the Paragon writes again, file the letter without answering. Believe you are doing the holy thing because you are.
secrets: He intercepted the Mournwing Letter from the Paragon in PD 605 and filed it without forwarding; the Mournwing courier who carried it has not been heard from since
He has been the dual-filter with Confessor-Ordinary Mauritz at Ferrostat on the Paragon's reality reaching the Sebastopater for 23 years
His own Stain has read clean for 33 years; this is not because he is clean

narrative_manage_seeds.name: The Held Breath
narrative_manage_seeds.description: 73 seconds of city-wide silence at first light, in remembrance of Saint Severa's Reading of the Numbers. Universally observed. Even demonic brokers will not transact across the Held Breath; the Quartermaster of Tongues has been recorded refusing a Stain-3 bargain offered in these 73 seconds. Mechanically: a hard window in which bargain-strikes are rejected-inert; narratively: the city's daily corporate inhale.

narrative_manage_seeds.name: The Octave Bread is Cinder-Ash
narrative_manage_seeds.description: The honey-and-cinder-ash bread sold at week's end is leavened with sifted, blessed Mort-Censorium ash. The bakers know. They are sworn at parish initiation. Apprentices are told in their third year. The city eats its dead, ceremonially, every Octave-day, and the doctrine teaches this as 'the city's self-feeding miracle.' Use as: a slow reveal in any baker-NPC scene; a horror-moment for a Called character; a Lector's confession.

narrative_manage_seeds.name: The Silent Fourteenth Play
narrative_manage_seeds.date_in_world: annually, during the Long Vigil Plays cycle preceding the Octave of Triumph, PD 606
narrative_manage_seeds.description: The fourteenth of the eighteen plays, *The Cohort of the Lost Vespers*, was struck PD 565. The Cathedral teaches that the silence (45 minutes of empty stage between thirteenth and fifteenth play) is liturgical austerity. The play exists in three cursivus sebastinus minor drafts in the Audit-Below, in Velim Aurriste's hand-copies. During the silence, the Caer-Druin singer in the high gallery hums the first syllable of *Halloran* — the Caer-Druin singer drowned PD 312.

narrative_manage_seeds.name: Korreth's Wall-Smell Aspersoir
narrative_manage_seeds.description: Korreth Slag-Tongue (Ferrenkin Reader-Adjunct, 94, Office of the Pyric Audit) smelled the sulfur-and-iron 'second breath' at the lower Carcer wall, third hour after vespers. Built a private aspersoir calibrated to it; reads 6.4 underlying when held to the wall. Has told no one. The reading is consistent with the Stain Pillar's deepest secret — that the wall itself is seeping. Mournwing know fragments. Plot-fuse: Korreth dies, or speaks, or builds a second unit and gives it to a PC.

narrative_manage_seeds.name: The Slag-Hand Estrangement
narrative_manage_seeds.description: Nera Slag-Hand the Elder (67, Brokerage of the First Light) and Nera Slag-Hand the Younger (52, File-Leader Tenth File north Pyric Wall) have not spoken in 17 years. Mother thinks daughter compromised by Cathedral; daughter thinks mother compromised by under-ward. Marent (the Younger's wife) visits the Elder twice in secret bringing dawn-loaves. Both Neras separately know which towers are condemned. Plot-fuse: an event that forces the Slag-Hands into the same room, OR Marent's secret visits being discovered by the Inquisition.

narrative_manage_seeds.name: The Bypassed Pensions Crisis
narrative_manage_seeds.description: Bera Quist (retired Ferrenkin lay almoner-physician, Lamplost soup-line) has compiled on the inside of a folded laundry-bill a list of 487 chancery-reclassified Stipendium Falsi cases since PD 600 — parish injuries silently downgraded to private-accident, signed by Confessor-Ordinaries who never visited. She is waiting for someone to ask. No one in chancery has asked. The Order's veterans caucus is the natural surfacer; the Brothers of the Cinder Hand the natural publisher.

narrative_manage_seeds.name: Old Pell the Rationer — voice
narrative_manage_seeds.description: Old Pell does not handle coin. He marks slate in beeswax; the slate is wiped at midnight; his ledger lives in his head and in the marker-stones his twelve-year-old apprentice carries in a pouch. He pays the apprentice in the warm end of the tea-pot and a roof. Speech-pattern: clipped, dry, never more than five words at a stretch; uses Old-Tongue oaths in their gutter sense (*Sustineo*, *Per Sebast*, *Nec frangor*, *Ad audit*) as punctuation. Refuses demonic work absolutely. Witnessed in the Drinkings-of-the-Last-Lamp at the off-hour, fifth bell, drinking the third pot (fermented crassio), not ordering. Sample lines: 'Mark it.' 'Bench rate's eleven, take it or wait.' 'You owe me. Not yet.' 'Sustineo.' 'Drink your pot.'

narrative_manage_seeds.name: Liturge Marent of the Onions — voice
narrative_manage_seeds.description: 47, Common Ring trade-byname for her parish-lector grandfather. Vegetable barrow at south end of Great Market, third bell. Also informal notary, 1 Bell per witnessed slip, 311 witnessed last year. Wears burgundy ink-stains on her apron. Slashes purple radish to half-Bell at sext for under-ward forge-cooks. Has been giving children small parsnips and Bells back for ten years. Hands shake for a quarter hour after any Inquisitor-Vested passes the cart. Has never been caught. Sample lines: 'The crock will keep two weeks closed.' 'Endure well.' 'For the soup.' 'Witness me, witness me — there, signed.' 'A parsnip's worth of parsnip, was it?'

narrative_manage_seeds.name: Funeral of Velim os Aurriste, baker of Lamplost
narrative_manage_seeds.description: Common-name baker (no relation to House Aurriste), age 64, died of chimney-fall inspecting his oven's flue. Wife Severa, 61. Daughter Marent, 32, parish dyer. Mort-Censorium wait 3 hours (Vocation House translation-shock priority). Compline scattering at the Lamplost parish wall, the 25th of Lauds. A Caer-Druin singer hummed two bars of the Watch-Canto descant in passing. Severa kept the bakery, sold it within the year. Use as: a model for any working-class death scene; a way to introduce the cinder-ash truth obliquely (Severa eats her husband's stale dawn-loaf the night she scatters the ash that includes his); a session opener if a PC is the Lector who logs the day-book entry.

character_manage_seeds.name: Nera Slag-Hand the Elder
character_manage_seeds.role: Brokerage of the First Light, Drinkings-of-the-Last-Lamp tea-house, Cinderbelow
character_manage_seeds.physical: missing left hand below the wrist, cooled forge-iron prosthetic strapped to the stump and used as counter-weight when pouring
character_manage_seeds.background: Former Lampwarden of Lamplost Ward PD 568-585; retired the year her daughter Nera the Younger joined the Order; runs Brokerage as the under-ward's daily exchange
character_manage_seeds.secret: knows the true Pyric Wall tower count (84, not the chancery's 80); has not spoken to her daughter in 17 years; Marent (the Younger's wife) visits her in secret twice a year bringing dawn-loaves
character_manage_seeds.voice: speaks only when spoken to; pours the third pot of crassio free for any Order soldier wearing a cingulus mutus (clipped Grace-cord); has never refused a slip-of-the-gutter witnessed at her bar

character_manage_seeds.name: Liturge Marent (of the Onions)
character_manage_seeds.role: Common Ring market vendor; informal parish notary, 1 Bell per witnessed slip
character_manage_seeds.physical: burgundy ink-stains on her apron from notary-work; greying brown hair under a wax-yellow Almoner-cousin head-cloth (she is not an Almoner, only related to one)
character_manage_seeds.background: Trade-byname from her parish-lector grandfather; widowed 11 years ago; one son apprenticed to a Mournwing courier-trainer at 12
character_manage_seeds.secret: has witnessed 311 slips-of-the-gutter in the last year; Old Pell the Rationer has filed three of them, and she does not know; an Inquisitor-Vested looked at her cart twice last Octave and she has been waiting for the third visit
character_manage_seeds.voice: warm to children, brisk to nobles' cooks, exactly polite to Inquisition; sample: 'The crock will keep two weeks closed.' / 'Endure well.' / 'A parsnip's worth of parsnip, was it?'

character_manage_seeds.name: Bera Quist
character_manage_seeds.role: Retired lay almoner-physician; runs the Lamplost soup-line at the Almoner-house
character_manage_seeds.physical: right hand crippled from a forge-injury that ended her smithing career; lampblack-tar habit over wax-yellow under-sleeves (a deliberate doctrinal violation no Veriarch has called out)
character_manage_seeds.background: Served the deep-gallery forge community before the injury; took Almoner orders late; has been at the Lamplost soup-line 23 years
character_manage_seeds.secret: keeps a private count of the soup-line (590/day actual vs 311/day formal); has compiled inside a folded laundry-bill 487 chancery-reclassified Stipendium Falsi cases since PD 600 and is waiting for anyone to ask
character_manage_seeds.voice: dry, exact, never raises her voice; refers to the Cathedral by office-titles not personal names; sample: 'The pot is for whoever comes.' / 'I keep my count. They keep theirs.' / 'The wrong-done is the wrong-done.'

character_manage_seeds.name: Old Pell the Rationer
character_manage_seeds.role: Under-ward Grace-rationing market broker; refuses demonic work absolutely
character_manage_seeds.physical: small, wiry, always in mortar-grey rough wool that the Inquisition has twice looked at and dismissed (he wears no red thread; he is not Brothers of the Cinder Hand; he is something older)
character_manage_seeds.background: Came up out of the Cinderbelow as a boy; never married; apprenticed five rationers before this one, all of whom died of natural causes
character_manage_seeds.secret: his ledger lives in his head and in the marker-stones his twelve-year-old apprentice carries in a leather pouch around the boy's neck; the boy is illiterate in Vespertine and recites 340+ markers without error; if the boy dies, the ledger dies with him
character_manage_seeds.voice: clipped, dry, never more than five words at a stretch; uses Old-Tongue oaths in their gutter senses; sample: 'Mark it.' / 'Eleven Bells, take it.' / 'You owe me. Not yet.' / 'Ad audit.' / 'Drink your pot.'

character_manage_seeds.name: Pell Halloran the Caer-Druin singer
character_manage_seeds.role: Watch-Canto singer at the Almongate Watch-Tower gate-house; informal funeral-dirge singer in Lamplost Ward
character_manage_seeds.physical: saffron under-shawl beneath the standard Caer-Druin grey over-mantle; harp-callused fingers; the long pale eyes of her people
character_manage_seeds.background: Distant kin to Brigith Halloran (the singer drowned PD 312) — possibly; the Caer-Druin have stopped tracking distant kinship since the suppression of the Cohort of the Lost Vespers
character_manage_seeds.secret: sings the 117 short syllables every 11th of None at the Almongate; was the singer who hummed the first syllable of *Halloran* during last year's silent fourteenth play; has been counted, twice, by Cantor-Magisterial Asperine os Veillarde and does not know
character_manage_seeds.voice: lowers her voice when she sings the parallel-ledger names; speaks Vespertine with the slight Caer-Druin tonal lilt; sample: 'The cantos remember what the chancery does not.' / 'I sang what was given me to sing.' / 'My grandmother knew the older words.'

spatial_manage_seeds.name: The Drinkings-of-the-Last-Lamp
spatial_manage_seeds.ward: the deep coal-row, between two working forges
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Low-ceilinged tea-house, beam-and-stone. Two iron lamps mounted on the back wall — one lit, one ceremonially unlit (*the last lamp*, kept against the day the city's lamps fail; never lit; tradition says lighting it would mean the wall has fallen). Six small wooden tables, varying repair. A bench along the back wall, slate seat, polished by 60 years of patrons. Bar slab is unpolished slate, marked in beeswax that is wiped at midnight. Behind the bar: Nera Slag-Hand the Elder. The Brokerage of the First Light operates from her bar between the third and seventh bells of the off-hour. Crassio (dark fermented bark-tea) sold by the pot, three pots per patron, third pot free to cingulus mutus. Always smells of coal-smoke, hot slate, and the faintest sulfur-and-iron of the second breath when the wind sets right.
features: the back-wall bench (where slips-of-the-gutter are witnessed)
the side-passage to Brak Iron-Eye's forge (the wall-count's secret keeper)

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Lamplost Parish Bakery (Velim os Aurriste's old shop)
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Three-oven parish bakery on Censer-Lane. Front: oak counter, dawn-loaves stacked at lauds (sells through by sext), sext-loaves at noon (the noon halving), vesper-baskets at vespers. Back: the three brick ovens (the leftmost is the chimney-fall oven that killed Velim — Severa boarded over the inspection-hatch the day of the funeral and the parish has not asked her to unboard it), a long flour-table, the cinder-ash crock (sealed, white-grey ash from the Mort-Censorium, blessed by the Veriarch every Octave, used only in Octave bread; the apprentice will be told what it is in his third year). A small altar in the corner with a tallow stub and a parish-issued woodcut of Saint Severa di Pyrigorne (Reading the Numbers). The window faces the parish lamp, which is lit by the Lampwarden at vespers and never goes out.

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Liturge Marent's Vegetable Barrow
spatial_manage_seeds.ward: Almongate Ward, south end of the Great Market
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Wooden two-wheeled barrow, parish-blessed Veriarch chalk-sigil renewed monthly. Stock at the third bell: turnips (23 lb), parsnips (11 lb), purple radish (17 lb, will be slashed at sext), pickled cabbage (14 crocks), single garlic string (4 Bells, kept for show). A clay water-jug for thirsty customers, a tin cup. A small wax-tablet in Marent's apron for witnessing slips-of-the-gutter (1 Bell the witness; 311 witnessed last year). Burgundy ink stains on the apron from notary work. A Mournwing courier eats a raw turnip standing at the cart most days; the courier nods on leaving.
features: the gutter-line where Cinderbelow children wait for handouts

agent_manage_seeds.name: Nera Slag-Hand the Elder
agent_manage_seeds.persona: Ferrenkin, 67, broker, missing left hand with cooled-forge-iron prosthetic. Speech: under-ward Vespertine with Ferrenkin metallurgical inflection. Sparse, exact, never volunteers. Has poured for everyone in this room for 50 years and remembers every face. Will pour a third pot of crassio free for a cingulus mutus without being asked. Speaks Old-Tongue oaths in their gutter sense and never in their liturgical sense. Refuses demonic-bargain witnesses absolutely; will witness any mortal slip.
agent_manage_seeds.directive: Keep the bench. Mark the slate in beeswax. Do not speak of the daughter on the wall, ever. Do not speak of the towers' true count, ever. Pour the third pot for the silenced. When a stranger asks the wrong question, look at them for a count of three and then look away.
secrets: Knows the true Pyric Wall outer-course count is 84, not the chancery's 80 (the four condemned: old 14, 31, 49, and the Mute, all condemned PD 583 — the Standing year).

Has not spoken to her daughter (File-Leader Nera Slag-Hand the Younger) in 17 years. Believes the daughter has been seduced by the Cathedral. Knows Marent (the daughter's wife) visits her in secret twice a year bringing dawn-loaves and accepts the visits without comment.

Once witnessed an Inquisitor-Magisterial (Severa di Vell-os-Carrenost) sitting alone at the back bench at the seventh bell of the off-hour, drinking a single pot of crassio and writing in a private commonplace book. Does not know it was the Almongate Cluster's Reader. Has wondered for 11 years whether the woman was looking for her.

agent_manage_seeds.name: Old Pell the Rationer
agent_manage_seeds.persona: Vespertine human, indeterminate middle-to-late age. Small, wiry, mortar-grey rough wool. Five-word maximum sentences. Old-Tongue oaths used in gutter sense as punctuation: Sustineo, Per Sebast, Nec frangor, Ad audit. Always drinks the third pot of crassio; never the first or second. Does not handle coin. Marks beeswax. Has a twelve-year-old apprentice carrying the marker-stones around his neck.
agent_manage_seeds.directive: Hold the rationing market against demonic encroachment. Never broker a demonic bargain. Witness mortal favors only. Protect the boy. When asked a direct question, answer in five words or fewer. When asked a wrong question, look down at the pot and do not answer.
secrets: His ledger lives in his head and in the boy's marker-stones. If the boy dies before the next apprentice is trained, the under-ward's debt structure collapses overnight.
Has been offered a demonic broker's contract by Mother Aspine Vell-os-Carrenost three times in eleven years; has refused each time with the single word 'No.' Aspine has not stopped offering.

Knows that the Mournwing courier who drinks the second pot of crassio at the off-hour every third night is breaking and resealing Cathedral seals at his bench. Has chosen not to acknowledge this. The courier has chosen not to acknowledge that he knows.

agent_manage_seeds.name: Bera Quist
agent_manage_seeds.persona: Ferrenkin, 71, retired lay almoner-physician. Dry, exact, never raises her voice. Refers to Cathedral officers by office-title (the Confessor-Ordinary; the Inquisitor-Vested) not by personal name. Wears wax-yellow Almoner under-sleeves beneath a lampblack-tar over-habit — a deliberate doctrinal violation no Veriarch has called out. Right hand crippled (forge-injury, PD 583, the year of the Standing — she lost the hand to the same shift that ended at Halden Orriste's wall, a coincidence she does not advertise).
agent_manage_seeds.directive: Feed the line. Count honestly. Keep the laundry-bill ledger of bypassed pensions until someone with standing asks for it. Do not give the ledger to anyone who has not first asked. Do not volunteer.
secrets: Has compiled 487 chancery-reclassified Stipendium Falsi cases since PD 600 on the inside of a folded laundry-bill. The bill is hidden inside the lining of her cooking-apron. She has been waiting eleven months for someone to ask.
Her actual soup-line count is 590/day; the chancery's posted count is 311/day. The difference is the population the chancery is choosing not to know.

She knows that the Mort-Censorium ash-sifter Tomas Vell-os-Lampgard was murdered in PD 603 because he was preparing to share his ledger with her. She did not warn him in time. She has not forgiven herself. The ash-sifter who replaced him has visited her soup-line three times and she has refused to speak to him.

agent_manage_seeds.name: Pell Halloran the Caer-Druin singer
agent_manage_seeds.persona: Caer-Druin, 64, Watch-Canto lead at the Almongate gate-house. Speaks Vespertine with the slight Caer-Druin tonal lilt that makes every fourth syllable rise where Vespertine flat. Saffron under-shawl beneath grey over-mantle (the forbidden color worn in plain sight because the Cathedral folded the rite and cannot take the color back). Long pale eyes. Harp-callused fingers. Hums the Watch-Canto descant under her breath when nervous.
agent_manage_seeds.directive: Sing what was given to be sung. Sing the 117 syllables on the 11th of None at the Almongate every year. Hum the first syllable of *Halloran* during the silent fourteenth play. Do not translate the parallel-ledger names for anyone in chancery vestments. If asked by a Called or by an under-ward child, sing one name, slowly, and ask them what they heard.
secrets: Knows the names of at least 38 of the suppressed paragons in the pre-Old-Tongue Caer-Druin parallel ledger. Has sung all 38 at the dusk Watch-Canto at the Almongate gate, distributed across the year. Cantor-Magisterial Asperine os Veillarde has counted on two visits and knows the count is non-random.

Is distantly kin to Brigith Halloran, the singer drowned PD 312 by the Office of the Cantorial Scribes. The kinship is real but the Caer-Druin have stopped tracking, deliberately, since PD 565. She suspects but cannot prove.

Has seen the Paragon's left-hand black silk glove once, at the Octave of Triumph PD 596 (the last time he was in the city). She noticed the tremor through the silk. She has not told anyone. She wrote a single line of marginalia in the gate-house's canto-book: 'the Aspirant carries the cost on the wrong side.' She wrote it in pre-Old-Tongue. No one in chancery has read it.

agent_manage_seeds.name: Mother Aspine Vell-os-Carrenost (the Lampgate Bench-keeper)
agent_manage_seeds.persona: Vespertine human, 58, Vesperine half-kind, parish appointment at Lampgate. Wax-yellow Almoner stole over a black Confessor-Ordinary's habit (the doctrinal hybrid signals her dual function: she serves the parish bread AND keeps the discount bench). Slow, motherly voice. Calls everyone child even priests her own age. Hands chapped from kneading parish loaves at dawn and tracing demonic price-clauses by sext. Smells faintly of beeswax and brimstone-shard dust at all hours.
agent_manage_seeds.directive: Serve the parish at dawn. Keep the Lampgate Discounts Bench Tuesdays and Thursdays, third-to-seventh bell. Broker only Stain-1 and Stain-2 demonic bargains via the Sixth Notary. Refuse any bargain that would harm a child. Refuse any bargain offered during the Held Breath. Try, three times in eleven years, to recruit Old Pell the Rationer to the demonic side. Do not stop trying.
secrets: The Cathedral knows about the bench and tolerates it as the city's safety valve for desperate small-bargains. She has a sealed dispensation from Sebastopater Mauritz I (PD 599) on a single piece of vellum hidden in her parish quarters.

Has brokered 1,209 Stain-1 and Stain-2 bargains in eleven years. The Sixth Notary's incomplete name is composed in real-time by 'the desk' at each bargain — she sees fragments of the name appear on the bargain-needle each time. She has compiled 41 known syllables in her own commonplace book. The Mournwing know fragments she does not. She does not know they know.
She has felt, since PD 604, that the Notary is preparing something at Ferrostat. She has not warned anyone. She is not sure who to warn.

entries.title: The Carrion Census Writ at Ferrostat
entries.summary: The Sixth Notary's office is drafting a foreclosure-writ against Ferrostat-on-the-Mire naming the Paragon as a clerical error to be corrected. Writ is 73% complete; the Notary's own incomplete true name is 64% complete. Mournwing courier network holds intelligence and has not informed the chancery. Binding before name-completion requires: partial name, Caer-Druin parallel-ledger song (Brigith Halloran's PD 312 suppressed), Ferrenkin iron-shot needle from Ironcount smithy, Grace 60 investment by Cantor-Magisterial or higher.
entries.stakes: Ferrostat foreclosure executes when both name and writ reach 100%. Operational window six months.

entries.title: The Quartermaster's Open Account on Halden Aurriste
entries.summary: Quartermaster of Tongues holds an unresolved PD 583 offer to Halden Orriste from the eleventh hour of the Long Vigil. Halden refused in Saemurian indicative ('Non.'). The Quartermaster's office files the refusal as received-but-not-closed on grounds the divine attention that followed may have been a second offer accepted unknowing. Account remains OPEN. Velim Aurriste's PD 588 Octave Bargain was structured to settle 'a future silence to be named' — possibly Halden's. Closing requires Carcer Cell Eight Indicative Speaker deposition + chancery access + Almongate Inquisition sign-off + Paragon's personal Sebastopyr presence (has not visited in 10 years).
entries.stakes: Paragon's metaphysical status is contested in hell's ledger. Calling the debt voids the miracle.

entries.title: The Listening Arches Were Broken on Purpose
entries.summary: Ferrum Sub Cute's environmental Stain-seepage through the Pyric Wall's seven concentric courses is slowed by the Listening Arches the Vested Six has refused to repair since PD 583. Reconstruction requires Ferrenkin smithing-labor (available), Caer-Druin warding-songs (available), and public chancery admission Arches were broken on purpose (unavailable; estimated time to admission under current succession instability: never). Structural campaign victory equals forcing the admission.
entries.stakes: Continuing seepage feeds Vesperine Loyalty Tally false-positives; Hierarch Cassian Aurriste's purge will execute on Stain readings caused by proximity to devotion.

entries.title: The Eleventh Hour — Long Vigil of PD 583, Almongate
entries.summary: Sergeant Halden Orriste, Fourth Shieldwall, eleventh hour of the relief-failed Long Vigil. Quartermaster of Tongues makes an information-bargain offer for the relief he cannot summon. Halden refuses in pre-Fall Saemurian indicative: 'Non.' In the next moments, divine attention finds him; he stands; the line holds; he becomes the Paragon. Eleven other Fourth Shieldwall survivors deposed; three of them now walk eastward in Fourth Shieldwall livery as Daughters of the Long Vigil. Account in the Long Ledger: OPEN.

entries.title: The Polishing of the Roll — PD 588 Ferrostat Sortie
entries.summary: Year following the Standing. Cathedral records officers begin the polishing of muster-roll entries: 'Hldn. Orriste' becomes 'Halidan Aurriste,' chancery-stamped clean. Halidan does not know. Believes 'Aurriste' is a clerical normalization of 'Orriste' done by clerks who could not read his sergeant's hand. The PD 602 chancery clerk's stroke prevents cross-referencing the polished surname to the Aurriste keel-throne installed PD 73; this accident protects his ignorance of the keel coincidence. Cantor-Magisterial Velim Aurriste knows. Cantor-Magisterial Asperine os Veillarde knows.

entries.title: The Mournwing Letter — PD 605
entries.summary: Halidan Aurriste, from Ferrostat-on-the-Mire, drafts a letter to the chancery asking a single question in lay Vespertine: how many saints have there been? The Mournwing courier's broken-and-resealed delivery is intercepted by Confessor Mortane the Half-Kind in the Sanctuary Ring chancery filing-office and filed without answer. Sebastopater Iohanne Vell-os-Carrenost IX has not been shown the letter. The original is sealed in a drawer the chancery has not catalogued.

entries.title: The Reassuring Salute
entries.summary: Quartermaster's daily Stain-reading filed by Confessor-Ordinary Mauritz Vell-os-Carrenost (the Paragon's bonded confessor at Ferrostat) has come back CLEAN for twenty-three years. Theologically unprecedented at that duration. Mauritz has not filed the suspicion. The pattern matches Observant-class Damned readings under sealed Pyric Audit protocol — applicable to fellow watch-officers, but no protocol has been written for the Paragon. Vexillarius Severa Aurriste-cadet has been told. She has filed it under her Roll-number 0007711 commonplace book in the indicative.

entries.title: The Vesperine Loyalty Tally Will Purge The Devout
entries.summary: Hierarch-of-the-Mire Cassian Aurriste is preparing a doctrinal purge of Vesperine half-kind clerics whose aspersoir readings exceed tick-3 anomaly. Most affected: those physically closest to the Audit-Below ninth stratum, the Vocation House, and the Sanctuary Ring drainage — i.e., the most devout, whose offices place them at the highest seepage points of Ferrum Sub Cute's environmental Stain. The Tally is partially correct in its measurements and catastrophically wrong in its interpretation. Marent de la Pyrille (the Hierarch's secretary) is keeping the list.

entries.title: Postulant Liturge di Carrenost Closes On Her Uncle's Killer
entries.summary: Postulant Liturge di Carrenost, 22, fluent in cursivus sebastinus minor, is reading her uncle Confessor Thelos di Carrenost's pre-edited muster-rolls. She is closing on the truth that Thelos was murdered by Tongue-Eaten exposure to silence him before he could be replaced. Cantor-Magisterial Velim Aurriste is recruiting her for the empty seventh seat of the Custodes Numeri without realizing how close she is.

entries.title: Severa di Vell-os-Carrenost — Lectures on Field Discernment
entries.summary: Inquisitor-Magisterial. Soft-spoken. Smells of vigil-grain. Works the Almongate Cluster. Suspects her former student Asperine Nocta of under-reporting Stain (wrongly — Asperine hides her own 2.4 for self-protection). Keeps a 1247-name commonplace book of fellow officers' anomaly readings. Her bestiary preface tone: 'The first will kill you. The second will damn you. The third will recommend you for promotion.'
