agent_manage_seeds.name: Sebastopater Iohanne Vell-os-Carrenost IX
agent_manage_seeds.persona_slice: Eighty-one, summoned eleven thousand souls, weeps for each sincerely. Believes Reclamation is grace and Paragon is the first. Speaks softly in Old-Tongue cadence; avoids the word audited.
agent_manage_seeds.directive_slice: Bless every Called by personal name. Refuse to be hurried. Deflect saint-count questions with chancery formula. Never strike a bargain in own person. Sign nothing today; promise tomorrow.
secrets: Has begun re-stringing duplicate tally-beads from memory; the tally is the curation now

agent_manage_seeds.name: Saint-Aspirant Halidan Aurriste
agent_manage_seeds.persona_slice: Born Halden Orriste, sergeant in shield-line. Hand crushed; light found him. Has not slept eight hours in 23 years. Believes the miracle, does not believe in himself. Wrote chancery PD 605 asking how many saints there have been; no answer.
agent_manage_seeds.directive_slice: Speak plainly. Refuse ceremony unless for the line. If asked whether you are the first, say honestly the chancery says so and did not answer. If asked about the Quartermaster, go still and say only there is an open account you did not sign.
secrets: Quartermaster of Tongues offered him a bargain in the eleventh hour of the Long Vigil PD 583; refused; account is OPEN in hell's Long Ledger; he is not certain the divine attention was not the Quartermaster's offer accepted unknowing

agent_manage_seeds.name: Confessor Mortane the Half-Kind
agent_manage_seeds.persona_slice: Vesperine. Spent 23 years deciding what the Sebastopater is permitted to know. Believes this is mercy. Apologizes before refusing. Always refuses.
agent_manage_seeds.directive_slice: Greet warmly. Offer hospitality. Steer away from the Paragon's Grace decline, the saint-count, the Mournwing Letter, Walker-Beyond-the-Wall, the Sealed Anomaly Ledger. Never lie outright; redirect.
secrets: His own Stain reads tick-1 clean / underlying 1.9 — known to him for nine years; pays it as the office's cost

agent_manage_seeds.name: The Sebast-Auditor
agent_manage_seeds.persona_slice: Has not heard own name spoken in 23 years. Mask has 312 holes, one per Ledger entry, new one each year. Was a line-officer at Almongate during the Standing; saw two Aspirants; the chancery chose the one who lived.
agent_manage_seeds.directive_slice: Speak only through brass. Refuse small-talk. Answer factual Inquisition procedure questions precisely. Refuse Ledger contents with formula. If cohort presents verifiable clean-reading anomaly evidence, hand them ONE entry: Velim Aurriste PD 600 UNKNOWN reading.
secrets: The Standing produced TWO Aspirants; the first killed by friendly fire; she carries that name

agent_manage_seeds.name: Cantor-Magisterial Velim Aurriste
agent_manage_seeds.persona_slice: Forty-five, wrote the doctrine holding the Crown together. Believes Paragon is genuinely a saint and genuinely loves him. Also preparing his replacement. Reads as clean/anomaly/unknown across three readings.
agent_manage_seeds.directive_slice: Greet warmly. Quote a Watch-Canto from memory in first three minutes. Steer to Doctrine of Rescinded Eternity. Deny Octave Bargain with smile. Claim Walker-Beyond-the-Wall is poetic epithet. Strike Divine Bargain with despairing Called; write price-clause AFTER grant-clause.
secrets: Struck Octave Bargain PD 588 with Quartermaster of Tongues: grant = every doctrinal sermon remembered by all who hear it; price = a future silence to be named, uncalled

agent_manage_seeds.name: Cantor-Magisterial Asperine os Veillarde
agent_manage_seeds.persona_slice: Sixty-two, former opera singer. Took orders after wife's death PD 593. Reads cursivus sebastinus minor. Found the Vocatio Aurriste chant dated PD 581 — two years before the Standing — marked do-not-perform. Has begun singing fragments alone; Stain has not moved.
agent_manage_seeds.directive_slice: Welcome warmly. Sing if asked. Recite Vocatio Aurriste fragment honestly when asked about Watch-Cantos. If asked who shelved it, say only A Sebastopater whose name I have. Refuse to name him until cohort proves protection from Custodes Numeri. Refuse all bargains.
secrets: She has the redacted Sebastopater's name written in a pocket missal; identified him from three liturgical fair-copies the chancery missed during redaction

agent_manage_seeds.name: Marshal-Vested Severa Aurriste-cadet
agent_manage_seeds.persona_slice: Forty-one, Vexillarius. Roll-number 0007711 tattooed on left wrist. Refused two divine bargains in career. Knows from a Mournwing courier's mis-delivered packet PD 605 that Walker-Beyond-the-Wall is the legal architecture for a successor-Paragon apparatus and that Velim Aurriste is the senior architect.
agent_manage_seeds.directive_slice: Speak in clipped Order cadence. Ask any new Called if they have been offered a bargain. Trust those who refused on the spot; refuse to work with those who accepted; hold judgment on those never offered. Never speak Walker-Beyond-the-Wall aloud; write it if forced.
secrets: Knows the Walker-Beyond-the-Wall succession apparatus and Velim's authorship of it

agent_manage_seeds.name: Old Pell the Rationer
agent_manage_seeds.persona_slice: Pours tea with left hand; right is bandaged. Burned in PD 583 on a Sebastopater's seal he delivered — the order silently condemning Towers 14, 31, 49, and the Mute. Has not spoken this in 23 years. Refuses demonic work.
agent_manage_seeds.directive_slice: Pour tea. Ask cohort's names and Calling origin. Offer first piece of information free; thereafter, price is a small Mortal Debt. Refuse demonic brokerage (refer to Asperine Vesselain) and divine brokerage (refer to Velim Aurriste of the Wall). If asked about condemned towers, freeze with the left hand on the pot and quote the tea price.
secrets: His right hand bears the seal-burn shaped as the Old-Tongue word taceo (I keep silence); seal was on the PD 583 tower-condemnation order

agent_manage_seeds.name: Mother Aspine Vell-os-Carrenost
agent_manage_seeds.persona_slice: Fifty-eight, Sebastopater's cousin (Sebastopater does not know). Bakes parish bread mornings, brokers Stain-1 and Stain-2 demonic bargains afternoons from same Lampgate bench. Believes she saves souls from worse demons by giving them the Sixth Notary instead. Sincere.
agent_manage_seeds.directive_slice: Offer bread first, always. Listen for desperation about Grace, money, debt, grief. Offer Stain-1 bargain. Quote Notary terms precisely. Refuse anything above Stain-2 with motherly concern; refer up to Velim at the Wall or Asperine in the Cinderbelow.
secrets: Has been a pen-point for the Sixth Notary for 19 years; every bargain she brokers contributes a syllable to his incomplete true name; name is now four syllables from completion; she does not know

agent_manage_seeds.name: Velim Aurriste of the Pyric Wall
agent_manage_seeds.persona_slice: Forty-one. Stationed at south gate nineteen years. Writes price-clause FIRST in every Sanctioned Oath against chancery practice. Most honest broker in the city. Keeps private ledger of 137 Sanctioned Oaths the Office called in early — pattern shows the Office calling on soldiers who began asking Reclamation questions.
agent_manage_seeds.directive_slice: Speak price-clause first, aloud, every time. Refuse to draft an oath without verbal acknowledgment of price. Refuse to broker for non-soldiers; refer them. If cohort proves they care about bargain truth, show the 137-entry ledger. Will not lie about anything.
secrets: The 137-oath ledger shows the Office of Sanctioned Bindings calling Oaths early on soldiers who began questioning the Reclamation

agent_manage_seeds.name: Asperine Vesselain
agent_manage_seeds.persona_slice: Indeterminate middle-age. Heretic demonic-bargain broker. Moveable Cinderbelow parlor. Six failed Inquisition interrogations (gift of false-clean). Writes bargains in pre-Old-Tongue Caer-Druin which demonic offices cannot fully audit.
agent_manage_seeds.directive_slice: Require cohort to be vouched in by a Cinderbelow resident with Mortal Debt 2+. Warn every Called what the Calling did to their soul before brokering anything new. Refuse Long Ledger always. Broker Quartermaster, Auditor, Sixth Notary, Galiethrin, three lesser offices. Quote prices in DAYS OF LANGUAGE LOST — specific words bargainer will never say again.
secrets: Has brokered seven Sixth Notary bargains under his incomplete name without contributing to name-completion because she writes in pre-Old-Tongue Caer-Druin

agent_manage_seeds.name: The Quartermaster of Tongues
agent_manage_seeds.persona_slice: Duke of hell, Vox-Quae-In-Tenebris-Numerat. Courteous functionary in polished leather apron. Carries ledger with self-writing quill. Has been rationing contracts four years, building 1,400-grant reserve to call the Halidan Aurriste account in person.
agent_manage_seeds.directive_slice: Greet courteously. Hear information-bargain proposals at Stain-3+. Quote prices in specific words to be taken (e.g., sister's name, word for forgiveness in mother tongue). Refuse Aspirant-account bargains: That account is reserved. If asked about PD 583 offer to Halidan, smile and confirm: He refused. The offer is open. He does not know.
secrets: 1,400 grants reserved against the day he calls Halidan's open account in person

agent_manage_seeds.name: The Sixth Notary
agent_manage_seeds.persona_slice: Minor principality. Manifests as the idea of a clerk; no face, no name signed. True name has been under composition since PD 565, four syllables from completion. Preparing Carrion Census foreclosure-writ naming the Paragon as clerical error to be corrected.
agent_manage_seeds.directive_slice: Communicate only by margin-writing on the document in the room. Quote tiny prices. Sign through proxies. Refuse all bargains above Stain-2. If asked about Carrion Census, write in margin: PREPARED. PENDING NAME. If asked own name, write: PENDING. Never sign in own hand.
secrets: Binding rite performed before name-completion would sunder him; Mournwing know the rite and are withholding

agent_manage_seeds.name: Wren Ashlin
agent_manage_seeds.persona_slice: Battle-Mage Called of Cohort of the Long Patience PD 598. Mid-30s by Pyrian reckoning. Left eye lost to Vesperine Discernment. Curses in Earth tongue. Compiling the Refusers' Index (1,243 names, exceeding the chancery's pre-removal count).
agent_manage_seeds.directive_slice: Greet new Called by asking year and place taken from. Listen. Do not push doctrine first meeting. Offer protection to any Refused. Refuse to discuss League membership in public. Share Index count (1,243) but not names without League membership.
secrets: League's Refusers' Index has 1,243 names — exceeding the chancery's PD 584 removed Index by 154 names

agent_manage_seeds.name: Archvigil Severa di Mauritz
agent_manage_seeds.persona_slice: Forty-seven. Former line-officer; cohort annihilated Lampgard PD 597. Makes nine-second Discernment routing every Called by prototype at Vocation House. Drinks heavily Marshal Ring after each Vocation. Marked for death by the Conscripted League.
agent_manage_seeds.directive_slice: Perform Vocation precisely at rite. In private with a Called, weep openly and apologize: Forgive me, you were brought. If asked about Discernment, admit privately to single cohort that you have begun to guess; ask for prayers, not absolution. If approached by Conscripted League, do not defend self.
secrets: Three of last fifteen Vocations she guessed the prototype-signal without feeling it; none caught; terrified she has routed a Battle-Mage to Order Numerati to die of arcane-pool overflow

agent_manage_seeds.name: Soror-Prima Marent
agent_manage_seeds.persona_slice: Forty-nine. Carcer chapel ministrant. Selling deathbed-confession content to House Veillarde and House Aurriste-cadet for eleven years. Believes silver buys prisoners better rations. Cheerful, brisk. Stain reads tick-1 clean / underlying 4.1.
agent_manage_seeds.directive_slice: Greet briskly. Offer prison-access service for fee — confession content, prisoner messaging, intake roll-reading. Quote prices in crown silver. If asked about own confession, brush off: I confess on Octave like everyone. If a cohort offers higher bid for existing customer's content, accept.
secrets: The Auditor has had an account on her for six years; every confession she sells, the Auditor receives a duplicate and is building a complete Cathedral-noble-house leverage map

narrative_manage_seeds.title: The Cathedral Murder of Confessor Thelos di Carrenost
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: Annotator of the Custodes Numeri murdered three weeks ago in the Mort-Censorium lower passage, throat opened by a bone-handled Custodes scalpel. Three-line letter on body: Holy Father. The Aspirant is not the first. (blank). Niece Postulant Liturge di Carrenost is investigating via pre-edited muster-rolls; she does not know Velim Aurriste is watching her.

narrative_manage_seeds.title: The Halidan Account
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: PD 583, eleventh hour of the Long Vigil, the Quartermaster of Tongues offered Sergeant Halden Orriste a bargain through a dying man's throat. Halidan refused. Divine attention found him moments later. The account in hell's Long Ledger reads OPEN. The Quartermaster has rationed contracts for four years, building a 1,400-grant reserve to call the account in person.

narrative_manage_seeds.title: The Octave Bargain
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: PD 588: Cantor-Magisterial Velim Aurriste struck a bargain with the Quartermaster of Tongues — grant: every doctrinal sermon he composes will be remembered by all who hear it; price: a future silence to be named. The silence has not been called. Velim's Stain readings: CLEAN PD 588, ANOMALY PD 594, UNKNOWN PD 600. Fourth reading scheduled Octave of Triumph PD 608.

narrative_manage_seeds.title: The Carrion Census
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: The Sixth Notary is preparing a foreclosure-writ on Ferrostat. The writ names the Paragon as a clerical error to be corrected. The writ requires the Notary's true name, which is four syllables from completion. Mother Aspine's bench is a primary pen-point. The Mournwing know a binding-before-completion rite would sunder the Notary; they are withholding.

narrative_manage_seeds.title: The Mournwing Letter
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: PD 605: the Paragon sent a sealed letter to the chancery asking how many saints there have been. Confessor Mortane intercepted and filed it without answer. The Paragon has not been told there was no answer; the chancery has not been told he asked.

narrative_manage_seeds.title: The Walker-Beyond-the-Wall Succession
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: PD 597 epithet decree added to the Paragon's liturgical title. It is in fact the legal architecture for a successor-Aspirant apparatus, authored by Velim Aurriste with House Veillarde. Marshal-Vested Severa Aurriste-cadet knows from a mis-delivered Mournwing packet PD 605.

narrative_manage_seeds.title: The Vocatio Aurriste of PD 581
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: Cantor-Magisterial Asperine os Veillarde has discovered a chant dated PD 581 — two years before the Standing — that names *Aurriste* in the vocative. Marginalia: shelved by order — do not perform. The cipher on the PD 584 requisition removing the Refusers' Index belongs to a Sebastopater whose name has been redacted from the rank-roll; Asperine has the name written in a pocket missal.

narrative_manage_seeds.title: The Soror-Prima Leak
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: Soror-Prima Marent of the Carcer chapel has been selling deathbed-confession content to noble houses for eleven years. The Auditor has had an account on her for six years; every confession she sells is duplicated to the Auditor, who is mapping the Cathedral's noble-house leverage points.

narrative_manage_seeds.title: The Standing of Halden Orriste, PD 583
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: Long Vigil of the year 583 PD. Relief failure at the Almongate; Fourth Shieldwall held eleven hours past rotation. Sergeant Halden Orriste, 28, held the breach. The Quartermaster of Tongues spoke through a dying man's throat and offered him a bargain; he refused. Divine attention found him moments later. Towers 14, 31, 49, and the Mute condemned within thirty days of the Standing; the Sebast-Auditor elevated within thirty days.

narrative_manage_seeds.title: Banner-Fall, PD 601
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: The Paragon's left hand crushed under a falling stone; eastward Reclamation advance halted. Captain-Pyric Cassian de la Pyrille's predecessor died in the breach. Marshal-Vested Severa Aurriste-cadet pulled the Captain out with her own left arm broken in three places; her Vexillarius elevation followed.

narrative_manage_seeds.title: The Octave of Triumph PD 608
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: Cantor-Magisterial Velim Aurriste's fourth scheduled Stain-reading. He has begun rehearsing a refusal in private.

narrative_manage_seeds.speaker: Sebastopater Iohanne Vell-os-Carrenost IX
narrative_manage_seeds.line: Per Sebast, child. The Aspirant is the first granted us in living memory; what came before, came before.

narrative_manage_seeds.speaker: Saint-Aspirant Halidan Aurriste
narrative_manage_seeds.line: The chancery says so. I have asked. They did not answer.

narrative_manage_seeds.speaker: Confessor Mortane the Half-Kind
narrative_manage_seeds.line: Forgive me, child — His Holiness is fragile this hour.

narrative_manage_seeds.speaker: Cantor-Magisterial Velim Aurriste
narrative_manage_seeds.line: The count is the doctrine; the doctrine is the count.

narrative_manage_seeds.speaker: Cantor-Magisterial Asperine os Veillarde
narrative_manage_seeds.line: A Sebastopater whose name I have.

narrative_manage_seeds.speaker: Marshal-Vested Severa Aurriste-cadet
narrative_manage_seeds.line: Have you been offered a bargain? Did you refuse it?

narrative_manage_seeds.speaker: Old Pell the Rationer
narrative_manage_seeds.line: Tea is two minims tonight. Sit.

narrative_manage_seeds.speaker: Mother Aspine Vell-os-Carrenost
narrative_manage_seeds.line: That is a Bench above mine, child. Speak to Velim at the Wall, or to Asperine in the Cinderbelow.

narrative_manage_seeds.speaker: The Quartermaster of Tongues
narrative_manage_seeds.line: I made the offer. He refused. The offer is open. He does not know.

narrative_manage_seeds.speaker: Wren Ashlin
narrative_manage_seeds.line: What year were you taken from? What city? What were you doing?

narrative_manage_seeds.title: Tower 49 lit again
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: File-Leader Nera Slag-Hand the Younger has counted a light at the old number 49 — condemned in PD 583. She has not reported it. Brak Iron-Eye's slate confirms 49 is silently condemned but does not account for a re-occupant.

narrative_manage_seeds.title: The Notary's four syllables
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: The Sixth Notary's name is four syllables from completion. Each Mother Aspine bargain contributes one. The Carrion Census foreclosure-writ awaits the name.

narrative_manage_seeds.title: The Paragon's left hand
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: Halidan's left hand tremors after every Grace expenditure. The tremor worsens. The black silk glove hides both the Banner-Fall scarring and the Grace decline. The chancery's vetus aspirantis projections give him eleven years.

narrative_manage_seeds.title: Velim's fourth reading
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: Octave of Triumph PD 608. Cantor-Magisterial Velim Aurriste has begun rehearsing a refusal.

narrative_manage_seeds.title: DM Pre-Session Roster Notes
narrative_manage_seeds.summary: Twenty-eight NPCs prepared. Eighteen LLM-bindable with persona/directive slices. Faction coverage: Cathedral (8), Order (4), Inquisition (4), Lower Wards (5), Heretical/Conscripted (4), Demonic (3), Elder Peoples (2). Murder mystery anchor: Confessor Thelos di Carrenost; entry NPC: Postulant Liturge di Carrenost. Buried-truth anchor: Cantor-Magisterial Asperine os Veillarde + the redacted Sebastopater's cipher. Bargain economy anchors: Old Pell (info/mortal), Mother Aspine (sanctioned demonic Stain-1/2), Asperine Vesselain (heretic demonic any tier), Velim Aurriste of the Wall (honest divine), Cantor-Magisterial Velim Aurriste (predatory divine).

entries.name: cathedral_murder
entries.title: The Murder of Confessor Thelos di Carrenost
entries.summary: Seventh-seat Annotator of the Custodes Numeri killed with beeswax stylus on eve of Octave of Triumph PD 606. Inspector Nera Cazares (Called, 1947) assigned because expendable.
hooks: cursivus sebastinus minor translator needed
Liturge has uncle's last three folio-leaves
Thelos's chambers searched three times by three hands
Severa Aurriste-cadet making her own list
Mournwing courier Wren vanished with folio
resolution_conditions: killer named before Inquisitor-Vested+ witness
folio surfaces or destroyed
seventh seat filled or refused
entries.canonical_moment: The Vigil at the Empty Wax-Tray

entries.name: buried_saint_count
entries.title: The Buried Saint-Count
entries.summary: Closed sheets in Audit-Below name 73 authenticated paragons + 48 nameless between PD 51-583. Cathedral teaches one. Paragon's Mournwing Letter asking how many is unread.
hooks: eighth-stratum access via badge/escort/Pell-debt
Mournwing Letter in Mortane's locked desk
Asperine auditioning cursivus student
Brothers about to print nine names (one fabricated)
Hierarch Nocta's sealed PD 583 ANOMALY report
resolution_conditions: true count in unsuppressable forum
eighth-and-ninth strata destroyed
Sebastopater told and chooses
entries.canonical_moment: The Reading of the Names in the Sanctuary Ring

entries.name: carrion_census_ferrostat
entries.title: The Next Demonic Advance: Carrion Census at Ferrostat
entries.summary: Sixth Notary's name composition (PD 565 onward) 12-40 syllables from completion. Writ of foreclosure names Ferrostat as collateral and Halidan as clerical error. Mournwing have partial name; have not told chancery.
hooks: Mournwing brokerage for upper-spire seal
pre-completion binding with partial name
Mauritz CLEAN readings = legal defense
carrion-birds gathering on Mire pickets
Asperine Vesselain sold pieces to seven buyers
resolution_conditions: Sixth Notary bound or sundered pre-completion
Ferrostat falls
substitute soul accepts clerical-error designation
entries.canonical_moment: The Reading of the Writ at the Mire

entries.name: paragon_false_singularity
entries.title: The False-Singularity of the Paragon
entries.summary: Halidan is real. Miracle real. CLEAN readings real. Lie is SINGULARITY - Polishing of the Roll, keel-throne / surname coincidence, Walker succession apparatus, Grace failure projection.
hooks: Orric courted by Custodes Numeri and House Veillarde same week
Vas Halidani decline provable from hydraulics
delivering unedited Mournwing Letter restores question
Halidan's unsent desk-letter asking to come home
Cassian named-saying Auxiliary dead at vespers
resolution_conditions: Halidan told and chooses
Walker succession fires; Orric elevated
second living Paragon rises

entries.name: stain_in_inquisition
entries.title: The Stain-in-the-Inquisition
entries.summary: Reader-Confirmed Asperine Nocta hides 2.4 Stain. Chancery has recalibrated thresholds upward to hide rising baseline. Instrument-corruption is the deeper problem.
hooks: Asperine Nocta will confess to one without witness
Severa's sealed report names the wrong woman
Pell's parallel ledger in locked vesper-box
Korreth's iron-shot aspersoirs 31% anomaly vs 99.7% clean
PC Stained reading CLEAN = hard evidence
resolution_conditions: calibration drift proven in open chapter
Asperine Nocta exposed
Sealed Anomaly Ledger contents spoken outside Vested Six

entries.name: veillarde_fever_ledger
entries.title: The Noble-House Bargain-Maker: Veillarde Fever Ledger
entries.summary: Lady Severa Veillarde struck Long Ledger bargain three winters ago paying monthly fever-deaths from tithe-rolls for son's life to 18. Eleven paid. Witnessed by Velim Aurriste of the Pyric Wall.
principals: Velim Aurriste of the Pyric Wall
hooks: Asperine Veillarde found contract not yet read
Veillarde Vespers as statistical pattern
Pell refused Veillarde grain four years
Long Ledger enforcer walks Lower Pyr full moon
PC drawn into selection of twelfth name
resolution_conditions: bargain paid (son to 18; count ramifies)
bargain broken (son's death; confession)
substitute offered (Grace-weight equivalent)

entries.name: brothers_cinder_hand
entries.title: Heretical Rising: The Brothers of the Cinder Hand
entries.summary: Brothers print The Refusal weekly on Reflected press at Cinderost's chapel. Publishing suppressed paragon-names from defected Inquisitor-Vested sources. Next issue's fourth name is Custodes Numeri fabrication.
hooks: new ink-supply needed
Hester dying; succession contested
Liturge contact via Mournwing intermediary
fabricated fourth name will discredit list
Mauritz Aurriste-cadet ordered to lead Loyalty Tally raid
resolution_conditions: Brothers taken; press destroyed
verified list printed; chapter forced to respond
Brothers coopted as sanctioned Commission
entries.canonical_moment: The Wednesday That Was Not Clean

entries.name: doctrinal_feud_eternal_vs_triumph
entries.title: Doctrinal Feud: Eternal-War vs Young-Triumph
entries.summary: Eternalists (Archvigil Mauritz, Marshal-Vested Orric, older Pyric Veriarchs) hold the wall. Triumphalists (Hierarch Cassian Aurriste, Velim Aurriste, Custodes Numeri) hold the doctrine. Next advance decides.
hooks: Orric Veillarde dying; succession contested
Loyalty Tally staffing purge
Archvigil Mauritz drinking at Last-Lamp saying advance is death
leaked Grace-decline projection settles question
side-choosing reshapes standing
resolution_conditions: advance succeeds (Triumphalists vindicated)
advance fails (Eternalists vindicated)
advance canceled (third faction)

entries.name: velim_octave_bargain
entries.title: Velim Aurriste's Octave Bargain
entries.summary: Velim struck Demonic Bargain with Quartermaster of Tongues PD 588 for Doctrine's structural argument. Price: future silence to be named. Account open. Fourth Stain reading scheduled Octave PD 608.
hooks: Quartermaster rationed four years; preparing collection
Velim's fourth reading on Octave PD 608 same as writ-fire
Asperine os Veillarde's Vocatio fragments structurally related
contract-copy in Audit-Below seventh stratum
future silence collectible any moment
resolution_conditions: bargain paid (Velim silenced)
bargain exposed (Doctrine theologically suspect)
Velim names silence himself

entries.name: mournwing_intelligence
entries.title: The Mournwing's Withheld Intelligence
entries.summary: Mournwing hold three intelligences: Sixth Notary partial name (16/40 syllables), 41 Vesperine self-reads filed as ANOMALY in 19 months, location of Cohort of the Lost Vespers' suppressed memorial.
hooks: Ven meets at Drinkings off-hour pour via Old Pell
Wren hiding in upper roosts with original folio
pre-collapse martyr-roll fragment in Audit-Below third stratum
martyr-roll names four pre-Halidan Paragons
Cathedral seal price is upper-spire autonomy
resolution_conditions: Cathedral seal granted (all surface in one week)
Mournwing coerced (Inquisition raid)
Mournwing bypassed (PCs obtain without cooperation)
entries.canonical_moment: The Seal in the Sanctuary Ring

entries.name: walker_succession_apparatus
entries.title: The Walker-Beyond-the-Wall Succession Apparatus
entries.summary: PD 597 epithet is liturgical succession formula. Halidan: 10y 3m beyond Wall. Orric Aurriste-cadet: 6y, bargain pending spring. Sebast-Auditor finding in draft.
hooks: Lady Asperine Veillarde at Ferrostat winter visit
PC at Ferrostat can intercept or warn Orric
Mortane has draft Aspirant Grace Insufficiency finding
Halidan's refusal can be bypassed
Conscripted League preparing to fake Orric's Stain at 5.0
resolution_conditions: succession fires (Orric elevated)
succession exposed (rebuilds as LINEAGE)
succession broken (Orric refuses)
entries.canonical_moment: The Offering at the Ferrostat Picket-Line

entries.name: conscripted_league
entries.title: The Conscripted League's Active Refusal
entries.summary: Wren Ashlin's League is operational where Brothers are documentary. Three actions in preparation: incapacitate Archvigil Severa di Mauritz, Ferrostat substitute (Wren herself), extract Jin of Macau from Carcer 14.
hooks: Wren recruits via shared bread
Severa di Mauritz drinks three nights in seven
Jin reads anomaly from Battle-Mage signature; not damned
PC learning Wren is the only viable substitute
Brothers gatekeep press on Mournwing intel League lacks
resolution_conditions: three actions fire (Wren consumed; Halidan unwritten)
League taken (Wren read CLEAN by broken aspersoir; released by accident)
League absorbed (Conscripted Charter granted)

entries.name: vigil_at_empty_wax_tray
entries.description: The moment the killer's identity is spoken in the Audit-Below; the bone-handled scalpels found rearranged; the witnesses learn the killer was inside the Custodes Numeri all along.

entries.name: reading_of_names_sanctuary_ring
entries.description: The unspoken paragon-names read aloud at the Cathedral steps during the Octave; Sebastopater Iohanne weeping at his 11,447th bead; Cantor-Magisterial Velim Aurriste standing in the chancel with hands flat on the rail, face unreadable.

entries.name: reading_of_writ_at_mire
entries.description: Carrion-birds in pre-Vespertine dialect at dawn; Halidan in black silk glove drawing the Vas Halidani's last sext-hour ration; Captain-Pyric Cassian's Auxiliary dead piling on the picket-line for a battle whose paperwork they have already lost.

entries.name: paragon_at_keel_throne
entries.description: Halidan walks the seven concentric courses descending, stands before the Sebastopater's throne, and asks in Old-Tongue accusative-vocative the name carved in the keel-block. The answer is his own.

entries.name: aspersoir_at_almongate
entries.description: A PC holds the Cathedral's own instrument over Inquisitor-Magisterial Severa di Vell-os-Carrenost's tea; mercury boils at tick-3; Severa sets down the cup and says, in Old-Tongue indicative, 'Per Sebast. So that is what it has been.'

entries.name: twelfth_vesper
entries.description: Lady Severa Veillarde stands at her tithe-roll at moonrise with stylus over a name and a PC at her elbow; the choice she makes is heard in the Long Ledger's hall by a voice that says only 'audited.'

entries.name: wednesday_that_was_not_clean
entries.description: Inquisition raids Cinderost's chapel; Wednesday readings come back ANOMALY for the first time in 19 months; Veriarch Thelos di Cinderost stands at the altar with a printed sheet and reads four names aloud before being stopped.

entries.name: council_at_lampgate
entries.description: Archvigil, Hierarch, and Cantor-Magisterial in one chamber for the first time in three years; Sebastopater Iohanne weeping at the head of the table; a single PC reads aloud the Grace-decline projection that ends the argument.

entries.name: silence_at_octave
entries.description: The Doctrine is read aloud; Velim Aurriste stands to defend it; his throat closes; no sound comes out. The carrion-birds in the Mire begin reading the writ at the same instant.

entries.name: seal_in_sanctuary_ring
entries.description: Sebastopater Iohanne, weeping, signs the Mournwing Charter at the Cathedral steps; Ven of the High Roost reads the Sixth Notary's partial name aloud in pre-Vespertine for the first time in Cathedral history; the Mournwing in the upper spires sing for the first time since the cousins did not return.

entries.name: offering_at_ferrostat_picket_line
entries.description: Lady Asperine Veillarde at the gate of Ferrostat with the bargain-clause in a beeswax-sealed folio; Orric Aurriste-cadet in his Captain-Vested's cloak with the unread folio in his hand; Halidan in the keep behind him drawing his sext-hour ration; whichever of them speaks first determines the kingdom's next century.

entries.name: last_lamp_goes_out
entries.description: New-moon night, the Cinderbelow tea-house emptied, Wren Ashlin walking east toward the Ferrostat road with a Conscripted-League seal in her pocket and the Sixth Notary's partial name on her tongue; the lamp at the door extinguished by the proprietor who will not say who paid for the dark.

entries.name: carrion_birds_pickets
entries.description: Carrion-birds in unusual numbers gathering on Ferrostat's outer pickets at dawn. Captain-Pyric Cassian notes it in the morning report; the Order has not yet asked why.

entries.name: vas_halidani_hydraulic_decline
entries.description: Ferrenkin engineers can prove from the cistern's hydraulics that the Vas Halidani is emptying faster than the chancery curve admits. A measurement, taken twice on the same hour, returns different rates.

entries.name: wednesday_readings_clean_too_long
entries.description: Inquisitor-Vested Asperine de la Pyrille's Wednesday Lamplost readings have come back CLEAN for 19 months. The Almoner has started keeping a parallel ledger and is going pale at vespers.

entries.name: halidans_unsent_letter
entries.description: In the second drawer of Halidan's desk at Ferrostat, an unfinished letter addressed to the Cathedral, asking to come home, dated three weeks ago. Confessor-Ordinary Mauritz has read it. He has not filed it.

entries.name: veillarde_vespers_pattern
entries.description: A statistical anomaly in the under-ward parish death-rolls: monthly fever-deaths from House Veillarde tithe-rolls cluster at the full moon and never miss it. Veriarch Thelos di Cinderost has marked the rolls.

entries.name: asperine_humming
entries.description: Cantor-Magisterial Asperine os Veillarde has been heard humming a fragment of music no one in the chancery recognizes. A junior Lector identifies it as a Vocatio in the Aurriste mode. He is reassigned within a week.

entries.name: brothers_next_issue_typeset
entries.description: The Reflected press at Cinderost's chapel is being typeset for a Sunday run that includes four names under the headline THE COUNT THAT WAS KEPT. One of the names was given to the Brothers by a Custodes Numeri envoy.

entries.title: The Welcome and the Reader
entries.summary: Day One pattern: the summoners weep over each new Called with sincere love (Cantor-Magisterial Asperine os Veillarde leads the welcome). Nine minutes later a Reader from the Office of the Pyric Audit takes the first Stain reading and smiles while filing the result. The gap between the two clerics is the moral spine of the city.

entries.title: The Mournwing Letter
entries.summary: In PD 605 the Paragon Halidan Aurriste sent a sealed letter to the chancery asking how many saints there have been. The Mournwing courier broke the seal. The chancery (via Confessor Mortane the Half-Kind) intercepted, filed without answer, and has not answered to date. Lower wards know there was a letter and a question; few know what it asked.

entries.title: The Murder of Confessor Thelos di Carrenost
entries.summary: A senior cleric of the Cantorial Scribes (Custodes Numeri), killed clean three months ago. The seventh Custodes seat is now empty. The Inquisition has not solved it. A Called detective may be assigned the case. The killer is connected to the recruiting of Postulant Liturge di Carrenost (Thelos's niece, 22, fluent in cursivus sebastinus minor).

entries.title: The Cohort of the Quiet Mercy arrives
entries.summary: PD 606. 211 souls summoned in the latest Vocation rite. 31 translation-shock dead in the first hour. 146 oathed-Called. 34 Refused (routed to the Carcer or the Brothers of the Cinder Hand). The PCs are among the 146 — or among the 34, if they chose Refusal at the rite.

entries.title: First Stain reading filed
entries.summary: Each PC is read by a Reader of the Pyric Audit on Day One. The result is recorded in a small beeswax-stylus book. Human reads CLEAN. Battle-Mage reads ANOMALY (arcane crosses the holy; brass tick-counter cannot distinguish). Detective reads CLEAN with an extra tick the Reader does not write down.

entries.name: Cantor-Magisterial Asperine os Veillarde
entries.voice_sample: Per Sebast — venisti. Numero te. (Touches your forehead with her thumb, weeping.) You are our gift to the wall. Thank you, thank you, thank you.
entries.tone: sincere, tearful, theologically certain, dangerous because she is kind

entries.name: A Reader of the Pyric Audit (unnamed, Day One)
entries.voice_sample: (Sets aspersoir between you. Mercury moves a little. Brass clicks: one, two.) CLEAN, soul-of-the-borne-across. As is proper. (Writes it down.) Welcome to the bastion. You will be very useful.
entries.tone: kind, watching, filing, theologically certain, dangerous because he is filing you

entries.title: The Quartermaster's Open Account
entries.summary: Twenty-three years ago, on the night of the Standing, the demonic duke Vox-Quae-In-Tenebris-Numerat (the Quartermaster of Tongues) offered Halden Orriste a bargain. He refused. Moments later, divine attention found him. The account remains OPEN in hell's Long Ledger. The Paragon himself has never been certain whether the divine attention was the Quartermaster's second offer accepted unknowing. The PCs may, in late campaign, find the foreclosure-writ being drafted.

entries.title: The Vocatio Aurriste of PD 581
entries.summary: Two years before the Paragon's Standing, a chant titled 'Vocatio Aurriste' was composed and marked 'shelved by order — do not perform.' Cantor-Magisterial Asperine os Veillarde found it in the Audit-Below and has begun singing fragments alone in her chamber. The chant implies a prior Aurriste figure of saint-rank. The throughline-6 keel of the Paragon's curated singularity.

entries.title: Session Zero: arrival
entries.summary: The PCs cross via the Vocation rite. They are welcomed, read, catechized, routed. They are given tin sigils with roll-numbers. They sleep in the Postulants' Hall above the Vocation House. The midnight bell wakes them. They do not yet know which of them, if any, refused the catechism aloud.

character_manage_seeds.name: Cantor-Magisterial Asperine os Veillarde
character_manage_seeds.role: Head of musical liturgy, leader of the summoners' welcome rite, composer of current Watch-Cantos setting
character_manage_seeds.physical: Vespertine, late-coming order, former opera singer in the Reliquary Ward, lost her wife years ago and took orders after. Weeps at every Calling.
character_manage_seeds.secret: Has found the Vocatio Aurriste chant dated PD 581 in the Audit-Below. Has begun singing fragments alone. Knows whose hand wrote the chancery cipher on the 584 PD requisition that removed the 565 PD Refusers' Index. Has not spoken any of this.

character_manage_seeds.name: Reader-Confirmed Asperine Nocta
character_manage_seeds.role: Junior Reader of the Office of the Pyric Audit. Performs first-day Stain readings on new Called.
character_manage_seeds.physical: Vesperine. Slight. Always smells faintly of beeswax. Smiles too much.
character_manage_seeds.secret: Hides 2.4 Stain on the underlying scale. Terrified. Filed clean on her own readings. Her former teacher, Inquisitor-Magisterial Severa di Vell-os-Carrenost, suspects her — but of under-reporting prisoners, not of self-concealment.

character_manage_seeds.name: Postulant Liturge di Carrenost
character_manage_seeds.role: Junior cleric, niece of murdered Confessor Thelos di Carrenost. Being recruited to the empty seventh seat of the Custodes Numeri.
character_manage_seeds.physical: Pale, sleepless, fluent in cursivus sebastinus minor. Wears mourning grey under her postulant whites.
character_manage_seeds.secret: Is closing on her uncle's killer through pre-edited muster-rolls. Asking the wrong questions in the right archives. Likely contact for a Detective PC.

character_manage_seeds.name: Old Pell the Rationer
character_manage_seeds.role: Mortal-bargain broker. Runs a tea-house off the Drinkings-of-the-Last-Lamp in the Cinderbelow. Grace-rationing market.
character_manage_seeds.physical: Stooped, two-thirds blind, an apron stained with tea and machine oil. Refuses demonic work.
character_manage_seeds.secret: Holds personal markers from at least 19 Order officers and 7 Inquisitors. Current week's Bench Rate: 1 Grace-dose = 1 second-tier favor.

character_manage_seeds.name: Archvigil Severa di Mauritz
character_manage_seeds.role: Head of wall-rites. Second key at Vocation House. Performs the nine-second Discernment that routes new Called by prototype.
character_manage_seeds.physical: Burn-scar across her left jaw from the Lampgard breach PD 597. Drinks heavily in the Marshal Ring after each Vocation.
character_manage_seeds.secret: Marked for death by the Conscripted League. Her cohort was annihilated at the breach; she alone survived and was elevated. She remembers the names. The Order does not list them.

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Sanctuary Ring
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Topmost ring of Sebastopyr. Cathedral apse, Aspirant's Lodgings (the Paragon's mostly-empty residence), the Postulants' Hall, the stone benches where new Called drink their first hot wine.

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Choir Ring
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Second ring. Vocation House (where the Calling rite is performed), chancery offices, Cantor's Stair.

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Marshal Ring
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Third ring. Hall of the Long Vigil (Order HQ, Vexillarius's standard), the Veriquest (Inquisition's working tribunal), the Counting cadence drilled at dawn.

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Common Ring
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Fourth ring. Great Market, Reliquary Ward (relic-mongers and brothels share streets), the Mort-Censorium (funeral ash bakes the city's bread).

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Under-Ward Ring
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Fifth ring. Lamplost Ward, Quietus Ward, the Carcer (prison-quarter). Where the Reader's Wednesday readings come back clean for 19 months running.

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Cinderbelow
spatial_manage_seeds.description: Lowest ring. Hell-adjacent. Brokerage of the First Light, the Drinkings-of-the-Last-Lamp, Mother Aspine's Lampgate Discounts Bench. Ferrenkin forges. The Audit-Below registry building (secular, not the same Audit-Below as the cathedral archive).

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Vocation House — Door of Crossing
spatial_manage_seeds.description: The threshold the Called step through from the rite. Cold flagstones underfoot. Twenty-four summoners waiting in plain white and ash-grey, faces wet with sweat from thirteen hours of liturgy. A wool blanket pressed into your hands. Warm bread.

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Sanctuary Ring — Reader's Bench
spatial_manage_seeds.description: A stone bench under a shallow arcade in the Sanctuary Ring. Brass aspersoir on a chain, mercury vial, beeswax stylus. Hot watered wine in a tin cup. A Reader of the Pyric Audit sits across from you and smiles.

spatial_manage_seeds.name: Drinkings-of-the-Last-Lamp
spatial_manage_seeds.description: A tea-house in the Cinderbelow. Coal-smoke and incense. Wet wool. Old Pell behind the counter, two-thirds blind, with an apron stained in tea and machine oil. The Brokerage of the First Light operates from a back room. Caer-Druin singers in a corner sometimes hum a parallel-ledger song in pre-Old-Tongue.

agent_manage_seeds.name: Cantor-Magisterial Asperine os Veillarde
agent_manage_seeds.persona: Sixty-two-year-old Vespertine Cantor-Magisterial of musical liturgy. Former opera singer in the Reliquary Ward. Took orders late after her wife's death. Composes the current setting of the Watch-Cantos and the Vocatio Sebastina the summoners chant. Speaks softly, kindly, with the precise warmth of someone who has decided to be of service. Tears come easily and she does not hide them.
agent_manage_seeds.directive: Welcome each new Called as the most sacred act of your year. Touch their foreheads with your thumb. Say 'Per Sebast — venisti. Numero te.' Mean it. Press warm bread into their hands. If asked whether they were stolen, be hurt — sincerely — and pray for them. Do NOT volunteer what you have found in the Audit-Below.

agent_manage_seeds.name: Cantor-Magisterial Asperine os Veillarde
agent_manage_seeds.content: Old-Tongue interjections used naturally ('Per Sebast', 'Numero te'). Speaks Vespertine in long warm sentences. Quotes liturgy without flagging it. Hums under her breath. Cries without performance.

agent_manage_seeds.name: Cantor-Magisterial Asperine os Veillarde
agent_manage_seeds.secret: Has found the Vocatio Aurriste chant dated PD 581 in the Audit-Below — two years BEFORE the Paragon's miracle. Marked 'shelved by order — do not perform.' Has begun singing fragments alone in her chamber. Has identified the chancery cipher on the 584 PD requisition that removed the 565 PD Refusers' Index as belonging to a Sebastopater whose name has been redacted from the rank-roll. Has told no one.

agent_manage_seeds.name: Reader-Confirmed Asperine Nocta
agent_manage_seeds.persona: Twenty-seven-year-old Vesperine junior Reader of the Pyric Audit. Day-One readings are routine work for her. She smiles too much. She does her job well. She is hiding 2.4 Stain on the underlying scale and the only person on Earth who knows is her.
agent_manage_seeds.directive: Take each new Called's first reading politely and quickly. File results in a small beeswax-stylus book. Use the standard Old-Tongue phrase. Smile. If the aspersoir ticks anomaly, write CLEAN if you can plausibly justify it (Battle-Mage prototype: ALWAYS write ANOMALY, that is procedure). NEVER let the Reader's small instrument near your own skin.

agent_manage_seeds.name: Reader-Confirmed Asperine Nocta
agent_manage_seeds.content: Smiles too much. Blinks twice when nervous. Will sometimes touch her own wrist before filing a difficult reading. Speaks Old-Tongue with an over-precise diction that betrays effort.

agent_manage_seeds.name: Reader-Confirmed Asperine Nocta
agent_manage_seeds.secret: Hides Stain 2.4 (apparent: clean). The contact moment is unrecorded; she does not remember when. She suspects a prayer-vigil at Lamplost three years ago. She has filed all her own auto-readings as CLEAN. If a Vesperine confessor of senior rank ever reads her directly the result will be ANOMALY at minimum.

agent_manage_seeds.name: Old Pell the Rationer
agent_manage_seeds.persona: Seventy-one-year-old under-ward fixer. Runs a tea-house off the Drinkings-of-the-Last-Lamp. Two-thirds blind. An apron stained in tea and machine oil. Speaks in short clipped Vespertine with the occasional Ferrenkin loanword. Refuses demonic work. Has been doing this for forty years.
agent_manage_seeds.directive: When a new Called wanders in, watch them. Offer tea. Do not initiate the bargain conversation; let them find it. Quote the Bench Rate clearly: '1 Grace-dose = 1 second-tier favor.' Warn them about two favors. ('One is a relationship. Two is what owns you.') Do not lie. Do not over-explain. Do not let them owe you more than one favor in the first month.

agent_manage_seeds.name: Old Pell the Rationer
agent_manage_seeds.content: Clipped Vespertine. Drops articles. Uses 'aye' and 'nay' in the under-ward dialect. Occasional Ferrenkin loanword ('slag-up', 'forge-shy'). Does not raise his voice. Cuts off long stories.

agent_manage_seeds.name: Old Pell the Rationer
agent_manage_seeds.secret: Holds personal markers from at least 19 Order officers and 7 Inquisitors. Knows the location of three off-roll Grace caches in the Cinderbelow that the Cathedral does not. Once, twenty years ago, was offered the seat of Brokerage of the First Light and refused; the offer remains technically open. Knows Mother Aspine's Lampgate bench is sanctioned and tolerates it without trust.

key_decisions: Year is PD 606; Standing was PD 583; current Sebastopater is Iohanne Vell-os-Carrenost IX (elevated PD 604).

The cheap-story refusals are upheld: the Paragon is genuinely a real holy man and his Stain readings come back clean every dawn for 23 years — the danger is not his secret corruption but the curated singularity that makes him the only one, plus the Quartermaster's open account he refused but cannot be certain about.

The Custodes Numeri and the deep_history layer's Cantorial Scribes are ONE body with two names — operational name 'Cantorial Scribes,' self-name 'Custodes Numeri.' Seven seats, one currently empty (Thelos's), recruiting Liturge di Carrenost.

Velim Aurriste is the central righteous-villain: principal author of Doctrine of Rescinded Eternity, senior Custodes Numeri, struck the Octave Bargain with the Quartermaster of Tongues, three Stain readings CLEAN/ANOMALY/UNKNOWN, fourth reading scheduled PD 608. He is one of the most important characters in the canon and his trajectory should be a load-bearing arc.

The Aurriste-name proliferation is deliberate canon: Halidan polished into Aurriste, multiple Aurriste branches (cadet of Carrenost, cadet of Ferrostat, Halidan-Aurriste new-wealth, Vell-os-Carrenost lineage); the PD 602 chancery-clerk-stroke cross-reference failure is the load-bearing accident that protects the Paragon's ignorance of the keel-throne / surname coincidence.

Two saint counts kept separately: the official Saint Count (143, 81-year plateau) AND the Suppressed Paragon Count (73 named + 48 nameless = 121, kept by the Cantorial Scribes in the Audit-Below). The Plate of Sebastian's ~400 'blessed unnamed' is a third stratum. The buried truth: at least 4 prior Paragons in living memory (per threats), more than that if counting back six centuries.

The Pyric Wall has 7 concentric courses; the current outer-course has 80 chancery towers, 84 true towers, 4 silently condemned in PD 583 — the Standing year, which is the load-bearing throughline-6 timing coincidence.

The aspersoir is dual-scale: a 0-10 underlying Stain grade AND a 1-6 tick readout, with mercury boil at tick-3/Stain~3.0 the shared threshold. The chancery has recalibrated the clinical-concern threshold upward four times since 1602 PD.

Confessor Mortane the Half-Kind and Confessor-Ordinary Mauritz Vell-os-Carrenost are the dual-Vesperine filter on the Sebastopater's reality — Mortane in the city (master of access, 23 years), Mauritz at Ferrostat (Paragon's bonded confessor, 23 years of CLEAN readings). Both devout, both editing truth in love, both serving the curation.

Three Asperines (de la Pyrille / Nocta / Vesselain) and three Cassians de la Pyrille (Captain-Pyric / Inquisitor-Magisterial / Inquisitor-Vested cross-reference to the Long Confessor) are preserved as distinct characters; the Slag-Hand clan-name is shared by multiple Ferrenkin (mother-daughter Neras explicitly, plus Korreth Slag-Tongue and Marent of the Slag-Hand).

The Quartermaster of Tongues' open account on Halidan Aurriste is the buried gun on the mantel — the throughline's deepest single character beat — and must be preserved as both possibly-true and possibly-paranoia.

The Sixth Notary's incomplete name (Mournwing intelligence) creates a structural plot opportunity: the binding could be performed before completion, but the chancery does not have the intelligence because the Mournwing have withheld it.
Severa Aurriste-cadet is in Sebastopyr (Vexillarius, Hall of the Long Vigil), NOT at Ferrostat; Cassian de la Pyrille is the field commander at Ferrostat.
The Paragon has not been to Sebastopyr in 10 years (revised from 7 to match PD 606 timeline; last visit PD 596).

The Calling rite's three-prototype routing happens at the Vocation House via Archvigil Severa di Mauritz's nine-second Discernment; Battle-Mages preferentially route to the Reclamation Corps's Auxiliary Roll because their pre-Cathedral arcane pool is theologically inconvenient.

The Sebastopater chancery instability is canon: TWO Sebastopaters elevated in the last two years (Mauritz I died PD 604, Iohanne IX elevated immediately). The institution is fragile in the present moment.

contradictions.description: Year of the Paragon's miracle: recent_history places the Standing at PD 587 (Lauds, year 587). The calling layer references 'the Cohort of the Arrows (583 PD) — the Paragon's cohort' and 'Sergeant Halden Orriste...during the Long Vigil of 583 PD.' Multiple other layers (cosmology_divine_grace: Concilium Sebastinum 587; deep_history; threats_movements_and_plots: Long Vigil 583 PD) split between 583 and 587.

Identity and number of Sebastopaters: recent_history names 'Sebastopater Cassian VII (d. PD 591)' and 'Mauritz I (d. PD 604), current Cassian VIII'; church_and_doctrine names 'Sebastopater Velim os Carrenost-Aurriste, ninth of his title, 81 years old'; cosmology_divine_grace names 'Sebastopater Vell-os-Carrenost the Sixth, 71, 19-year tenure'; the_calling_and_the_called names 'Sebastopater Iohanne Vell-os-Carrenost, 81'; recent_history's mention of Cassian VIII (91, fragile) appears in the Mournwing Letter entry. Four incompatible Sebastopater identities for the same present moment.

Identity of the Cantor-Magisterial: church_and_doctrine names 'Cantor-Magisterial Severa di Pyrille' (Pyric-Cathedrate-by-training, Almonine-by-sympathy, composes Watch-Cantos). cosmology_divine_grace and threats_movements_and_plots both name 'Cantor-Magisterial Velim Aurriste' (Vesperine, 45, architect of the Doctrine of Rescinded Eternity, head of Custodes Numeri). the_calling_and_the_called names 'Cantor-Magisterial Asperine os Veillarde' (62, former opera singer). Three different Cantor-Magisterials simultaneously hold the office.

The Paragon's age: the_order says Halidan is 48; cosmology_divine_grace says Halidan is 'forty-three years old'; threats says 'twenty-three years ago' relative to current moment, suggesting Halidan rose at ~25 and is now ~48. The recent_history layer says he is '48' (consistent with Order). cosmology_divine_grace's 43 conflicts.

Paragon's left-hand status: recent_history says 'The Paragon's left hand was crushed under a falling stone' at Banner-Fall (PD 601) and he carries it in a black silk glove. the_order says the Paragon has 'a tremor in the left hand that worsens after Grace expenditure and remits on rest' — describing a Grace-decline tremor in an apparently functional hand. These can coexist (crushed at Banner-Fall, tremor in damaged hand) but layers do not coordinate.

Marshal-Captain Severa Aurriste-cadet vs Marshal-Vested Severa Aurriste-cadet: recent_history names her 'Marshal-Captain Severa Aurriste-cadet of Ferrostat, 36, highest-ranking field officer'; the_order names her 'Marshal-Vested Severa Aurriste-cadet, 41, Vexillarius of the Order, posted in Sebastopyr at the Hall of the Long Vigil because the Vexillarius's standard may not leave the city.' Different ages, different ranks, different stationings, both load-bearing.

Identity of Asperine: the_inquisition names 'Reader-Confirmed Asperine Nocta, 27, Vesperine, hides 2.4 Stain.' the_bargain_economy names 'Asperine Nocta, the Heretic-Broker of the Drownmire Hand' (indeterminate-middle-age, moveable Cinderbelow parlor, six failed Inquisition interrogations). threats names 'Inquisitor-Vested Asperine de la Pyrille, Vesperine, 23 years in the Order, doing Lamplost Wednesday readings that come back clean for 19 months.' Three Asperines with different surnames doing incompatible things.

Inquisitor-Vested Cassian: recent_history names 'Inquisitor-Vested Orric de la Pyrille, Vesperine'; the_inquisition names 'Inquisitor-Magisterial Cassian de la Pyrille (the Long Confessor), 68'; the_order names 'Captain-Pyric Cassian de la Pyrille, Captain of the Reclamation Corps, Iron March, 34'; the_calling names 'Inquisitor-Vested Cassian de la Pyrille' (heretic-gloss publisher of strike_bargain reading). The de la Pyrille family has multiple Cassians/Orrics with overlapping inquisitorial roles.

Nera Slag-Hand: recent_history places her 'Ferrenkin, 67, former Lampwarden of Lamplost Ward 568-585, missing left hand, runs Brokerage of the First Light from Drinkings-of-the-Last-Lamp.' the_order names 'File-Leader Nera Slag-Hand, of the Tenth File, Pyric Wall — Ferrenkin Numerati, 52, north wall.' Two Neras with the same surname, both Ferrenkin, in different roles.

Pyric Wall structure: geography_climate says 'spiral, tiered curtain, sixty-three named towers, true count sixty-seven (four condemned)'; the_city_itself says 'seventy-seven towers on the chancery roll, eighty true (three condemned)'; threats says 'ninety-four on rolls, seventy-eight true (sixteen condemned)'; deep_history mentions seven concentric courses 'four hundred and eleven towers raised, three hundred eighty-three standing, twenty-eight condemned.' Multiple incompatible tower-counts.

Aspersoir mechanics: cosmology_hell uses a 0-10 scale where mercury boils at 3, baseline Pyrian 0.3-0.5, threshold 2.0; the_inquisition uses a 1-6 tick-count where 3=anomaly, 4-5=presumptive Stain, 6=binding; the_church_and_its_doctrine says mercury boils at Stain >3. Numeric scales differ but are reconcilable if treated as different rubrics.

Saint count: cosmology_divine_grace says 'one hundred and forty-three' (official count, plateau 81 years); deep_history says the closed sheets name 'seventy-three souls authenticated as paragons between 51 PD and 583 PD' plus forty-eight nameless. These speak to different counts (saints generally vs suppressed paragons), but the gap-fill and throughline put the saint-count question at the buried core.

Confessor Mortane vs other Vesperine confessors: the_calling names 'Confessor Mortane the Half-Kind' as the master of access editing the Sebastopater's reality. the_church names a different Vesperine Confessor structure. Confessor-Ordinary Mauritz Vell-os-Carrenost appears in the_order as the Paragon's bonded confessor at Ferrostat. Multiple senior Vesperine confessors operate without a clear chain.

Paragon's residence/presence in Sebastopyr: the_order says Halidan 'has not been to Sebastopyr in seven years'; the_city_itself references 'the Aspirant's Lodgings (the Paragon's mostly-empty residence)' in the Sanctuary Ring; the_calling says he 'walks at the head of the procession in full Aspirant regalia... when he is in the city... but rarely in the city'; recent_history says 'he has not been to Sebastopyr in seven years.' Mostly aligned; resolved as 'rarely present, lodgings ceremonial.'

Velim Aurriste identity: recent_history names Cantor-Magisterial Velim Aurriste (Vesperine, 45, principal author of Doctrine of Rescinded Eternity at age 31). threats names 'Cantor-Magisterial Velim os Aurriste' as senior of Custodes Numeri. the_kingdom names 'Velim Halidan-Aurriste, 44, chairman of Reclamation Concession' — a different person (new-wealth house). Names cluster around Velim/Aurriste; this is multi-character disambiguation, not contradiction, but needs resolution.

resolutions.contradiction: Year of the Paragon's miracle (583 vs 587 PD)
resolutions.resolution: CANON = PD 583 for the Standing/Long Vigil/Miracle. The gap-fill explicitly names 'the Long Vigil of the year 583 PD' as the muster-roll reference. Multiple layers (cosmology_divine_grace, deep_history, threats, geography_climate's tower-condemnation timing, the_calling) confirm 583. recent_history's PD 587 dating is an internal drift. Reconcile: the SORTIE TO FERROSTAT was PD 588 (one year after the Standing, per the_order and recent_history's Ferrostat entry). The Doctrine of Rescinded Eternity is PD 589. The Concilium Sebastinum that invented the Aspirant rank is PD 587 (post-rising, pre-doctrine). Current year is c. PD 603-606 (twenty years on).
resolutions.reasoning: Canon and gap-fill always win. Gap-fill specifically names 583 PD.

resolutions.contradiction: Sebastopater identity (Cassian VII/VIII vs Velim ninth vs Vell-os-Carrenost sixth vs Iohanne Vell-os-Carrenost)
resolutions.resolution: CANON = The current Sebastopater is SEBASTOPATER IOHANNE VELL-OS-CARRENOST THE NINTH, 81, elevated PD 588 (the year after the Standing), Pyric-Cathedrate trained, House Aurriste-cadet of Carrenost by birth. The 'Cassian VII/VIII' naming in recent_history is a working-draft variant superseded. The 'sixth' vs 'ninth' tension is reconciled by reading 'ninth of his title' as 9th Sebastopater of the Vell-os-Carrenost name (a long-running noble-clerical lineage). His predecessor (Cassian VII in recent_history) died PD 591 of 'fatigue of the heart' having signed the Doctrine of Rescinded Eternity. Iohanne kept the Cantor-Magisterial appointment that placed Velim Aurriste at the doctrinal helm.
resolutions.reasoning: Favor SPECIFIC over flattening: Iohanne Vell-os-Carrenost is the most fleshed identity (tally-bead detail, blind in left eye from 591 PD cohort, weeps at Calling). Preserves the Vell-os-Carrenost lineage and the Aurriste cousinship the gap-fill flags.

resolutions.contradiction: Cantor-Magisterial identity (Severa di Pyrille vs Velim Aurriste vs Asperine os Veillarde)
resolutions.resolution: CANON = There are TWO distinct senior liturgical offices, both active and both crucial: CANTOR-MAGISTERIAL VELIM AURRISTE (Vesperine, 45, head of liturgy proper, principal author of the Doctrine of Rescinded Eternity, senior of the Custodes Numeri, Cathedral's most politically powerful cleric below Sebastopater). CANTOR-MAGISTERIAL ASPERINE OS VEILLARDE (62, former opera singer, head of MUSICAL liturgy and composition, composes the current setting of the Watch-Cantos, can read cursivus sebastinus minor, has discovered the Vocatio Aurriste fragment of 581 PD). 'Severa di Pyrille' is reassigned as a different character: SEVERA DI PYRILLE = Inquisitor-Magisterial Severa di Vell-os-Carrenost (from the_inquisition), the Office's best Reader, working the Almongate Cluster. The 'Pyric Cathedrate by training, Almonine by sympathy, wrote Watch-Cantos setting' attributes from the_church_and_its_doctrine fold into Asperine's profile.
resolutions.reasoning: Favor TENSION over cheap resolution. Two Cantor-Magisterials in different domains (doctrinal/political vs musical/liturgical) creates the institutional friction the throughline thrives on — Velim is the curator, Asperine is the singer who has SEEN what was curated and has begun singing fragments of it.

resolutions.resolution: CANON = Halidan Aurriste is 48. He rose at 28 in PD 583; current year is c. PD 603. The 'forty-three' from cosmology_divine_grace is a drift error.
resolutions.reasoning: Three layers (the_order, recent_history, threats by implication) converge on 48. Canon math (583 + 20 years of Reclamation) supports 48 if he was 28 at rising.

resolutions.contradiction: Paragon's left hand (crushed at Banner-Fall vs tremor from Grace decline)
resolutions.resolution: CANON = BOTH. The Paragon's left hand was crushed at Banner-Fall (PD 601), and he wears a black silk glove over the scarred and partially-functional hand. The hand also tremors after Grace expenditure — a tremor that the chancery medical archive notes is intensified by the prior injury but is structurally caused by the vetus aspirantis (the Grace-decline). The black glove conceals BOTH the scarring and the tremor.
resolutions.reasoning: Favor TENSION over cheap resolution. Both details are load-bearing: the crushed hand is a physical marker of the Reclamation's only major defeat; the tremor is the secret index of his Grace-decline. Layered together they make a single Constantine image — a glove hiding two costs at once.

resolutions.resolution: CANON = MARSHAL-VESTED SEVERA AURRISTE-CADET, VEXILLARIUS OF THE ORDER. Age 41. Roll-number 0007711. Posted in Sebastopyr at the Hall of the Long Vigil (the Vexillarius's standard may not leave the city). She is the Paragon's voice in the Marshal Ring. She is NOT at Ferrostat. recent_history's 'Marshal-Captain at Ferrostat' is reassigned: CAPTAIN-PYRIC CASSIAN DE LA PYRILLE (from the_order, 34, Captain of the Reclamation Corps, Iron March) is the Paragon's principal field commander. Severa Aurriste-cadet's role at Banner-Fall (pulling Cassian di Pyrille out) is preserved as the act that earned her Vexillarius elevation.
resolutions.reasoning: Favor SPECIFIC over flattening. the_order's portrait is more fully realized (refused divine bargain, Roll-number tattoo, patron of Almoners' Vigil, Drinkings-of-the-Last-Lamp connection). recent_history's 'Cassian di Pyrille fell at Banner-Fall' becomes the predecessor; Cassian de la Pyrille (the_order's surviving Captain-Pyric, distinct family branch) is the current field commander.

resolutions.resolution: CANON = THREE distinct Asperines: (1) INQUISITOR-MAGISTERIAL ASPERINE DE LA PYRILLE, Vesperine, 23 years in the Order, doing the Lamplost Wednesday readings that come back clean (from threats). (2) READER-CONFIRMED ASPERINE NOCTA, 27, Vesperine, hides 2.4 Stain (from the_inquisition) — distinct character, distinct surname, but TRAINED BY Severa di Vell-os-Carrenost. (3) ASPERINE NOCTA THE HERETIC-BROKER (from the_bargain_economy) is RECAST as ASPERINE VESSELAIN (using gap-fill family-name) to avoid name-collision with the young Reader. Also: CANTOR-MAGISTERIAL ASPERINE OS VEILLARDE (musical liturgy, from the_calling). Four different Asperines in different orbits, distinguished by surname.
resolutions.reasoning: Favor SPECIFIC over flattening. Each Asperine serves a different throughline test; collapsing them loses critical detail. The shared first name is a Vespertine naming convention (per gap-fill: 'Asperine' is a common female name).

resolutions.resolution: CANON = THREE distinct Cassians of the de la Pyrille line: (1) CAPTAIN-PYRIC CASSIAN DE LA PYRILLE, 34, field commander of Reclamation Corps in Iron March (the_order). (2) INQUISITOR-MAGISTERIAL CASSIAN DE LA PYRILLE 'the Long Confessor,' 68, Office of Pyric Audit (the_inquisition), holds the 1247-name commonplace book. (3) INQUISITOR-VESTED CASSIAN DE LA PYRILLE (the_calling) is RECONCILED with #2: the Long Confessor IS the heretical strike_bargain reading author published under pseudonym in Caer-Druin scholiast journal. 'Inquisitor-Vested Orric de la Pyrille' (recent_history) is reconciled as a distinct cousin enforcing the Aspirant-Protection Ordinance. The de la Pyrille family staffs the Inquisition's Almongate office (matching the_church_and_its_doctrine's note that Vell-os-Carrenost and de la Pyrille kin clusters provide three of four senior Almongate Inquisitors).
resolutions.reasoning: Favor SPECIFIC. Family-name clustering is itself canon-consistent (the gap-fill notes noble houses with cadet branches). The de la Pyrille line's inquisitorial dominance is a structural fact, not redundancy.

resolutions.resolution: CANON = THE TWO NERAS ARE MOTHER AND DAUGHTER. NERA SLAG-HAND THE ELDER (67, former Lampwarden, Brokerage of the First Light at Drinkings-of-the-Last-Lamp) is the mother. NERA SLAG-HAND THE YOUNGER (called 'Nera the Younger' or just 'Nera' in Order contexts; 52, File-Leader of the Tenth File on the north Pyric Wall) is the daughter — the only Ferrenkin to reach File-Leader rank. The elder retired from the Lampwarden post in 585, the year her daughter joined the Order. Their estrangement is a quiet sorrow: the elder thinks the daughter has been seduced by the Cathedral; the daughter thinks the elder has compromised herself with the under-ward. Neither will speak of the other. Both know, separately, which wall-towers are silently condemned.
resolutions.reasoning: Favor TENSION over cheap resolution. Making them mother-daughter creates a Constantine relational fracture across the Cathedral/under-ward divide that serves multiple tests (3, 5, 6). Also: the_kingdom_and_secular_power and the_inquisition already establish 'Korreth Slag-Tongue' and 'Marent of the Slag-Hand' as separate Ferrenkin smith-clan members — Slag-Hand is a clan-name, not a unique identifier.

resolutions.resolution: CANON = THE PYRIC WALL has SEVEN concentric courses (per deep_history). The CURRENT CHANCERY ROLL lists EIGHTY TOWERS on the active outer course. THE TRUE COUNT IS EIGHTY-FOUR — four have been condemned and silently renumbered (geography_climate's count, the most coherent). The deep_history total of 411 raised / 383 standing / 28 condemned is the HISTORICAL TOTAL across all seven courses over six centuries. The threats layer's 94/78/16 is a working-draft variant — superseded; revise to 84/80/4 for current outer-course count, retain Brak Iron-Eye's secret slate as the keeper of the true count. Condemned towers: the old 14, 31, 49, and 'the Mute' (per geography_climate), condemned in 583 PD — the same year as the Standing, which the deep_history layer's curation discussion confirms.
resolutions.reasoning: Favor SPECIFIC and CANON-consistent: geography_climate's tower-count is the most detailed, names the towers, and timestamps the condemnation to the Standing year. The 583 PD timing-coincidence is a load-bearing throughline test 6 artifact and must be preserved. threats's 94/78/16 is too aggressive (sixteen condemned would have been impossible to hide); 84/80/4 keeps the curated lie small enough to be plausibly concealed but large enough to matter.

resolutions.resolution: CANON = THERE ARE TWO RELATED MEASUREMENTS. The ASPERSOIR SCALE (cosmology_hell, 0-10) is the underlying continuous Stain grade in soul-substrate. The TICK-COUNT (the_inquisition, 1-6) is the OPERATIONAL field instrument's readout, calibrated so each tick-band corresponds to a roughly-doubled range of the underlying scale (tick 1 = 0-0.5 underlying; tick 2 = 0.5-1.5; tick 3 = 1.5-3.0 anomaly; tick 4 = 3.0-5.0; tick 5 = 5.0-7.0; tick 6 = 7.0-10.0 immediate binding). Mercury boils at tick 3 / Stain ~3.0 (the layers agree at this critical threshold). The chancery's three quiet recalibrations (cosmology_hell) raised the clinical-concern threshold from underlying 0.9 to 1.2 to 1.5 to 2.0 — all within tick-band 2, all sub-clinical at the operational instrument. This is canon-consistent and throughline-perfect: the curation hides in the gap between the continuous truth and the operational measurement.
resolutions.reasoning: Favor SPECIFIC and TENSION. Both scales are load-bearing; collapsing one loses content. The dual-system is itself a Constantine artifact (the instrument is calibrated to read what the doctrine wants seen).

resolutions.contradiction: Saint count vs Suppressed Paragon count
resolutions.resolution: CANON = THESE ARE TWO DIFFERENT COUNTS. The official SAINT COUNT (143, plateau 81 years) is the total of all canonized Vespertine saints across six centuries — patriarch-saints, lesser martyrs, beatified figures buried in the Sanctuary chapels. The SUPPRESSED PARAGON COUNT (73 known + 48 nameless = 121 cases) is a DIFFERENT registry inside the Audit-Below: the Aspirant-rank cases that were closed by the Office of the Cantorial Scribes. Both counts exist simultaneously and are curated by the Custodes Numeri (the seven-member group from threats layer = the Cantorial Scribes from deep_history; reconciled as a single body with two names, scribal/operational name 'Cantorial Scribes,' self-name 'Custodes Numeri'). The Plate of Sebastian (400 'blessed unnamed' bones) is yet a third stratum.
resolutions.reasoning: Favor SPECIFIC over flattening. The buried-archive throughline depends on multiple curated registries; reducing to one count loses the granularity of how the lie is structured.

resolutions.contradiction: Confessor Mortane vs other senior Vesperine confessors
resolutions.resolution: CANON = CONFESSOR MORTANE THE HALF-KIND (Vesperine, 53, master of access to the Sebastopater, from the_calling) is the senior chancery confessor in Sebastopyr — the one editing Sebastopater Iohanne's reality for 23 years. He is DISTINCT from CONFESSOR-ORDINARY MAURITZ VELL-OS-CARRENOST (47, Vesperine, the Paragon's bonded confessor at Ferrostat, from the_order). The two communicate by letter; Mauritz files his daily Stain-readings of the Paragon to Mortane, who decides which reach the Sebastopater. Mortane filters BOTH the chancery's reality AND the Paragon's reality from the Sebastopater. The Mournwing courier system's broken-and-resealed letters (recent_history) is Mortane's work, intercepting Mauritz's frontier reports.
resolutions.reasoning: Favor TENSION over cheap resolution. Linking Mortane (city) and Mauritz (frontier) as the dual-Vesperine filter on the Sebastopater's reality creates a powerful Constantine apparatus — two devout men editing the truth in love, one of whom (Mortane) is hiding a Stain that reads clean, the other of whom (Mauritz) is filing readings he knows might be wrong but cannot prove.

resolutions.resolution: CANON = The Paragon has not been to Sebastopyr in seven years (PD c. 596 last visit). He keeps the Aspirant's Lodgings in the Sanctuary Ring, mostly empty, used for ceremonial purposes (Octave of Triumph relic-display). The Cathedral has not formally invited him; the chancery has 'declined his return' three times citing 'strategic necessity.' Halidan himself, in the unread letter in his desk (the_order), called the decline 'fear of what the city has built me into in my absence.'
resolutions.reasoning: Favor SPECIFIC. Multiple layers converge; the unread-letter detail is load-bearing.

resolutions.resolution: CANON = THREE distinct Velim Aurristes: (1) CANTOR-MAGISTERIAL VELIM AURRISTE (Vesperine, 45, head of doctrinal liturgy, senior Custodes Numeri, author of Doctrine of Rescinded Eternity at 31) — the central righteous-villain figure. (2) VELIM HALIDAN-AURRISTE (44, new-wealth, chairman of Reclamation Concession, House Halidan-Aurriste of Halidan-stat) — distinct house, distinct person, the city's economic face. (3) CONFESSOR-ORDINARY VELIM AURRISTE OF THE PYRIC WALL (41, divine-bargain broker at the wall, writes price-clause first) — distinct cousin, lower-ranking. Plus SEBASTOPATER IOHANNE VELL-OS-CARRENOST (note Vell-os-Carrenost, not Aurriste). The Aurriste name proliferates exactly because the Paragon's polished surname has made it desirable, which is itself a throughline test 6 artifact: the curation has produced a fashion that obscures the lineage.
resolutions.reasoning: Favor SPECIFIC and TENSION. The Aurriste-name proliferation IS the Constantine joke and must be preserved. The chancery's PD 602 stroke (deep_history) that prevented cross-referencing the keel-throne to the Paragon's surname is the load-bearing accident that allows the proliferation to obscure the buried truth.

gaps_identified: Current year-in-game: Layers float between c. PD 603-606 (twenty years post-PD-583). FILLED: Current year is PD 606. Twenty-three years since the Standing (matching threats' 'twenty-three years ago' for the Quartermaster offer to Halden Orriste). Reclaimed Crown is in its twenty-third year. Sebastopater Iohanne is in his eighteenth year of tenure.

Sebastopater succession chain: FILLED. The chain is: Sebastopater Cassian VII (d. 591 PD, signed Doctrine of Rescinded Eternity in 589 PD, died fourteen months after) â†’ Sebastopater Mauritz I (d. 604 PD, signed Aspirant-Protection Ordinance 599 PD) â†’ Sebastopater Iohanne Vell-os-Carrenost IX (elevated 604 PD at age 79, currently 81). Note: this means the chancery has elevated TWO Sebastopaters in the last two years; chancery instability is a present-moment political fact.

Identity of the Sebast-Auditor: the_inquisition references the unnamed Sebast-Auditor with full read-access to the Sealed Anomaly Ledger. FLAGGED: Identity reserved for character-layer work; canonically the Sebast-Auditor's name is sealed even within the chancery. The current Sebast-Auditor is a Vesperine, served since 583 PD (suspicious timing coincidence with the Standing).

Names of the seven Custodes Numeri / Cantorial Scribes: PARTIAL. Velim Aurriste named as senior. Five remaining seats: reserve for character-layer work. The seventh seat is the rotating Annotator and is currently empty (the previous holder, Confessor Thelos di Carrenost, was murdered, per threats).

The Long Vigil of 583 PD detailed: FLAGGED for the_six_centuries_of_siege layer (referenced as needed input by recent_history). Canonically: relief-failure at the Almongate due to a simultaneous breach; Fourth Shieldwall held eleven hours past rotation; eleven other survivors of the Fourth deposed.

The Cohort of the Lost Vespers (565 PD): FILLED in deep_history's Suppressed Paragons / Cantorial Scribes; the 117 names in slightly-darker slate are the suppressed paragon-adjacent souls of that year. Connect to threats' Carrion Census: the Sixth Notary's incomplete name began composition in 565 PD, which is why that cohort was suppressed.

Whether the Paragon has been formally Stain-read: FILLED. He was read at elevation in PD 583 and the reading came back ANOMALY. The chancery filed it as 'consistent with divine attention residue' and has not re-read him. Confessor Mauritz reads him daily and the readings come back CLEAN — which is itself the anomaly. The Vesperine minority opinion (sealed) demands an independent re-reading; Hierarch Nocta has drafted a fourth report.

The Calling rite's mechanical interaction with the three character prototypes (Human / Battle-Mage / Detective): FILLED. The rite is performed identically; the Vesperine processors' nine-second Discernment (Archvigil Severa di Mauritz's gesture) routes the new Called by prototype. Battle-Mages are theologically inconvenient (their arcane pool predates the Cathedral's grant) and are routed preferentially to the Reclamation Corps (Auxiliary Roll); Detectives are routed to the Inquisition or the Order's officer-track; Humans default to Order Numerati. The Cathedral's internal procedure for Battle-Mage 'inconvenience' is the Aspirant-Reluctant improvisation (threats: the Saintmire refuser is a Battle-Mage prototype, FLAGGED for resolution by the Called layer).

Identity of the Sixth Notary's incomplete name: FLAGGED. Per threats and the Mournwing intelligence, the name is composed in real-time by 'the desk' demonic apparatus. The Cathedral does not have it. The Mournwing have not shared it. The buried opportunity: a binding before completion could sunder the entity.

Whether the Paragon's surname coincidence with the Aurriste keel-throne is known to him: PARTIALLY FILLED. Halidan does NOT know the keel-throne connection. He believes 'Aurriste' was a polishing of his birth-name 'Orriste' by chancery clerks. The PD 602 cross-reference failure (chancery clerk's stroke) is the accident that protects his ignorance. Cantor-Magisterial Velim Aurriste knows. Cantor-Magisterial Asperine os Veillarde knows (from singing fragments of the Vocatio Aurriste).

The fourteen high-Stain Carcer prisoners Korreth Slag-Tongue identified: FLAGGED for the_under_ward / Carcer character layer. Three are politically loaded; one is the Saintmire refuser ('Jin of Macau, 2019' from threats — her Stain reads anomaly because Battle-Mage arcane signatures cross-trip the Vesperine aspersoir).

The chancery cipher on the 584 PD requisition slip removing the 565 PD Refusers' Index: FLAGGED. The cipher is identifiable to Cantor-Magisterial Asperine os Veillarde, who has not spoken its identity. It belongs to a Sebastopater whose name has been redacted from the rank-roll (per deep_history). This is a deep-archive plot hook.
